metadata.framework_name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

framework.FrameworkName: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
framework.FrameworkDescription: The Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) is a set of security standards designed to ensure that all companies that accept, process, store or transmit credit card information maintain a secure environment. PCI DSS applies to all entities involved in payment card processing including merchants, processors, acquirers, issuers, and service providers.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the necessary measures to protect cardholder data throughout its lifecycle, ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements. It emphasizes the importance of securing payment card transactions against fraud and data breaches, which have become prevalent in the digital age. The policy applies to all personnel involved in processing, storing, or transmitting cardholder data.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units and personnel involved in payment card processing, including merchants, service providers, and acquirers. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, including on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. The policy also extends to third-party vendors and service providers that interact with cardholder data.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a comprehensive framework for protecting cardholder data, ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements, and mitigating risks associated with data breaches. Specific goals include reducing the number of vulnerabilities identified during assessments, ensuring timely remediation of security issues, and maintaining a secure environment for payment card transactions.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for assessing all locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted. It includes identifying IT assets and business processes that handle cardholder data and analyzing them for vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a comprehensive assessment of all systems and processes that handle cardholder data. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for conducting the assessment. WHEN: Assessments must be conducted annually and after any significant changes to the environment. HOW: Use automated tools and manual reviews to identify vulnerabilities. WHERE: Assessments must cover all organizational units, including on-premises and cloud environments.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Assessment of Cardholder Data Locations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct a comprehensive assessment of all systems and processes that handle cardholder data annually.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To identify vulnerabilities in systems and processes handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches leading to financial losses and reputational damage

mitigation.1: Implement security patches regularly
mitigation.2: Train employees on data security best practices
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, regulatory fines
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Post-Change Assessment of Cardholder Data Locations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct a comprehensive assessment of all systems and processes that handle cardholder data after any significant changes to the environment.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To identify vulnerabilities in systems and processes handling cardholder data post-change
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches due to unaddressed vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Perform penetration testing after changes
mitigation.2: Implement change management processes
mitigation.3: Review security configurations post-change

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unaddressed vulnerabilities leading to data breaches, system downtime
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Cloud Environment Assessment of Cardholder Data Locations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct a comprehensive assessment of all systems and processes in cloud environments that handle cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All cloud-based systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To identify vulnerabilities in cloud-based systems handling cardholder data
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units with cloud environments
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches in cloud environments

mitigation.1: Implement cloud security best practices
mitigation.2: Encrypt data in transit and at rest in the cloud
mitigation.3: Regularly audit cloud configurations

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches in cloud environments, unauthorized access to cardholder data
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units with cloud environments

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the steps required to remediate vulnerabilities identified during assessments. It emphasizes the importance of securely removing unnecessary cardholder data storage and implementing secure business processes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Remediate all identified vulnerabilities within the specified timeframe. WHO: The IT Security Team and relevant department heads are responsible for remediation efforts. WHEN: Vulnerabilities must be addressed within 30 days of identification. HOW: Implement patches, updates, and secure configurations as necessary. WHERE: Remediation efforts must be applied across all systems that handle cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All identified vulnerabilities must be remediated within 30 days of identification.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the IT Security Team and relevant department heads
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely remediation of vulnerabilities to reduce security risks
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: IT Security Team, relevant department heads
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to remediate vulnerabilities within the specified timeframe may lead to unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Prioritize critical vulnerabilities for immediate remediation
mitigation.2: Implement automated vulnerability scanning tools
mitigation.3: Establish a remediation tracking system

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Secure configurations must be implemented on all systems that handle cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Enhance security posture by ensuring secure configurations are in place
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to implement secure configurations may result in data breaches and non-compliance with industry standards

mitigation.1: Use configuration management tools to enforce secure settings
mitigation.2: Regularly audit system configurations for compliance
mitigation.3: Implement least privilege access controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, non-compliance with industry standards

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement patches and updates as necessary to address vulnerabilities.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure systems are up-to-date with security patches to mitigate vulnerabilities
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: IT Security Team, relevant department heads
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to apply patches may leave systems vulnerable to exploitation and compromise

mitigation.1: Establish a patch management process with defined timelines
mitigation.2: Test patches in a controlled environment before deployment
mitigation.3: Monitor vendor security advisories for critical patches

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: System compromise, data breaches

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for documenting assessment and remediation activities, as well as submitting compliance reports to relevant stakeholders.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document all assessment findings and remediation actions taken. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for compiling and submitting reports. WHEN: Reports must be submitted quarterly and upon completion of remediation actions. HOW: Use standardized reporting templates to ensure consistency. WHERE: Reports must be submitted to the acquiring bank and relevant card brands.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Submit a detailed compliance report to the acquiring bank and relevant card brands on a quarterly basis.
compliances.Scope: All business units within the organization
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely and accurate reporting of compliance activities to stakeholders
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to submit reports may result in fines, penalties, or loss of business opportunities

mitigation.1: Establish a reminder system for report submission deadlines
mitigation.2: Implement a review process to ensure report accuracy
mitigation.3: Provide training on report submission requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance leading to regulatory scrutiny, reputational damage, or financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units may be impacted by regulatory fines or reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Document all assessment findings and remediation actions taken to address compliance issues.
compliances.Scope: Compliance Officer and relevant stakeholders
compliances.Objective: To track and monitor remediation efforts to ensure compliance with regulations
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of documentation may result in incomplete remediation actions and recurring compliance issues

mitigation.1: Implement a centralized documentation system for remediation activities
mitigation.2: Assign responsibility for documenting remediation actions to specific individuals
mitigation.3: Conduct regular audits of documentation completeness

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incomplete remediation actions leading to regulatory non-compliance and financial penalties
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compliance department may face regulatory penalties or reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Submit a detailed report upon completion of remediation actions to the acquiring bank and relevant card brands.
compliances.Scope: Compliance Officer and relevant stakeholders
compliances.Objective: To inform stakeholders about the successful completion of remediation activities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to report completion may result in stakeholders being unaware of compliance status

mitigation.1: Establish a process for timely reporting of completed remediation actions
mitigation.2: Implement a review process to ensure report accuracy
mitigation.3: Provide training on report submission requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Stakeholders being unaware of compliance status, leading to loss of trust or business opportunities
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compliance department may face reputational damage or loss of business opportunities

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for compliance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS) to protect cardholder data. It applies to all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, ensuring that security measures are in place to mitigate risks associated with payment card transactions. Compliance with this policy is mandatory for all personnel involved in payment processing activities.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, personnel, systems, applications, and infrastructure involved in the storage, processing, or transmission of cardholder data. It covers on-premises, cloud, and remote environments, as well as third-party vendors and service providers that handle cardholder data.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of cardholder data through compliance with PCI DSS requirements. Specific goals include reducing the risk of data breaches, ensuring the integrity and confidentiality of cardholder information, and maintaining regulatory compliance. Measurable outcomes include successful completion of compliance assessments and implementation of required security controls.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the access control measures required to restrict access to cardholder data based on business need. It outlines the processes for user identification, authentication, and authorization to ensure that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive information.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement role-based access controls to restrict access to cardholder data. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for managing access controls. WHEN: Access reviews must be conducted quarterly. HOW: Access logs will be monitored and reviewed for unauthorized access attempts. WHERE: This control applies to all systems and applications that store or process cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement role-based access controls to restrict access to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that store or process cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive information
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Regular access reviews to ensure access controls are up to date
mitigation.2: Implement multi-factor authentication for added security
mitigation.3: Provide regular training on access control policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, financial losses

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct quarterly access reviews to ensure access controls are up to date.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that store or process cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To regularly review and update access controls to prevent unauthorized access
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated access controls leading to unauthorized access and data breaches

mitigation.1: Automate access review processes to ensure timely reviews
mitigation.2: Implement regular training on access review procedures
mitigation.3: Enforce strict access control policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated access controls, unauthorized access, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Monitor and review access logs for unauthorized access attempts.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that store or process cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To detect and prevent unauthorized access to cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Undetected unauthorized access leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement real-time monitoring of access logs
mitigation.2: Regularly analyze access log data for anomalies
mitigation.3: Establish incident response procedures for unauthorized access

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected unauthorized access, data breaches

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for encrypting cardholder data during transmission and storage. It specifies the encryption standards and protocols to be used to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Encrypt all cardholder data during transmission over open, public networks and when stored. WHO: Network Security Team is responsible for implementing encryption measures. WHEN: Encryption must be applied at all times when data is transmitted or stored. HOW: Use industry-standard encryption protocols such as AES-256 for data at rest and TLS 1.2 or higher for data in transit. WHERE: This control applies to all systems that handle cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Data Encryption for Cardholder Data Transmission
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Encrypt all cardholder data during transmission over open, public networks.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To protect sensitive information from unauthorized access during transmission
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement AES-256 encryption for data transmission
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor encryption protocols for compliance
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic security audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches resulting in financial penalties and loss of customer trust

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Data Encryption for Cardholder Data Storage
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Encrypt all cardholder data when stored.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To protect sensitive information from unauthorized access when stored
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches due to unauthorized access to stored cardholder data

mitigation.1: Implement AES-256 encryption for data storage
mitigation.2: Regularly audit stored data for compliance
mitigation.3: Enforce access controls for stored data

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches resulting in financial penalties and loss of customer trust

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Use industry-standard encryption protocols such as AES-256 for data at rest and TLS 1.2 or higher for data in transit.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure the use of secure encryption protocols for protecting cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches due to weak encryption protocols

mitigation.1: Regularly update encryption protocols to meet industry standards
mitigation.2: Conduct regular security assessments for encryption compliance
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication for data access

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches resulting in financial penalties and loss of customer trust

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for maintaining a vulnerability management program, including the regular updating of anti-virus software and the development of secure systems and applications.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Regularly update anti-virus software and conduct vulnerability scans on all systems. WHO: IT Operations Team is responsible for maintaining the vulnerability management program. WHEN: Anti-virus updates must occur daily, and vulnerability scans must be conducted monthly. HOW: Utilize automated tools for vulnerability scanning and ensure timely application of security patches. WHERE: This control applies to all systems connected to the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All systems connected to the cardholder data environment must have their anti-virus software updated daily.
compliances.Scope: IT Operations Team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all systems have the latest protection against known vulnerabilities and malware.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of malware infections and data breaches due to outdated anti-virus software.

mitigation.1: Implement automated update mechanisms for anti-virus software
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor and verify anti-virus software update status
mitigation.3: Implement strict access controls to prevent unauthorized changes to anti-virus software

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, malware infections, unauthorized access to sensitive information

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Monthly vulnerability scans must be conducted on all systems connected to the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: IT Operations Team
compliances.Objective: To identify and remediate vulnerabilities in a timely manner to prevent potential security breaches.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unidentified vulnerabilities may be exploited by attackers leading to data breaches and system compromise.

mitigation.1: Utilize automated vulnerability scanning tools
mitigation.2: Establish a process for timely remediation of identified vulnerabilities
mitigation.3: Regularly review and update vulnerability scanning procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, unauthorized access, system compromise

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure timely application of security patches on all systems connected to the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: IT Operations Team
compliances.Objective: To address known security vulnerabilities and protect systems from potential exploits.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to apply security patches promptly may expose systems to known vulnerabilities and increase the risk of security incidents.

mitigation.1: Establish a patch management process
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor security advisories for critical patches
mitigation.3: Test patches in a controlled environment before deployment

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, system compromise, unauthorized access

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for maintaining a secure network and firewall configuration to protect cardholder data. It is essential for preventing unauthorized access to sensitive payment card information and ensuring compliance with PCI DSS standards. The policy outlines specific controls for firewall management, router configurations, and access restrictions.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including merchants, service providers, and third-party vendors. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that process, store, or transmit cardholder data, including on-premises and cloud environments. All personnel with access to the cardholder data environment are included in this scope.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of cardholder data through effective network security measures. Specific goals include preventing unauthorized access, ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements, and establishing a secure environment for payment card transactions. Measurable outcomes include regular assessments of firewall configurations and adherence to security standards.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the standards for configuring firewalls and routers to protect cardholder data. It includes requirements for documentation, testing, and review of configurations.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and implement firewall and router configuration standards. WHO: Network security team. WHEN: Upon configuration changes and reviewed every six months. HOW: Document all connections, business justifications, and technical settings; conduct formal testing. WHERE: All systems within the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Documentation of Firewall and Router Configurations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All firewall and router configurations must be documented, including connections, business justifications, and technical settings.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the network security team responsible for configuring firewalls and routers.
compliances.Objective: To ensure transparency and accountability in firewall and router configurations.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential unauthorized access to cardholder data due to undocumented configurations.

mitigation.1: Regular audits of configuration documentation
mitigation.2: Training on proper documentation practices
mitigation.3: Automated documentation tools

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Formal Testing of Firewall and Router Configurations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All firewall and router configurations must undergo formal testing to ensure they meet security standards.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the network security team responsible for configuring firewalls and routers.
compliances.Objective: To verify the effectiveness of firewall and router configurations in protecting cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inadequate configuration testing may result in vulnerabilities and security breaches.

mitigation.1: Automated testing tools
mitigation.2: Regular security audits
mitigation.3: Continuous monitoring

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security breaches, data leaks

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Review of Firewall and Router Configurations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All firewall and router configurations must be reviewed every six months to ensure compliance with standards.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the network security team responsible for configuring firewalls and routers.
compliances.Objective: To maintain the security and integrity of firewall and router configurations over time.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated configurations may lead to security vulnerabilities and breaches.

mitigation.1: Regular configuration reviews
mitigation.2: Automated alerts for configuration changes
mitigation.3: Periodic security assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security breaches, data leaks

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Basel III Capital Conservation Buffer (CCB) Compliance  Compliance Type
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: This compliance item ensures adherence to the Basel III Capital Conservation Buffer (CCB) requirements, mandating banks to hold an additional 2.5% of Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) capital above the minimum capital adequacy ratio.
compliances.Scope: Applicable across all business units and consolidated entities of the bank under Basel III capital reporting.
compliances.Objective: To ensure that the bank consistently maintains the Capital Conservation Buffer as per Basel III guidelines and implements automatic restrictions on capital distributions when CET1 ratios fall below prescribed thresholds.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Regulatory sanctions or supervisory actions.

mitigation.1: Implement automated CET1 monitoring dashboards integrated with real-time capital ratio computation and alert triggers at every 0.25% decline in CET1.
mitigation.2: Review capital adequacy plans quarterly and ensure capital raising or retained earnings adjustments are initiated when buffer levels approach the minimum threshold.

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misreporting of CET1 due to data reconciliation issues between finance and regulatory systems.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Documentation of Firewall and Router Configurations And Secure all the routers
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All firewall and router configurations must be documented, including connections, business justifications, and technical settings.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the network security team responsible for configuring firewalls and routers.
compliances.Objective: To ensure transparency and accountability in firewall and router configurations.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential unauthorized access to cardholder data due to undocumented configurations.
compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the restriction of all traffic from untrusted networks to protect cardholder data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Build firewall configurations that restrict traffic from untrusted networks. WHO: IT security team. WHEN: At the time of configuration and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Deny all traffic except for necessary protocols. WHERE: Cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Build firewall configurations that restrict traffic from untrusted networks in the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To protect cardholder data by denying all traffic except for necessary protocols from untrusted networks.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Regularly update firewall rules to adapt to new threats
mitigation.2: Implement intrusion detection systems to monitor network traffic
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits to identify vulnerabilities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, financial losses, reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review firewall configurations that restrict traffic from untrusted networks in the cardholder data environment on a quarterly basis.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure the effectiveness of firewall rules in protecting cardholder data by reviewing and updating configurations regularly.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated firewall rules leading to vulnerabilities and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Document and track changes made during each review
mitigation.2: Conduct penetration testing to identify weaknesses
mitigation.3: Implement automated configuration management tools

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, regulatory non-compliance

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Deny All Traffic Except Necessary Protocols
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement firewall rules that deny all traffic from untrusted networks except for necessary protocols in the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To minimize the attack surface by allowing only essential network traffic from untrusted networks.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, malware infections

mitigation.1: Regularly monitor network traffic for anomalies
mitigation.2: Implement network segmentation to isolate critical systems
mitigation.3: Enforce strict access control policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, malware infections

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy prohibits direct public access to any system component in the cardholder data environment.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Ensure no direct public access to the cardholder data environment. WHO: Network administrators. WHEN: Continuously monitored. HOW: Implement network segmentation and access controls. WHERE: All systems within the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement network segmentation to ensure no direct public access to the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network administrators
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access to cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement firewall rules to restrict access
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor network traffic for anomalies
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement access controls to restrict public access to the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network administrators
compliances.Objective: To limit access to authorized personnel only
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Implement role-based access control
mitigation.2: Regularly review access permissions
mitigation.3: Implement least privilege principle

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Continuously monitor the network to ensure no direct public access to the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network administrators
compliances.Objective: To detect and respond to any unauthorized access attempts
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement intrusion detection systems
mitigation.2: Regularly conduct security audits
mitigation.3: Establish incident response procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires the installation of personal firewall software on devices accessing the cardholder data environment.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install personal firewall software on all devices accessing the cardholder data environment. WHO: IT support staff. WHEN: Before device deployment and updated regularly. HOW: Use approved firewall software and ensure configurations are compliant. WHERE: All employee devices accessing the network.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All devices accessing the cardholder data environment must have personal firewall software installed.
compliances.Scope: All employee devices accessing the network
compliances.Objective: To protect cardholder data by preventing unauthorized access through devices
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Regularly update firewall software
mitigation.2: Monitor firewall configurations for compliance
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach resulting in financial penalties and loss of customer trust

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Only approved firewall software should be used on devices accessing the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: All employee devices accessing the network
compliances.Objective: To ensure consistency and effectiveness of firewall protection across all devices
compliances.PossibleDamage: Incompatibility issues, security vulnerabilities, unauthorized access

mitigation.1: Regularly update approved firewall software
mitigation.2: Implement strict software approval process
mitigation.3: Provide training on approved software usage

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security vulnerabilities, unauthorized access, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Firewall configurations must be regularly checked for compliance with security standards.
compliances.Scope: All employee devices accessing the network
compliances.Objective: To ensure that firewall settings are optimized for security and compliance
compliances.PossibleDamage: Misconfigurations, security gaps, unauthorized access

mitigation.1: Implement automated configuration checks
mitigation.2: Conduct manual configuration audits
mitigation.3: Enforce strict configuration change control

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security gaps, unauthorized access, data breaches

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the documentation of security policies and operational procedures related to firewall management.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document all related security policies and procedures. WHO: Compliance officer. WHEN: Annually reviewed and updated. HOW: Maintain a centralized repository for documentation. WHERE: Accessible to all affected personnel.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Review and Update of Security Policies
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All related security policies and procedures must be reviewed and updated annually by the compliance officer.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all personnel responsible for firewall management
compliances.Objective: To ensure that security policies are up-to-date and effective in mitigating risks
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated security policies may lead to vulnerabilities and increased risk of security breaches

mitigation.1: Regular training on policy updates
mitigation.2: Automated alerts for policy reviews
mitigation.3: Periodic audits of policy compliance

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to update security policies may result in unauthorized access to sensitive data, network breaches, and compliance violations

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain a centralized repository accessible to all affected personnel for security policies and operational procedures.
compliances.Scope: All personnel involved in firewall management
compliances.Objective: To ensure easy access to up-to-date security documentation for effective policy implementation
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of centralized documentation repository may lead to confusion, non-compliance, and security incidents

mitigation.1: Regular audits of documentation access logs
mitigation.2: Training on repository usage
mitigation.3: Implement version control for documents

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate access to security documentation may result in misconfigurations, policy violations, and security breaches

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Compliance Officer Oversight of Security Policies
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The compliance officer is responsible for overseeing the documentation of security policies and procedures.
compliances.Scope: Compliance officer
compliances.Objective: To ensure that security policies are properly documented and maintained according to standards
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of oversight may result in incomplete or outdated security documentation, increasing the risk of non-compliance

mitigation.1: Regular meetings with policy owners
mitigation.2: Review of documentation version history
mitigation.3: Training on policy documentation best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incomplete or outdated security documentation may lead to regulatory fines, security incidents, and reputational damage

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for managing default passwords and security parameters to prevent unauthorized access to payment card systems. It emphasizes the importance of changing default settings and maintaining strong security configurations.
policies.PolicyName: Default Password and Security Parameter Management
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all systems and applications involved in payment card processing, including those managed by third-party vendors. It covers all personnel responsible for system configuration and security management.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to mitigate risks associated with default passwords and security settings. Specific goals include ensuring all systems are configured securely, maintaining an inventory of system components, and achieving compliance with PCI DSS standards. Measurable outcomes include regular audits of system configurations and password management practices.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires the immediate change of all vendor-supplied default passwords and the removal of unnecessary default accounts.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Change all vendor-supplied default passwords. WHO: System administrators. WHEN: Before system installation and upon deployment. HOW: Follow documented procedures for password changes. WHERE: All systems within the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Change Default Passwords Before System Installation
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: System administrators must change all vendor-supplied default passwords before system installation.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access to systems through default passwords
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, potential data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement strong password policies
mitigation.2: Regularly audit password changes
mitigation.3: Enforce multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access leading to data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: System administrators must change all vendor-supplied default passwords upon system deployment.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To maintain the security of systems post-deployment
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to systems, potential data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement strong password policies
mitigation.2: Regularly audit password changes
mitigation.3: Enforce multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access post-deployment

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: System administrators must identify and remove any unnecessary default accounts.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To minimize the attack surface and potential vulnerabilities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of unauthorized access, potential data breaches

mitigation.1: Regularly review and disable unused accounts
mitigation.2: Implement access controls
mitigation.3: Enforce least privilege principle

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access due to default accounts

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the development of configuration standards for all system components to address known vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and maintain configuration standards. WHO: IT security team. WHEN: Reviewed annually and updated as needed. HOW: Align with industry-accepted definitions and best practices. WHERE: All systems in scope for PCI DSS.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Develop and maintain configuration standards for all system components to address known vulnerabilities.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that configuration standards are developed and maintained to address vulnerabilities
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All systems in scope for PCI DSS
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to unauthorized access or data breaches

mitigation.1: Regular security training for IT team members
mitigation.2: Implementing multi-factor authentication for system access
mitigation.3: Regular security audits and assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, financial losses

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review configuration standards annually and update as needed
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that configuration standards are regularly reviewed and updated to address new vulnerabilities
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All systems in scope for PCI DSS
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated configuration standards may not address new vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regular vulnerability assessments
mitigation.2: Continuous monitoring of system components
mitigation.3: Timely patch management

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Exploitation of outdated vulnerabilities, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Align configuration standards with industry-accepted definitions and best practices
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that configuration standards follow industry best practices for security
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All systems in scope for PCI DSS
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-alignment may lead to security vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regular industry benchmarking
mitigation.2: Engagement with industry experts
mitigation.3: Periodic review of industry standards

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security breaches, data leaks, reputation damage

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires the use of strong cryptography to encrypt all non-console administrative access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Encrypt all non-console administrative access. WHO: System administrators. WHEN: At the time of configuration and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Use approved encryption methods. WHERE: All systems accessing cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All non-console administrative access must be encrypted using strong cryptography.
compliances.Scope: System administrators
compliances.Objective: To protect sensitive data during administrative access
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches and unauthorized access to sensitive information

mitigation.1: Regular encryption key rotation
mitigation.2: Multi-factor authentication for administrative access
mitigation.3: Continuous monitoring of encryption protocols

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to critical systems, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Encryption methods used for non-console administrative access must be reviewed quarterly for effectiveness and compliance with approved standards.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure ongoing compliance with encryption standards and identify any weaknesses in encryption protocols
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated or weak encryption methods leading to security vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regular encryption method assessments
mitigation.2: Immediate remediation of any identified vulnerabilities
mitigation.3: Documentation of encryption method reviews

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches due to outdated encryption methods

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Approved Encryption Methods for Administrative Access
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Only approved encryption methods should be used for non-console administrative access.
compliances.Scope: System administrators
compliances.Objective: To maintain consistency and security in encryption practices
compliances.PossibleDamage: Incompatibility issues, security breaches

mitigation.1: Regular training on approved encryption methods
mitigation.2: Enforcement of encryption policy through access controls
mitigation.3: Periodic validation of encryption methods

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security breaches due to unauthorized encryption methods

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the maintenance of an inventory of all system components in scope for PCI DSS.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Maintain an up-to-date inventory of system components. WHO: IT asset management team. WHEN: Reviewed and updated quarterly. HOW: Use asset management tools to track components. WHERE: All systems involved in payment card processing.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review and update the inventory of system components quarterly.
compliances.Scope: IT asset management team
compliances.Objective: Ensure the inventory is up-to-date and accurate to meet PCI DSS requirements.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to unauthorized access to payment card data.

mitigation.1: Regular training for IT asset management team on inventory management
mitigation.2: Implement automated alerts for outdated components
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits of the inventory

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to payment card data due to outdated or missing components in the inventory

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize asset management tools to track system components.
compliances.Scope: IT asset management team
compliances.Objective: Ensure accurate tracking and monitoring of system components.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate tracking may lead to unidentified vulnerabilities in the system.

mitigation.1: Regular training on asset management tools for IT asset management team
mitigation.2: Implement automated alerts for discrepancies in tracking
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits of the tracking data

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unidentified vulnerabilities in the system due to inaccurate tracking of components

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Include all systems involved in payment card processing in the inventory.
compliances.Scope: IT asset management team
compliances.Objective: Ensure comprehensive coverage of all relevant systems in the inventory.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Exclusion of systems may lead to non-compliance with PCI DSS requirements.

mitigation.1: Regular review of systems involved in payment card processing
mitigation.2: Implement automated checks for new systems added to the processing flow
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits of system inclusion

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance with PCI DSS requirements due to exclusion of systems involved in payment card processing

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires the documentation of security policies and operational procedures related to password management.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document all related security policies and procedures. WHO: Compliance officer. WHEN: Annually reviewed and updated. HOW: Maintain a centralized repository for documentation. WHERE: Accessible to all affected personnel.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All security policies and operational procedures related to password management must be reviewed annually by the compliance officer.
compliances.Scope: All departments within the organization
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all security policies are up to date and relevant
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated policies could lead to security vulnerabilities and breaches

mitigation.1: Regular training for compliance officer on policy updates
mitigation.2: Automated alerts for policy review deadlines
mitigation.3: Regular audits of policy documentation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to review documentation could result in non-compliance with regulatory requirements
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by potential security breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All security policies and operational procedures related to password management must be stored in a centralized repository accessible to all affected personnel.
compliances.Scope: All departments within the organization
compliances.Objective: To ensure easy access and visibility of security policies for all employees
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccessible policies could lead to non-compliance and security incidents

mitigation.1: Regular audits of repository access logs
mitigation.2: User training on repository usage
mitigation.3: Automated alerts for unauthorized access

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to policies leading to security breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by unauthorized access to policies

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All operational procedures related to password management must be documented in addition to security policies.
compliances.Scope: All departments within the organization
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all procedures related to password management are clearly defined and documented
compliances.PossibleDamage: Undefined procedures could lead to errors and security incidents

mitigation.1: Regular audits of procedure documentation
mitigation.2: Employee training on updated procedures
mitigation.3: Automated alerts for procedure changes

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undefined procedures leading to password management errors
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by potential errors in password management procedures

versions.PolicyName: Default Password and Security Parameter Management

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for protecting cardholder data throughout its lifecycle. It emphasizes the importance of data retention policies and the secure handling of sensitive authentication data.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all entities that accept payment cards, including merchants and service providers. It covers all forms of cardholder data, including printed, processed, transmitted, or stored data, across all systems and environments.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the secure handling of cardholder data to prevent unauthorized access and data breaches. Specific goals include compliance with PCI DSS requirements, effective data retention practices, and the implementation of strong encryption methods. Measurable outcomes include regular audits of data storage practices and adherence to data protection standards.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for limiting cardholder data storage and retention to necessary business purposes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Limit cardholder data storage and retention time. WHO: Data management team. WHEN: Reviewed quarterly. HOW: Implement data retention policies and purge unnecessary data. WHERE: All systems storing cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Cardholder data storage should be limited to only necessary business purposes and should not exceed the defined retention period.
compliances.Scope: All systems storing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that cardholder data is not stored longer than necessary for business purposes
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to unauthorized access to sensitive cardholder data and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update data retention policies
mitigation.2: Implement automated data purging processes
mitigation.3: Provide regular training to data management team on data retention best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to stored cardholder data, data breaches, regulatory fines and penalties

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Quarterly Review of Data Storage and Retention Policies
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Data management team should review and update data storage and retention policies on a quarterly basis.
compliances.Scope: All systems storing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that data storage and retention policies are up-to-date and in compliance with business requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated policies may lead to improper data storage practices and non-compliance with regulations.

mitigation.1: Establish a formal review process for data policies
mitigation.2: Provide training to data management team on policy updates
mitigation.3: Implement automated policy review reminders

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance with data storage regulations, improper data retention practices

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Establish automated data purging processes to regularly remove unnecessary cardholder data from systems.
compliances.Scope: All systems storing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that only necessary cardholder data is retained and unnecessary data is promptly removed.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Accumulation of unnecessary data may increase the risk of data breaches and non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Automate data purging processes based on defined retention policies
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor data storage for unnecessary data
mitigation.3: Implement data encryption for stored data

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, non-compliance with data retention regulations

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Prohibition of Storing Sensitive Authentication Data
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy prohibits the storage of sensitive authentication data after authorization.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Ensure sensitive authentication data is not stored post-authorization. WHO: Compliance officer. WHEN: Continuously monitored. HOW: Implement data handling procedures. WHERE: All systems processing cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Prohibition of Storing Sensitive Authentication Data
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Sensitive authentication data must not be stored after authorization is completed.
compliances.Scope: All systems processing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access to sensitive authentication data
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches leading to financial loss and reputational damage

mitigation.1: Implement tokenization or encryption of sensitive data
mitigation.2: Regularly audit systems for unauthorized data storage
mitigation.3: Train employees on data handling procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to stored sensitive authentication data, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Regular Audit of Data Handling Procedures
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance with data handling procedures.
compliances.Scope: All systems processing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To verify adherence to data handling policies and identify any non-compliance
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to identify non-compliance leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement automated auditing tools
mitigation.2: Provide regular training on data handling procedures
mitigation.3: Enforce strict access controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance with data handling procedures, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Employee Training on Data Handling Procedures
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Provide regular training to employees on data handling procedures.
compliances.Scope: All employees handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure employees understand and follow data handling policies
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Employee error leading to non-compliance and data breaches

mitigation.1: Develop comprehensive training materials
mitigation.2: Conduct regular training sessions
mitigation.3: Monitor employee adherence to procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Employee error, non-compliance with data handling procedures
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires the masking of the Primary Account Number (PAN) when displayed.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Mask PAN when displayed. WHO: Application developers. WHEN: At the time of application development. HOW: Implement masking techniques. WHERE: All systems displaying cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Masking of PAN on User Interface
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All user interfaces displaying PAN must have the PAN masked to protect sensitive information.
compliances.Scope: All applications developed by the organization
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access to PAN information
compliances.PossibleDamage: Exposure of sensitive cardholder data leading to potential fraud

mitigation.1: Implement encryption for stored PAN data
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor access to PAN information
mitigation.3: Implement access controls to restrict PAN visibility

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to PAN data, data breaches, financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All log files containing PAN must have the PAN masked to prevent exposure of sensitive information.
compliances.Scope: All systems generating log files with PAN information
compliances.Objective: To secure PAN data from unauthorized access in log files
compliances.PossibleDamage: Exposure of sensitive cardholder data in log files

mitigation.1: Implement log file encryption
mitigation.2: Regularly review and rotate log files
mitigation.3: Implement access controls to restrict PAN visibility in logs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to log files, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All database queries involving PAN must have the PAN masked to protect sensitive information.
compliances.Scope: All database operations handling PAN data
compliances.Objective: To prevent exposure of PAN data in database queries
compliances.PossibleDamage: Exposure of sensitive cardholder data in database queries

mitigation.1: Implement parameterized queries
mitigation.2: Use stored procedures for PAN retrieval
mitigation.3: Implement access controls to restrict PAN visibility in queries

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: SQL injection attacks, data breaches

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates that PAN must be rendered unreadable wherever it is stored.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Render PAN unreadable when stored. WHO: IT security team. WHEN: At the time of data storage. HOW: Use strong encryption or hashing methods. WHERE: All systems storing cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All PAN data must be encrypted using strong encryption methods when stored.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that PAN data is protected from unauthorized access or theft.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breach leading to financial loss and reputational damage.

mitigation.1: Implement encryption protocols such as AES-256
mitigation.2: Regularly update encryption keys
mitigation.3: Implement access controls to limit data exposure

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to PAN data, data theft, regulatory fines

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All PAN data must be hashed using secure hashing algorithms when stored.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that PAN data is securely stored and protected from unauthorized access.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breach leading to financial loss and reputational damage.

mitigation.1: Implement secure hashing algorithms like SHA-256
mitigation.2: Regularly update hashing algorithms
mitigation.3: Implement access controls to limit data exposure

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach, unauthorized access to PAN data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement access controls to limit exposure of PAN data to authorized personnel only.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: To restrict access to PAN data and prevent unauthorized viewing or modification.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access leading to data breach and regulatory non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Implement role-based access controls
mitigation.2: Regularly review access permissions
mitigation.3: Monitor access logs for suspicious activity

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach, unauthorized access to PAN data

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires the documentation of data protection policies and operational procedures related to cardholder data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document all related data protection policies and procedures. WHO: Compliance officer. WHEN: Annually reviewed and updated. HOW: Maintain a centralized repository for documentation. WHERE: Accessible to all affected personnel.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All data protection policies and procedures related to cardholder data must be reviewed annually by the compliance officer.
compliances.Scope: All departments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure that data protection policies are up to date and in compliance with regulations
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to data breaches and financial penalties

mitigation.1: Regular training on updated policies
mitigation.2: Internal audits to ensure compliance
mitigation.3: External review by compliance experts

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach resulting in loss of customer trust and financial penalties
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All departments involved in handling cardholder data would be impacted

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain a centralized repository for all data protection policies and procedures related to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All departments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure easy access to updated policies for all affected personnel
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of centralized repository may lead to confusion and non-compliance

mitigation.1: Regular backups of repository
mitigation.2: Access controls to prevent unauthorized changes
mitigation.3: Regular audits of repository access logs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Confusion among personnel leading to non-compliance with data protection policies
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All departments involved in handling cardholder data would be impacted

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure that all affected personnel have access to the centralized repository of data protection policies and procedures.
compliances.Scope: All departments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Facilitate compliance by providing easy access to policies for all personnel
compliances.PossibleDamage: Restricted access may lead to non-compliance and errors in data handling

mitigation.1: Regular access reviews and updates
mitigation.2: Training on policy access and usage
mitigation.3: Implement role-based access controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Personnel unaware of updated policies leading to non-compliance
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All departments involved in handling cardholder data would be impacted

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for the encryption of cardholder data and the management of cryptographic keys. It is essential to protect sensitive information from unauthorized access and ensure compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy applies to all personnel involved in the handling of cardholder data and mandates the implementation of strong cryptographic practices.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units that handle cardholder data, including IT, security, and compliance teams. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure involved in the processing, storage, or transmission of cardholder data, including on-premises and cloud environments. Third-party vendors that interact with cardholder data are also included in this scope.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the confidentiality and integrity of cardholder data through effective encryption and key management practices. It aims to mitigate risks associated with data breaches and unauthorized access, ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements and protecting stakeholder interests.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the use of strong cryptography to encrypt cardholder data during storage and transmission. It specifies the encryption standards and protocols that must be used to safeguard sensitive information.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement strong encryption protocols (e.g., AES-256) for all cardholder data. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementation. WHEN: Encryption must be applied at all times when data is stored or transmitted. HOW: Use industry-standard encryption libraries and tools to encrypt data. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications that handle cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Implementation of AES-256 Encryption for Cardholder Data
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All cardholder data must be encrypted using AES-256 encryption algorithm.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and applications that handle cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure strong encryption is used to protect sensitive cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches and unauthorized access to cardholder data.

mitigation.1: Regularly update encryption libraries and tools
mitigation.2: Implement multi-factor authentication for access to encrypted data
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits and penetration testing

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, non-compliance penalties.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Continuous Encryption Monitoring and Compliance Audits
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regular monitoring of encryption protocols and conducting compliance audits to ensure adherence to encryption standards.
compliances.Scope: Applies to IT Security Team responsible for encryption implementation.
compliances.Objective: To maintain the integrity of encryption protocols and ensure ongoing compliance with encryption standards.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance penalties, data breaches due to outdated encryption protocols.

mitigation.1: Implement automated monitoring tools for encryption protocols
mitigation.2: Conduct regular compliance audits and assessments
mitigation.3: Provide regular training to IT Security Team on encryption best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance with encryption standards, outdated encryption protocols, unauthorized access to cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement secure encryption key management practices and regular rotation of encryption keys.
compliances.Scope: Applies to IT Security Team responsible for encryption key management.
compliances.Objective: To enhance the security of encryption keys and reduce the risk of unauthorized access.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Compromised encryption keys, unauthorized access to cardholder data.

mitigation.1: Implement secure key management practices
mitigation.2: Regularly rotate encryption keys
mitigation.3: Store encryption keys in secure, encrypted locations

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Compromised encryption keys, unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for managing cryptographic keys used for encryption. It includes guidelines for key generation, distribution, storage, rotation, and destruction.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and document key management processes for all cryptographic keys. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for key management. WHEN: Keys must be rotated at least annually or upon compromise. HOW: Use secure key management solutions to generate, store, and rotate keys. WHERE: Applies to all systems that utilize encryption for cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Document and implement key management processes for all cryptographic keys used for encryption.
compliances.Scope: All systems that utilize encryption for cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure secure management of cryptographic keys to protect sensitive data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Compromise of encryption keys leading to unauthorized access to sensitive data

mitigation.1: Regular security audits to detect any key compromise
mitigation.2: Implement multi-factor authentication for key access
mitigation.3: Encrypt keys at rest and in transit

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, regulatory fines
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Systems

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure all cryptographic keys are rotated at least annually or upon compromise.
compliances.Scope: All systems that utilize encryption for cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To reduce the risk of key compromise by regularly changing encryption keys
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to rotate keys can lead to prolonged exposure of sensitive data

mitigation.1: Automate key rotation processes
mitigation.2: Implement key rotation reminders and alerts
mitigation.3: Regularly review key rotation logs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Prolonged exposure of sensitive data, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Systems

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement secure key storage solutions for cryptographic keys.
compliances.Scope: All systems that utilize encryption for cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To protect encryption keys from unauthorized access or theft
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to stored keys can lead to data breaches

mitigation.1: Use hardware security modules for key storage
mitigation.2: Implement access controls and encryption for stored keys
mitigation.3: Regularly audit key storage security

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to stored keys, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Systems

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires that all related security policies and operational procedures are documented and communicated to all affected personnel.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document all encryption and key management policies and procedures. WHO: Compliance Officer is responsible for documentation and communication. WHEN: Documentation must be reviewed and updated annually. HOW: Use a centralized policy management system to maintain and distribute documentation. WHERE: Applies to all departments handling cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Review of Encryption and Key Management Policies
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All encryption and key management policies and procedures must be reviewed and updated annually.
compliances.Scope: All departments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure that encryption and key management policies are up to date and effective
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches due to outdated encryption and key management policies

mitigation.1: Regular training on encryption and key management best practices
mitigation.2: Automated alerts for policy review deadlines

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All encryption and key management policies and procedures must be maintained and distributed using a centralized policy management system.

compliances.Scope: All departments handling cardholder data

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for protecting systems against malware threats. It is crucial to maintain the integrity and security of systems that process cardholder data. The policy mandates the deployment of anti-virus solutions and regular updates to safeguard against evolving malware threats.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units that manage systems processing cardholder data, including IT and security teams. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that are susceptible to malware, including personal computers, servers, and mobile devices. Third-party vendors with access to these systems are also included.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to protect systems from malware threats, ensuring the security and integrity of cardholder data. It aims to establish a proactive approach to malware management, ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements and minimizing risks associated with malware infections.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the deployment of anti-virus software on all systems commonly affected by malware. It includes guidelines for installation, configuration, and maintenance of anti-virus solutions.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Deploy anti-virus software on all systems commonly affected by malware. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for deployment. WHEN: Anti-virus software must be installed on all applicable systems before they are connected to the network. HOW: Use approved anti-virus solutions and configure them according to best practices. WHERE: Applies to all systems processing cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Deploy anti-virus software on all systems commonly affected by malware.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: To protect systems from malware attacks by ensuring the deployment of anti-virus software.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches, system downtime, loss of sensitive information

mitigation.1: Regular updates and patches for anti-virus software
mitigation.2: Continuous monitoring of anti-virus software effectiveness
mitigation.3: Regular security audits and assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Systems vulnerable to malware attacks, data breaches, financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units relying on secure systems

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Configure anti-virus solutions according to best practices.

compliances.Objective: To ensure anti-virus software is optimally configured for maximum effectiveness.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective malware protection, system vulnerabilities, data breaches

mitigation.1: Regular configuration reviews and updates
mitigation.2: Continuous monitoring of configuration changes
mitigation.3: Periodic security assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misconfigured anti-virus software, system vulnerabilities, malware infections
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units relying on secure systems

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regularly maintain and update anti-virus software.

compliances.Objective: To ensure anti-virus software remains effective against evolving malware threats.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated malware protection, system vulnerabilities, data breaches

mitigation.1: Scheduled maintenance and updates for anti-virus software
mitigation.2: Continuous monitoring of software performance
mitigation.3: Periodic security audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated anti-virus software, system vulnerabilities, malware infections
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units relying on secure systems

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires that all anti-virus mechanisms are kept current and that regular scans are performed to detect and mitigate malware threats.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Ensure anti-virus software is updated regularly and scans are conducted. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for updates and scans. WHEN: Updates must occur at least weekly, and scans must be performed daily. HOW: Use automated tools to manage updates and schedule scans. WHERE: Applies to all systems with anti-virus software installed.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All anti-virus software must be updated weekly to ensure the latest malware definitions are in place.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems with anti-virus software installed
compliances.Objective: To ensure that anti-virus software is up to date to effectively detect and mitigate malware threats
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of malware infections and data breaches due to outdated anti-virus software

mitigation.1: Automate software update process
mitigation.2: Implement real-time monitoring for outdated software
mitigation.3: Regularly review update logs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Increased likelihood of malware infections leading to data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Daily malware scans must be conducted on all systems with anti-virus software installed.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems with anti-virus software installed
compliances.Objective: To detect and mitigate malware threats on a daily basis
compliances.PossibleDamage: Undetected malware infections leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Automate scan scheduling
mitigation.2: Implement real-time monitoring for scan results
mitigation.3: Regularly review scan logs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected malware infections compromising system security

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Use automated tools to manage anti-virus software updates and schedule daily scans.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems with anti-virus software installed
compliances.Objective: To streamline the update and scanning process for efficiency and accuracy
compliances.PossibleDamage: Manual errors in update and scan scheduling leading to security vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regularly review automated tool logs

mitigation.3: Provide training on automated tool usage
