subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the generation and retention of audit logs for all anti-virus mechanisms to ensure accountability and traceability.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Generate audit logs for all anti-virus activities and retain them as per PCI DSS requirements. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for log management. WHEN: Logs must be generated in real-time and retained for at least one year. HOW: Use centralized logging solutions to collect and store logs. WHERE: Applies to all systems running anti-virus software.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Generate audit logs for all anti-virus activities in real-time
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems running anti-virus software
compliances.Objective: To ensure immediate traceability of all anti-virus activities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of real-time audit logs may lead to undetected security breaches

mitigation.1: Implement automated log generation tools
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor log generation process
mitigation.3: Implement alerts for log generation failures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, malware infections, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Data Protection Team

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Retention of Audit Logs for One Year
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Retain audit logs for all anti-virus activities for at least one year
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems running anti-virus software
compliances.Objective: To ensure long-term accountability and traceability of anti-virus activities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Loss of historical data for incident investigation and compliance audits

mitigation.1: Implement secure storage solutions for logs

mitigation.3: Implement access controls for log retrieval

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Use centralized logging solutions to collect and store audit logs
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems running anti-virus software
compliances.Objective: To streamline log management and ensure efficient retrieval and analysis of audit logs

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for managing vulnerabilities within systems that process cardholder data. It is essential to identify, assess, and remediate vulnerabilities to protect against potential exploitation. The policy mandates regular vulnerability scans and the application of security patches.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units responsible for system security, including IT, security, and compliance teams. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure involved in processing cardholder data, including on-premises and cloud environments. Third-party vendors with access to these systems are also included.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a systematic approach to vulnerability management, ensuring timely identification and remediation of vulnerabilities. It aims to enhance the security posture of systems processing cardholder data and ensure compliance with PCI DSS requirements.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the regular scanning of systems for vulnerabilities to identify weaknesses that could be exploited.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct vulnerability scans on all systems at least quarterly. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for scanning. WHEN: Scans must be performed at least every three months or after significant changes. HOW: Use approved vulnerability scanning tools to perform scans. WHERE: Applies to all systems processing cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct vulnerability scans on all systems at least quarterly.
compliances.Scope: All systems processing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To identify weaknesses that could be exploited
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement patches promptly
mitigation.2: Regularly update vulnerability scanning tools
mitigation.3: Implement network segmentation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach resulting in loss of customer trust and financial penalties

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: IT Security Team is responsible for conducting vulnerability scans.
compliances.Scope: All systems processing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure accountability and expertise in vulnerability scanning
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inadequate scans leading to undetected vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regular training for IT Security Team on vulnerability scanning best practices
mitigation.2: Implement clear procedures for conducting scans
mitigation.3: Regularly review scan results

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected vulnerabilities leading to data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Scans must be performed at least every three months or after significant changes.
compliances.Scope: All systems processing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely identification of vulnerabilities post significant changes
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delayed detection of vulnerabilities post changes

mitigation.1: Implement change management process to trigger scans after changes
mitigation.2: Automate scanning schedule based on change logs
mitigation.3: Regularly review scan reports for post-change vulnerabilities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unidentified vulnerabilities post system changes

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires that identified vulnerabilities are remediated based on their risk ranking and priority.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Remediate identified vulnerabilities according to risk ranking (high, medium, low). WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for remediation. WHEN: High-risk vulnerabilities must be addressed within one month, medium within three months, and low within six months. HOW: Use a risk-based approach to prioritize remediation efforts. WHERE: Applies to all systems with identified vulnerabilities.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: High-risk vulnerabilities must be remediated within one month of identification.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely remediation of high-risk vulnerabilities to reduce potential security breaches.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches, system compromise, and unauthorized access.

mitigation.1: Patch management process
mitigation.2: Regular vulnerability scans
mitigation.3: Security training for IT staff

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, financial loss, reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Medium-risk vulnerabilities must be remediated within three months of identification.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely remediation of medium-risk vulnerabilities to reduce security risks.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches, system vulnerabilities exploitation.

mitigation.1: Regular vulnerability assessments
mitigation.2: Security awareness training for IT staff
mitigation.3: Implementing security controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, system vulnerabilities exploitation

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Low-risk vulnerabilities must be remediated within six months of identification.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely remediation of low-risk vulnerabilities to maintain overall system security.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Minor data exposure, limited system impact.

mitigation.1: Regular vulnerability scanning
mitigation.2: Implementing security best practices
mitigation.3: Periodic security audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Limited data exposure, minor system impact

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for applying security patches to systems to protect against known vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Apply security patches to all systems promptly after release. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for patch management. WHEN: Critical patches must be applied within one month of release. HOW: Use a patch management system to track and apply patches. WHERE: Applies to all systems processing cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All critical security patches must be applied to systems within one month of release.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely protection against known vulnerabilities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of exploitation of vulnerabilities leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Automate patch deployment processes
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor patch release notifications
mitigation.3: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, unauthorized access, system downtime

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Use a patch management system to track and apply security patches.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure efficient and accurate patch deployment
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective patch management leading to system vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regularly update patch management system
mitigation.2: Implement access controls for patch management tools
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits of patch management processes

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: System downtime, unauthorized access

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: IT Security Team is responsible for patch management procedures.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Establish clear accountability for patch management
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of clear responsibility leading to patch neglect

mitigation.1: Define roles and responsibilities for patch management
mitigation.2: Provide training on patch management best practices
mitigation.3: Implement regular performance evaluations for IT Security Team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Patch delays, system vulnerabilities

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for restricting access to cardholder data based on business need-to-know principles. It aims to ensure that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive information, thereby minimizing the risk of unauthorized access and data breaches. The policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers who interact with cardholder data.
policies.PolicyName: Access Control Policy for Cardholder Data
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, personnel, systems, applications, and infrastructure that process, store, or transmit cardholder data. It covers all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers with access to cardholder data environments, including on-premises and cloud-based systems.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to limit access to cardholder data to authorized personnel only, thereby protecting sensitive information from unauthorized access. Specific goals include ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements, reducing the risk of data breaches, and maintaining operational effectiveness in data handling.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for managing user access to cardholder data, including the assignment of unique user IDs and the implementation of authentication mechanisms.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement user access management procedures to assign unique user IDs and restrict access based on job responsibilities. WHO: IT Security Team and HR Department. WHEN: User access must be reviewed quarterly and updated as necessary. HOW: Use an access control system to manage user IDs and permissions. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications that store or process cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: User access must be reviewed quarterly and updated as necessary to ensure compliance with user access management procedures.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that store or process cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To maintain the security of cardholder data by regularly reviewing and updating user access permissions
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: IT Security Team and HR Department
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and non-compliance with regulations

mitigation.1: Implement automated user access review tools
mitigation.2: Provide regular training on user access management procedures
mitigation.3: Enforce strict access control policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive cardholder data, data breaches, regulatory fines

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement procedures to assign unique user IDs to all users accessing cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that store or process cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure accountability and traceability of user actions by assigning unique user IDs
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-repudiation issues, unauthorized access, data breaches

mitigation.1: Enforce strong password policies for user IDs
mitigation.2: Regularly audit user ID assignments
mitigation.3: Implement user ID rotation policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: User impersonation, unauthorized data access, compliance violations

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Use an access control system to manage user IDs and permissions for systems and applications storing or processing cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that store or process cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To enforce access control policies and prevent unauthorized access to cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, non-compliance with industry standards, financial losses

mitigation.1: Regularly update access control system patches
mitigation.2: Conduct regular access control system audits
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication for access control system

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized data access, data breaches, compliance violations

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes requirements for controlling physical access to cardholder data environments, ensuring that only authorized personnel can access sensitive areas.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement physical access controls, including ID badges and visitor logs, to monitor and restrict access to sensitive areas. WHO: Facilities Management and Security Personnel. WHEN: Access controls must be enforced at all times. HOW: Use access control systems and maintain logs of all personnel entering sensitive areas. WHERE: Applies to all facilities housing cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement physical access controls, including ID badges and visitor logs, to monitor and restrict access to sensitive areas as per the Physical Access Control subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: All facilities housing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure only authorized personnel can access sensitive areas
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive areas leading to data breaches and compliance violations

mitigation.1: Regular audits of access logs
mitigation.2: Employee training on access control procedures
mitigation.3: Periodic review of access control systems

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized individuals gaining access to sensitive areas and compromising cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Enforcement of Access Controls at All Times
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Access controls must be enforced at all times to restrict access to sensitive areas as per the Physical Access Control subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: All facilities housing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To maintain the security of sensitive areas by ensuring continuous enforcement of access controls
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inconsistent enforcement leading to unauthorized access and security breaches

mitigation.1: Regular monitoring of access control systems
mitigation.2: Automated alerts for unauthorized access attempts
mitigation.3: Escalation procedures for non-compliance

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Intermittent enforcement of access controls leading to security vulnerabilities

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain logs of all personnel entering sensitive areas to track access and identify unauthorized entries as per the Physical Access Control subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: All facilities housing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure accountability and traceability of access to sensitive areas
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of visibility into access history leading to difficulty in identifying security incidents

mitigation.1: Regular review of access logs
mitigation.2: Automated log retention policies
mitigation.3: Integration with security incident response procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inability to track unauthorized access due to lack of access logs

versions.PolicyName: Access Control Policy for Cardholder Data

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy defines the requirements for user identification and authentication to ensure that all access to cardholder data is traceable to authorized users. It emphasizes the importance of unique user IDs and strong authentication methods to protect sensitive information.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all personnel with access to cardholder data, including employees, contractors, and third-party service providers. It encompasses all systems and applications that process or store cardholder data, regardless of location.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all users accessing cardholder data are uniquely identified and authenticated, thereby enhancing security and accountability. Goals include compliance with PCI DSS requirements, reducing the risk of unauthorized access, and ensuring that all access actions can be traced to specific individuals.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the process for assigning unique user IDs to all individuals with access to cardholder data, ensuring that actions can be traced back to authorized users.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Assign unique user IDs to all users before granting access to cardholder data. WHO: IT Security Team. WHEN: User IDs must be assigned prior to access being granted. HOW: Maintain a centralized user management system to track user IDs. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications that store or process cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Assign unique user IDs to all individuals with access to cardholder data before granting access.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and applications that store or process cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure actions can be traced back to authorized users accessing cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, leading to data breaches and compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Implement multi-factor authentication for user access
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update user access permissions
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic user access audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, regulatory fines.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain a centralized user management system to track unique user IDs.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and applications that store or process cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure efficient tracking and management of user IDs for individuals accessing cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Loss of user ID tracking, unauthorized access to cardholder data.

mitigation.1: Regularly backup user ID data
mitigation.2: Implement access controls to the user management system
mitigation.3: Train staff on proper use of the user management system

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Loss of user ID tracking, unauthorized access to cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Assign user IDs to individuals before granting access to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and applications that store or process cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure user IDs are assigned and tracked prior to granting access to sensitive cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Implement user ID assignment workflow
mitigation.2: Automate user ID assignment process
mitigation.3: Enforce user ID assignment policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, compliance violations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the authentication methods that must be used to verify user identities before granting access to cardholder data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement strong authentication mechanisms, including multi-factor authentication for remote access. WHO: IT Security Team. WHEN: Authentication methods must be enforced at all times. HOW: Use a combination of passwords, tokens, and biometric methods for authentication. WHERE: Applies to all access points to cardholder data environments.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement multi-factor authentication for remote access to cardholder data environments.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: To enhance security by requiring multiple authentication factors for remote access.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Enforce strong password policies
mitigation.2: Regularly update authentication tokens
mitigation.3: Implement biometric authentication methods

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive cardholder data, data breaches, financial losses

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Enforce the use of strong passwords for authentication to cardholder data environments.
compliances.Scope: All Employees
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access through weak password usage.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Implement password complexity requirements
mitigation.2: Enforce regular password changes
mitigation.3: Implement account lockout policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Weak password usage leading to unauthorized access, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement biometric authentication methods for access to cardholder data environments.
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: To enhance security by using unique biometric identifiers for authentication.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access, data breaches

mitigation.1: Regularly update biometric templates
mitigation.2: Implement fallback authentication methods
mitigation.3: Conduct regular biometric system audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Biometric system failures, unauthorized access

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for tracking and monitoring access to network resources and cardholder data. It is crucial for forensic analysis and vulnerability management, ensuring that all access is logged and can be audited. The policy aims to protect sensitive data by implementing robust logging mechanisms across all environments.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units that handle cardholder data, including stores, regional offices, headquarters, and remote access points. It encompasses all systems, applications, and infrastructure involved in payment processing, including third-party service providers. The policy covers all types of cardholder data and operational environments, including on-premises and cloud-based systems.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure comprehensive monitoring and logging of access to cardholder data, facilitating effective forensic analysis and compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with unauthorized access and data breaches, ensuring that all access is traceable and auditable.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing audit trails that link all access to system components to individual users. It specifies the types of events that must be logged and the necessary security measures to protect these logs.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement audit trails for all system components to track user access. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementation. WHEN: Audit trails must be established prior to system deployment and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Use automated logging tools to capture access events and secure logs against tampering. WHERE: Applies to all systems handling cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize automated logging tools to capture access events as specified in the subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: All systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure accurate and timely logging of user access events
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access events may go undetected, leading to potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Regularly monitor and review access logs for anomalies
mitigation.2: Implement multi-factor authentication for user access
mitigation.3: Encrypt log files to prevent unauthorized access

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access events may lead to data breaches, financial losses, and reputational damage.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Handling Systems

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review audit trails quarterly to ensure completeness and accuracy of logged events.
compliances.Scope: All systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To verify the effectiveness of the audit trail implementation and identify any discrepancies or anomalies.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to review audit trails may result in undetected security incidents or compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Establish automated alerts for suspicious activities in audit logs
mitigation.2: Conduct regular training for IT Security Team on audit trail review best practices
mitigation.3: Implement periodic audits of audit trail review processes

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected security incidents, compliance violations, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Handling Systems

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Secure audit logs against tampering by encrypting log files.
compliances.Scope: All systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access or modification of audit logs.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unencrypted audit logs may be tampered with, leading to falsified records or undetected security incidents.

mitigation.1: Implement strong encryption algorithms for audit log files
mitigation.2: Store encryption keys securely and restrict access to authorized personnel only
mitigation.3: Regularly monitor integrity of audit logs for signs of tampering

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Falsified audit records, undetected security incidents, compliance violations
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Handling Systems

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the regular review of logs and security events to identify anomalies or suspicious activities. It establishes the frequency and procedures for log reviews.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Review logs for anomalies and suspicious activities. WHO: Security Analysts are responsible for conducting reviews. WHEN: Logs must be reviewed daily for critical events. HOW: Utilize log analysis tools to automate the detection of anomalies. WHERE: Applies to all systems within the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Security Analysts must review logs daily for critical events using log analysis tools to detect anomalies and suspicious activities.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely detection of anomalies and suspicious activities in log data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to detect and respond to security incidents promptly could lead to unauthorized access or data breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement real-time monitoring tools
mitigation.2: Provide regular training to Security Analysts on log analysis
mitigation.3: Establish incident response procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, compliance violations

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Logs must be reviewed daily for critical events, weekly for significant events, and monthly for trending analysis.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: Ensure comprehensive review of logs to identify anomalies and trends
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inadequate log review frequency may result in undetected security incidents and breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement automated log review schedules
mitigation.2: Assign dedicated resources for weekly and monthly reviews
mitigation.3: Regularly update log analysis tools

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected security incidents, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Security Analysts must utilize log analysis tools to automate the detection of anomalies in log data.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: Enhance efficiency and accuracy of anomaly detection in log data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Manual log analysis may lead to oversight of critical security events and vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Regularly update log analysis tools

mitigation.3: Implement automated alerting mechanisms

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected anomalies, security incidents

subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Retain audit trail history for a minimum of one year. WHO: Compliance Officer is responsible for retention management. WHEN: Audit trails must be reviewed and archived annually. HOW: Implement a secure log management system to store and retrieve logs. WHERE: Applies to all systems that process cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Retain audit trail history for a minimum of one year
compliances.Scope: All systems that process cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that audit trail history is retained for analysis and compliance purposes
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Loss of critical audit trail data, non-compliance with regulations

mitigation.1: Regular backups of audit trail data
mitigation.2: Encryption of stored logs
mitigation.3: Access controls to restrict unauthorized access

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, regulatory fines, loss of customer trust
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units relying on audit trail data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Compliance Officer is responsible for retention management
compliances.Scope: All compliance-related activities
compliances.Objective: To ensure clear ownership and accountability for audit trail retention
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of oversight, mismanagement of audit trail data

mitigation.1: Regular audits of compliance officer activities
mitigation.2: Training on retention policies and procedures
mitigation.3: Establishing backup roles for continuity

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Mismanagement of audit trail data, lack of oversight

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Audit trails must be reviewed and archived annually
compliances.Scope: All systems with audit trail capabilities
compliances.Objective: To ensure that audit trails are regularly reviewed for accuracy and completeness
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units utilizing audit trail data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated or incomplete audit trail data, missed security incidents

mitigation.1: Automated alerts for review deadlines
mitigation.2: Regular training on audit trail review procedures
mitigation.3: Documentation of review activities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Missed security incidents, inaccurate reporting
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units relying on audit trail accuracy

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for regularly testing security systems and processes to identify and remediate vulnerabilities. It is essential for maintaining the security of cardholder data and ensuring compliance with PCI DSS standards.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all systems and applications that process, store, or transmit cardholder data. It includes all organizational units, personnel involved in security testing, and third-party service providers. The policy covers all operational environments, including on-premises and cloud systems.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all systems are regularly tested for vulnerabilities, thereby reducing the risk of data breaches and ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy aims to maintain a proactive security posture through continuous monitoring and remediation of vulnerabilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the implementation of internal and external network vulnerability scans to identify security weaknesses. It specifies the frequency and procedures for conducting these scans.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct internal and external vulnerability scans. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for execution. WHEN: Scans must be performed quarterly and after significant network changes. HOW: Utilize approved scanning tools and vendors for vulnerability assessment. WHERE: Applies to all network components within the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: IT Security Team must conduct internal network vulnerability scans quarterly using approved scanning tools and vendors.
compliances.Scope: All network components within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To identify security weaknesses within the internal network
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of undetected vulnerabilities leading to potential data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement regular internal vulnerability scans
mitigation.2: Address vulnerabilities identified promptly
mitigation.3: Implement network segmentation to limit exposure

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected vulnerabilities leading to unauthorized access and data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Data Protection Team

compliances.ComplianceTitle: External Network Vulnerability Scans After Significant Changes
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: IT Security Team must conduct external network vulnerability scans after significant network changes using approved scanning tools and vendors.
compliances.Scope: All network components within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To identify security weaknesses within the external network after significant changes
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of undetected vulnerabilities leading to potential data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement external vulnerability scans after significant changes
mitigation.2: Address vulnerabilities identified promptly
mitigation.3: Implement firewall rules to restrict access

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected vulnerabilities after significant network changes leading to unauthorized access and data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Data Protection Team

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Approved Scanning Tools and Vendors for Vulnerability Assessment
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: IT Security Team must utilize approved scanning tools and vendors for conducting vulnerability assessments.
compliances.Scope: All network components within the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To ensure standardized and effective vulnerability assessment practices
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective vulnerability assessments leading to undetected vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update approved scanning tools and vendors list
mitigation.2: Conduct periodic assessments of scanning tool effectiveness
mitigation.3: Implement training for IT Security Team on tool usage

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Use of ineffective scanning tools and vendors resulting in undetected vulnerabilities
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Data Protection Team

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting penetration testing to assess the security of systems and applications. It establishes the frequency and methodology for testing.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Perform internal and external penetration testing. WHO: Qualified security professionals are responsible for testing. WHEN: Testing must occur annually and after significant changes. HOW: Develop a testing methodology that includes both external and internal assessments. WHERE: Applies to all systems that process cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct internal penetration testing annually to assess the security of systems processing cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems processing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To identify and address security vulnerabilities within the internal network
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, unauthorized access to sensitive information

mitigation.1: Implement security patches regularly
mitigation.2: Enforce strong password policies
mitigation.3: Implement network segmentation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct external penetration testing after significant changes to systems processing cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: Systems processing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To identify and address security vulnerabilities introduced by significant changes
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, unauthorized access to sensitive information

mitigation.1: Perform regular security audits after changes
mitigation.2: Implement change management processes
mitigation.3: Monitor network traffic for anomalies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Exploitation of vulnerabilities introduced by changes

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Develop a testing methodology that includes both external and internal assessments for penetration testing.
compliances.Scope: All systems processing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure comprehensive testing of systems from both internal and external perspectives
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, unauthorized access to sensitive information

mitigation.1: Engage third-party security experts for testing
mitigation.2: Regularly update testing methodology based on industry standards
mitigation.3: Implement multi-layered security controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incomplete testing leading to undetected vulnerabilities

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the testing for unauthorized wireless access points to ensure network security. It specifies the procedures for detection and response.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Test for the presence of unauthorized wireless access points. WHO: Network Security Team is responsible for testing. WHEN: Testing must be conducted quarterly. HOW: Use wireless detection tools to identify unauthorized access points and implement incident response procedures. WHERE: Applies to all organizational locations handling cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Quarterly Testing for Unauthorized Wireless Access Points
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct quarterly testing to detect unauthorized wireless access points using wireless detection tools.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all organizational locations handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure network security by identifying and responding to unauthorized wireless access points
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential unauthorized access to sensitive data leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement incident response procedures
mitigation.2: Regularly update wireless detection tools
mitigation.3: Provide ongoing training to network security team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, regulatory fines

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regularly update wireless detection tools to ensure effectiveness in identifying unauthorized access points
compliances.Scope: Applies to Network Security Team
compliances.Objective: Maintain up-to-date tools for accurate detection of unauthorized wireless access points
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective detection of unauthorized access points leading to security breaches

mitigation.1: Establish a schedule for tool updates
mitigation.2: Test tools after updates to ensure functionality
mitigation.3: Maintain a backup system in case of tool failure

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security breaches, data leaks, network vulnerabilities

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement incident response procedures for unauthorized wireless access points
compliances.Scope: Applies to Network Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely and effective response to unauthorized access points
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delayed or ineffective response to security incidents

mitigation.1: Regularly train team members on incident response
mitigation.2: Conduct drills to test response procedures
mitigation.3: Maintain communication channels for incident reporting

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, unauthorized access, network downtime

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for maintaining information security across the organization. It defines roles, responsibilities, and procedures for protecting cardholder data and ensuring compliance with PCI DSS.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers who have access to cardholder data. It encompasses all organizational units and locations, including remote access points. The policy covers all aspects of information security related to cardholder data.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all personnel are aware of their security responsibilities and that the organization maintains a strong security posture. The policy aims to protect cardholder data and ensure compliance with PCI DSS requirements through effective communication and training.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for developing and maintaining the information security policy. It specifies the review and update process.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and maintain the information security policy. WHO: Information Security Officer is responsible for policy management. WHEN: The policy must be reviewed annually and updated as needed. HOW: Conduct a formal review process involving key stakeholders. WHERE: Applies to all organizational units handling cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The information security policy must be reviewed annually to ensure it remains up-to-date and effective.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure the information security policy is regularly reviewed and updated to address changing security needs
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to review the policy annually may result in outdated security measures and increased vulnerability to security breaches.

mitigation.1: Schedule regular policy review meetings with key stakeholders
mitigation.2: Implement a tracking system to monitor policy review progress
mitigation.3: Engage external auditors to provide independent policy review

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated policy leading to non-compliance with security standards, increased risk of data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The information security policy must be updated as needed to reflect changes in security requirements.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure the information security policy remains current and aligned with evolving security standards
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to update the policy as needed may result in non-compliance with security regulations and increased risk of security incidents.

mitigation.1: Establish a clear process for policy updates
mitigation.2: Communicate policy changes to all relevant stakeholders
mitigation.3: Provide training on updated policy requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated policy leading to non-compliance with security standards, increased risk of data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct a formal review process involving key stakeholders to ensure the information security policy is comprehensive and effective.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To involve key stakeholders in the policy review process to gather diverse perspectives and ensure policy effectiveness
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of involvement of key stakeholders in the policy review process may result in oversight of critical security requirements and gaps in policy coverage.

mitigation.1: Establish a cross-functional review team with representatives from all relevant departments
mitigation.2: Document feedback and suggestions from stakeholders during the review process
mitigation.3: Implement a feedback loop to address stakeholder concerns

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Oversight of critical security requirements, gaps in policy coverage
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the implementation of a risk assessment process to identify critical assets, threats, and vulnerabilities. It establishes the frequency and procedures for conducting assessments.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct risk assessments to identify threats and vulnerabilities. WHO: Risk Management Team is responsible for assessments. WHEN: Assessments must be performed annually and after significant changes. HOW: Utilize a formal risk assessment methodology to evaluate risks. WHERE: Applies to all systems and processes related to cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct annual risk assessments to identify threats and vulnerabilities as per the risk assessment process subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and processes related to cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure regular identification of critical assets, threats, and vulnerabilities.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches and financial losses due to unidentified risks.

mitigation.1: Implement regular training for risk assessment team
mitigation.2: Utilize automated risk assessment tools
mitigation.3: Engage external auditors for independent assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to identify emerging threats leading to data breaches and non-compliance penalties.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct risk assessments after significant changes to identify new threats and vulnerabilities.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and processes related to cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely identification of risks introduced by changes.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unidentified risks from changes leading to security incidents and data breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement change management process with risk assessment step
mitigation.2: Conduct regular security audits post-change
mitigation.3: Engage external consultants for change impact assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to assess risks post-change leading to security vulnerabilities and non-compliance.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize a formal risk assessment methodology to evaluate risks as per the risk assessment process subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and processes related to cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure consistent and thorough evaluation of risks.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate risk assessments leading to inadequate risk mitigation.

mitigation.1: Training on formal risk assessment methodologies
mitigation.2: Regular review and update of assessment procedures
mitigation.3: External certification of risk assessment processes

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate risk assessments leading to unidentified critical vulnerabilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the proper use of critical technologies by personnel. It establishes guidelines for remote access, wireless, and other technologies.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop usage policies for critical technologies. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for policy development. WHEN: Policies must be reviewed and updated annually. HOW: Communicate policies to all personnel and provide training. WHERE: Applies to all organizational units and personnel with access to cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Review of Critical Technologies Usage Policies
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All critical technologies usage policies must be reviewed and updated annually by the IT Security Team.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units and personnel with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that usage policies for critical technologies remain up-to-date and relevant
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to unauthorized access to critical technologies and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Conduct regular policy reviews and updates
mitigation.2: Provide training on policy changes
mitigation.3: Implement access controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to critical technologies, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Communication of Critical Technologies Usage Policies
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All personnel must be informed of and trained on the usage policies for critical technologies.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units and personnel with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all personnel are aware of and adhere to the usage policies for critical technologies
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of awareness may lead to misuse of critical technologies and security incidents.

mitigation.1: Regular training sessions on policy guidelines
mitigation.2: Provide easy access to policy documents
mitigation.3: Implement monitoring and reporting mechanisms

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misuse of critical technologies, security incidents

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement access controls to restrict unauthorized access to critical technologies.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units and personnel with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access and misuse of critical technologies
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access may lead to data breaches, loss of sensitive information.

mitigation.1: Implement role-based access controls
mitigation.2: Regularly review access permissions
mitigation.3: Monitor access logs for suspicious activities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, unauthorized access

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for assigning information security responsibilities within the organization. It ensures that specific individuals or teams are designated to oversee security measures related to cardholder data, thereby enhancing accountability and compliance with PCI DSS requirements.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in the processing, storage, or transmission of cardholder data, including IT, HR, and compliance departments. It encompasses all personnel, systems, and processes that interact with cardholder data across all geographic locations.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all information security responsibilities are clearly assigned and understood, leading to improved security posture and compliance with PCI DSS. Measurable outcomes include the identification of responsible parties and the establishment of accountability mechanisms.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the process for assigning specific security roles and responsibilities to individuals or teams within the organization.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Assign security roles based on job functions and responsibilities. WHO: Information Security Officer (ISO) and department heads. WHEN: At the time of hiring and during annual reviews. HOW: Utilize a role-based access control framework to define and document roles. WHERE: Across all departments handling cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Assignment of Security Roles Based on Job Functions
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Assign security roles based on job functions and responsibilities as outlined in the subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: All departments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure that security roles are assigned appropriately to individuals based on their job functions to maintain data security.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, non-compliance penalties

mitigation.1: Regular training on role-based access control framework
mitigation.2: Periodic role reviews and adjustments
mitigation.3: Strict access control measures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, insider threats, unauthorized access

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Utilization of Role-Based Access Control Framework
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize a role-based access control framework to define and document security roles.
compliances.Scope: All departments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure that security roles are clearly defined and documented using a role-based access control framework.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Confusion in role assignments, unauthorized access, compliance violations

mitigation.1: Regular audits of role assignments
mitigation.2: Training on role-based access control framework
mitigation.3: Automated role assignment processes

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Role confusion, unauthorized access, compliance violations

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Assignment of Security Roles During Annual Reviews
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review and adjust security roles during annual performance reviews.
compliances.Scope: All departments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure that security roles are periodically reviewed and adjusted to align with job functions and responsibilities.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated role assignments, increased risk of unauthorized access, compliance gaps

mitigation.1: Include role review as part of annual performance evaluations
mitigation.2: Provide training on updated role assignments
mitigation.3: Implement role change approval process

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated role assignments, unauthorized access, compliance violations

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy mandates the implementation of a formal security awareness program to educate all personnel about cardholder data security policies and procedures. It aims to foster a culture of security awareness and compliance throughout the organization.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers who have access to cardholder data or systems that process such data. It includes all training materials, sessions, and assessments conducted across all locations.
policies.Objective: The objective is to ensure that all personnel are knowledgeable about security policies and practices, leading to reduced risk of data breaches. Measurable outcomes include training completion rates and assessment scores.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for implementing the security awareness training program.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and deliver security awareness training. WHO: Human Resources and Information Security teams. WHEN: Annually and upon onboarding new employees. HOW: Use e-learning platforms and in-person sessions to deliver training. WHERE: All organizational locations and remote work environments.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Human Resources and Information Security teams must develop and deliver security awareness training annually to all employees.
compliances.Scope: All organizational locations and remote work environments
compliances.Objective: Ensure all employees receive regular security awareness training to mitigate risks
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased susceptibility to security breaches and data leaks

mitigation.1: Regular monitoring of employee completion rates
mitigation.2: Implementing consequences for non-compliance
mitigation.3: Providing incentives for participation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Employees falling victim to phishing attacks due to lack of awareness

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Human Resources and Information Security teams must deliver security awareness training to all new employees upon onboarding.
compliances.Scope: All organizational locations and remote work environments
compliances.Objective: Ensure new employees are equipped with security knowledge from the start
compliances.PossibleDamage: New employees unintentionally causing security incidents due to lack of awareness

mitigation.1: Include security training in onboarding checklist
mitigation.2: Assign a mentor for new employees to guide them on security practices
mitigation.3: Provide easy access to security resources and contact points

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: New employees falling victim to social engineering attacks

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Security awareness training must be delivered using e-learning platforms in addition to in-person sessions.
compliances.Scope: All organizational locations and remote work environments
compliances.Objective: Provide flexible and accessible training options for employees
compliances.PossibleDamage: Limited engagement and effectiveness of training due to lack of varied delivery methods

mitigation.1: Regularly update e-learning content to keep it relevant
mitigation.2: Monitor employee feedback on training platforms for improvements
mitigation.3: Provide support resources for employees experiencing technical difficulties

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Employees disengaging from training due to lack of interactive content

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the procedures for screening potential personnel to minimize the risk of internal threats to cardholder data security. It establishes a framework for conducting background checks and assessments.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all new hires and contractors who will have access to cardholder data or systems that process such data. It covers all geographic locations where the organization operates.
policies.Objective: The objective is to ensure that all personnel with access to sensitive data are thoroughly vetted to reduce the risk of internal attacks. Measurable outcomes include the completion of background checks and the identification of any red flags.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the procedures for conducting background checks on potential employees.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct comprehensive background checks. WHO: Human Resources and Security teams. WHEN: Prior to hiring and at regular intervals for existing employees. HOW: Utilize third-party services for background checks and document findings. WHERE: All hiring locations and remote work environments.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All potential employees must undergo thorough background checks before being hired.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all hiring locations and remote work environments
compliances.Objective: To ensure the safety and security of the organization by screening potential employees effectively.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Hiring individuals with criminal records or false credentials could lead to legal liabilities, reputational damage, and security risks.

mitigation.1: Verify the accuracy of background check reports with multiple sources
mitigation.2: Implement regular audits of background check procedures
mitigation.3: Provide training to HR and Security teams on conducting effective background checks

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Hiring an individual with a criminal record who poses a threat to the organization's security and reputation.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Human Resources and Security departments would be directly impacted by the risk

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Regular Background Checks for Existing Employees
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Existing employees must undergo periodic background checks at regular intervals.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all existing employees
compliances.Objective: To ensure that employees maintain their eligibility and trustworthiness throughout their tenure.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to conduct regular background checks could result in employing individuals with newly acquired criminal records or misconduct.

mitigation.1: Establish a clear schedule for conducting background checks on existing employees
mitigation.2: Communicate the importance of background checks to employees to maintain transparency
mitigation.3: Implement a process for addressing any discrepancies found in background checks

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: An existing employee with a newly acquired criminal record engages in misconduct that harms the organization.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Human Resources and Security departments would be directly impacted by the risk

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All findings from background checks must be documented and maintained for record-keeping purposes.
compliances.Scope: Applies to Human Resources and Security teams
compliances.Objective: To ensure transparency and compliance with legal requirements regarding background checks.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to document background check findings could lead to legal non-compliance and challenges in defending hiring decisions.

mitigation.1: Establish a centralized system for storing background check reports securely
mitigation.2: Implement access controls to restrict unauthorized viewing of sensitive information
mitigation.3: Regularly review and update documentation retention policies to comply with legal standards

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Legal challenges arise due to lack of documentation on background check findings.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Human Resources and Security departments would be directly impacted by the risk

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for managing service providers that handle cardholder data. It ensures that service providers are compliant with PCI DSS and that their security practices align with the organization's standards.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all third-party service providers that process, store, or transmit cardholder data on behalf of the organization. It includes all contracts and agreements related to service providers.
policies.Objective: The objective is to ensure that all service providers are compliant with PCI DSS and that their security practices do not compromise cardholder data. Measurable outcomes include documented agreements and regular compliance reviews.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for service providers to acknowledge their security responsibilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Obtain written acknowledgment from service providers. WHO: Procurement and Compliance teams. WHEN: At the time of contract signing and during annual reviews. HOW: Include compliance clauses in contracts and maintain documentation. WHERE: All service provider agreements.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Obtain and maintain written acknowledgment from service providers regarding their security responsibilities in all service provider agreements.
compliances.Scope: All service provider agreements
compliances.Objective: To ensure service providers acknowledge their security responsibilities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to obtain acknowledgment may lead to service providers neglecting security responsibilities, resulting in data breaches or non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Regular audits to verify compliance
mitigation.2: Provide training to service providers on security best practices
mitigation.3: Implement penalties for non-compliance

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Service providers not adhering to security responsibilities, leading to data breaches or non-compliance.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Procurement, Compliance, and potentially all business units

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Include compliance clauses in all service provider contracts outlining their security responsibilities.
compliances.Scope: All service provider agreements
compliances.Objective: To legally bind service providers to acknowledge their security responsibilities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to include compliance clauses may result in service providers not being legally bound to security responsibilities, leading to non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Legal review of all contracts to ensure compliance clauses are included
mitigation.2: Regular contract audits to verify compliance
mitigation.3: Provide legal training to procurement and compliance teams

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Service providers not legally bound to security responsibilities, leading to non-compliance.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Procurement, Compliance, and potentially all business units

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct annual reviews of service provider agreements to ensure ongoing acknowledgment of security responsibilities.
compliances.Scope: All service provider agreements
compliances.Objective: To verify continued compliance and acknowledgment of security responsibilities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to conduct annual reviews may result in service providers neglecting security responsibilities over time, leading to non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Establish automated reminders for annual reviews
mitigation.2: Implement regular training sessions for service providers on security responsibilities
mitigation.3: Enforce penalties for non-compliance discovered during reviews

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Service providers gradually neglecting security responsibilities over time, leading to non-compliance.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Procurement, Compliance, and potentially all business units

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy mandates the implementation of an incident response plan to ensure the organization is prepared to respond to security breaches effectively. It outlines the procedures for identifying, responding to, and recovering from incidents.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all personnel involved in incident response, including IT, security, and management teams. It covers all systems and processes that handle cardholder data.
policies.Objective: The objective is to ensure a rapid and effective response to security incidents, minimizing damage and recovery time. Measurable outcomes include incident response times and the effectiveness of incident recovery efforts.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the procedures for responding to security incidents.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and implement incident response procedures. WHO: Incident Response Team and IT Security. WHEN: Immediately upon detection of an incident. HOW: Follow documented procedures for containment, eradication, and recovery. WHERE: All systems and locations handling cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Development and Implementation of Incident Response Procedures
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Develop and implement incident response procedures for immediate response to security incidents.
compliances.Scope: All systems and locations handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure a timely and effective response to security incidents to minimize potential damage
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches, financial losses, reputational damage

mitigation.1: Regular training and drills for incident response team
mitigation.2: Continuous monitoring of systems for early detection
mitigation.3: Regular review and update of incident response procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, financial losses, reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Respond immediately upon detection of a security incident following documented procedures for containment, eradication, and recovery.
compliances.Scope: All systems and locations handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To minimize the impact of security incidents by responding promptly and effectively
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data loss, system downtime, financial losses

mitigation.1: Automated incident response tools for faster detection and containment
mitigation.2: Regular testing and updating of incident response procedures
mitigation.3: Cross-training of incident response team members

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data loss, system downtime, financial losses

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Regular Review and Update of Incident Response Procedures
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regularly review and update incident response procedures to ensure they are effective and up-to-date.
compliances.Scope: All systems and locations handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To adapt incident response procedures to evolving security threats and technologies
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective incident response, increased vulnerability to security threats

mitigation.1: Annual review and update of incident response procedures
mitigation.2: Integration of lessons learned from past incidents into procedures
mitigation.3: Regular communication and training on updated procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Ineffective incident response, increased vulnerability to security threats

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Data Encryption for Cardholder Data Transmission and Emission
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Encrypt all cardholder data during transmission over open, public networks.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To protect sensitive information from unauthorized access during transmission
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement AES-256 encryption for data transmission
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor encryption protocols for compliance
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic security audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches resulting in financial penalties and loss of customer trust

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for compliance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS). It is essential for all entities that store, process, or transmit cardholder data to adhere to these standards to protect sensitive information and maintain trust with customers. The policy establishes a framework for assessing, reporting, and remediating compliance with PCI DSS requirements.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units and personnel involved in the handling of cardholder data, including IT, security, compliance, and management. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, including on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. The policy also encompasses third-party vendors and service providers that interact with cardholder data.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all entities comply with PCI DSS requirements to protect cardholder data, mitigate risks associated with data breaches, and maintain compliance with regulatory standards. Specific goals include achieving full compliance with PCI DSS, reducing vulnerabilities, and ensuring the security of cardholder data across all systems and processes.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for determining the scope of the cardholder data environment (CDE) and assessing compliance with PCI DSS requirements. It includes identifying all system components that handle cardholder data and ensuring they are included in the compliance assessment.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Identify and document all system components within the cardholder data environment. WHO: Compliance team, IT security personnel. WHEN: At least annually and prior to the annual assessment. HOW: Conduct a thorough review of network diagrams, data flow diagrams, and system inventories. WHERE: All locations where cardholder data is stored, processed, or transmitted, including backup and recovery sites.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Identify and document all system components within the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to compliance team and IT security personnel
compliances.Objective: To ensure all system components handling cardholder data are included in compliance assessment
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Regular audits of system components
mitigation.2: Implement access controls
mitigation.3: Encrypt cardholder data

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct a thorough review of network diagrams within the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to compliance team and IT security personnel
compliances.Objective: To ensure accurate representation of network components handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate network diagrams may lead to misidentification of system components

mitigation.1: Regularly update network diagrams
mitigation.2: Implement change management process
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic reviews

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misidentification of system components, non-compliance with PCI DSS

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review data flow diagrams within the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to compliance team and IT security personnel
compliances.Objective: To ensure accurate representation of data flows involving cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate data flow diagrams may lead to misinterpretation of data handling processes

mitigation.1: Regularly update data flow diagrams
mitigation.2: Implement data classification policies
mitigation.3: Conduct data flow analysis

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misinterpretation of data handling processes, data leakage

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for completing and submitting compliance documentation, including the Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ), Report on Compliance (ROC), and Attestation of Compliance (AOC). It ensures that all necessary documentation is accurately completed and submitted to the appropriate parties.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Complete and submit the SAQ, ROC, and AOC along with supporting documentation. WHO: Compliance officer, designated personnel from IT and management. WHEN: Within the timeframe specified by the payment card brands or acquirers. HOW: Utilize established templates and guidelines for documentation, ensuring all compensating controls are documented. WHERE: Documentation must be submitted to the acquirer or payment brand as required.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Completion and Submission of Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ)
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All designated personnel must accurately complete the SAQ and submit it to the compliance officer within the specified timeframe.
compliances.Scope: All business units handling payment card data
compliances.Objective: To ensure accurate completion and submission of SAQ for compliance purposes
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may result in fines, penalties, or suspension of payment processing services.

mitigation.1: Provide regular training on completing SAQ
mitigation.2: Implement automated reminders for submission deadlines
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits of completed SAQs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to submit SAQ on time, inaccurate completion of SAQ, lack of documentation supporting SAQ responses

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The compliance officer is responsible for compiling and submitting the ROC to the payment card brands or acquirers within the specified timeframe.
compliances.Scope: Compliance department
compliances.Objective: To provide a detailed report on the organization's compliance status
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to submit ROC may result in non-compliance fines or penalties.

mitigation.1: Establish clear submission guidelines and deadlines
mitigation.2: Conduct internal reviews of ROC before submission
mitigation.3: Engage external auditors for independent verification

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incomplete or inaccurate ROC submission, missed deadlines for ROC submission

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Designated personnel from IT and management must verify and attest to the organization's compliance status in the AOC document.
compliances.Scope: IT department, Management
compliances.Objective: To ensure accurate verification and attestation of compliance status
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate attestation may lead to non-compliance findings and penalties.

mitigation.1: Provide training on accurate attestation procedures
mitigation.2: Implement dual verification process for AOC
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits of AOC attestations

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inaccurate attestation in AOC, lack of supporting documentation for compliance claims

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the steps to be taken when compliance deficiencies are identified. It includes the process for remediating issues and updating compliance documentation accordingly.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Address and remediate any identified compliance deficiencies. WHO: IT security team, compliance officer. WHEN: Immediately upon identification of deficiencies, with updates provided within 30 days. HOW: Implement corrective actions, document changes, and update compliance reports. WHERE: All systems and processes identified in the compliance assessment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Address and remediate any identified compliance deficiencies immediately upon identification.
compliances.Scope: All systems and processes identified in the compliance assessment
compliances.Objective: Ensure prompt resolution of compliance issues to maintain regulatory adherence
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance fines, reputational damage

mitigation.1: Implement corrective actions promptly
mitigation.2: Document changes accurately
mitigation.3: Update compliance reports within 30 days
