compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to remediate compliance deficiencies leading to regulatory penalties and loss of trust

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Provide updates on compliance deficiencies remediation within 30 days of identification.
compliances.Scope: All systems and processes identified in the compliance assessment
compliances.Objective: Ensure transparency and accountability in compliance remediation efforts
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of visibility into compliance status, delayed corrective actions

mitigation.1: Establish clear reporting timelines
mitigation.2: Regularly communicate progress updates
mitigation.3: Escalate issues for prompt resolution

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of timely updates leading to prolonged non-compliance and regulatory scrutiny

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Document all changes made during compliance remediation process accurately.
compliances.Scope: All systems and processes identified in the compliance assessment
compliances.Objective: Maintain an audit trail of compliance remediation activities for accountability
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate documentation leading to audit failures, compliance breaches

mitigation.1: Implement robust documentation processes
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update documentation
mitigation.3: Conduct internal audits for accuracy

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inaccurate documentation leading to failed audits and compliance breaches

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for managing the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) to ensure compliance with PCI DSS. It emphasizes the importance of identifying, securing, and documenting cardholder data locations, as well as the processes for securely deleting or migrating data that is not in scope. The policy aims to maintain a secure environment for cardholder data and reduce the risk of data breaches.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units that handle cardholder data, including IT, compliance, and security teams. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that process, store, or transmit cardholder data, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy also includes third-party service providers that interact with the CDE.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all cardholder data is properly managed and secured within the CDE, reducing the risk of data breaches and ensuring compliance with PCI DSS. Specific goals include maintaining accurate documentation of data locations, securely managing data that is out of scope, and preparing for annual PCI DSS assessments.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for identifying and documenting all locations of cardholder data within the CDE. It includes procedures for securely deleting or migrating data that is not in scope and retaining documentation for compliance purposes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Identify and document all cardholder data locations within the CDE. Securely delete or migrate any data found outside the CDE. Retain documentation of scope determination. WHO: Compliance and IT teams are responsible for identifying and documenting data locations. WHEN: This process must be conducted annually and whenever new systems are introduced. HOW: Use inventory tools and regular audits to identify data locations. Documentation must be stored in a secure, accessible location for assessor review. WHERE: This applies to all systems and applications that process, store, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Identification and Documentation of CDE Cardholder Data Locations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Annually identify and document all cardholder data locations within the CDE using inventory tools and regular audits.
compliances.Scope: Compliance and IT teams
compliances.Objective: To ensure all cardholder data locations within the CDE are identified and documented annually.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to unauthorized access to cardholder data and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement regular audits to ensure ongoing compliance
mitigation.2: Provide training to staff on data identification procedures
mitigation.3: Implement access controls to restrict unauthorized access to data

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceTitle: New System Introduction Data Location Identification
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Identify and document cardholder data locations within the CDE whenever new systems are introduced.
compliances.Scope: Compliance and IT teams
compliances.Objective: To ensure all new systems introduced have their cardholder data locations identified and documented within the CDE.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to identify new system data locations may result in unsecured cardholder data and potential compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Implement a process for immediate data location identification upon system introduction
mitigation.2: Conduct regular training sessions for staff on data identification procedures
mitigation.3: Implement automated tools for continuous data monitoring

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unsecured cardholder data, compliance violations

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Secure Deletion or Migration of Data Outside CDE
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Securely delete or migrate any cardholder data found outside the CDE.
compliances.Scope: Compliance and IT teams
compliances.Objective: To ensure all cardholder data outside the CDE is securely deleted or migrated to the CDE.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to secure data outside the CDE may lead to data breaches and non-compliance with industry standards.

mitigation.1: Implement data encryption for secure migration
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor data movement to identify and address any non-compliant actions
mitigation.3: Establish clear policies and procedures for data deletion and migration

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, non-compliance

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for network segmentation to reduce the scope of the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE). It highlights the importance of isolating the CDE from other network components to minimize risk and compliance costs. The policy aims to ensure that only necessary systems are included in the CDE, thereby enhancing security and simplifying compliance.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all network infrastructure components, including routers, switches, firewalls, and servers that interact with the CDE. It covers all organizational units involved in network management and security, including IT and security teams. The policy is applicable to both on-premises and cloud environments.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to effectively reduce the scope of the CDE through proper network segmentation, thereby lowering compliance costs and risks. Specific goals include ensuring that only necessary systems are included in the CDE and maintaining documentation of segmentation methods.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing and documenting network segmentation to isolate the CDE from other network components. It includes procedures for maintaining documentation of segmentation methods and configurations.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement network segmentation to isolate the CDE from other systems. Maintain documentation of segmentation methods and configurations. WHO: Network and security teams are responsible for implementing and documenting segmentation. WHEN: Segmentation must be reviewed and updated annually or whenever changes are made to the network. HOW: Use network monitoring tools to ensure proper segmentation and maintain documentation in a secure repository. WHERE: This applies to all network components that interact with the CDE.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Network and security teams must conduct an annual review of segmentation methods and configurations to ensure the CDE is isolated from other systems.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network and security teams
compliances.Objective: To maintain the effectiveness of network segmentation and protect the CDE
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to review segmentation methods annually may lead to unauthorized access to the CDE, resulting in data breaches and non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Schedule regular annual reviews of segmentation methods
mitigation.2: Implement automated alerts for changes in network configurations
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic penetration testing to identify vulnerabilities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to the CDE, data breaches, non-compliance
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Network and security teams, CDE operations

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain detailed documentation of segmentation configurations for all network components that interact with the CDE.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network and security teams
compliances.Objective: To ensure visibility and accountability for segmentation configurations
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of documentation may lead to misconfigurations, mismanagement, and potential security vulnerabilities in the network.

mitigation.1: Implement a centralized repository for storing segmentation documentation
mitigation.2: Enforce documentation standards and version control
mitigation.3: Regularly audit documentation for accuracy and completeness

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misconfigurations, mismanagement, security vulnerabilities
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Network and security teams, CDE operations

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize network monitoring tools to ensure proper segmentation is maintained and detect any unauthorized access to the CDE.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network and security teams
compliances.Objective: To proactively identify and respond to potential security threats
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of monitoring may result in undetected security incidents, data breaches, and compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Implement continuous monitoring of network traffic for anomalies
mitigation.2: Set up alerts for suspicious activities or unauthorized access
mitigation.3: Regularly review monitoring logs and reports

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected security incidents, data breaches, compliance violations
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Network and security teams, CDE operations

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for managing third-party service providers that store, process, or transmit cardholder data on behalf of the organization. It emphasizes the importance of verifying the PCI DSS compliance of third-party providers and ensuring that appropriate agreements are in place. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with outsourcing cardholder data management.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all third-party service providers that handle cardholder data on behalf of the organization. It covers all organizational units involved in vendor management, compliance, and security. The policy is applicable to all geographic locations where the organization operates.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all third-party service providers comply with PCI DSS requirements, thereby reducing the risk of data breaches and ensuring the security of cardholder data. Specific goals include maintaining oversight of third-party compliance and ensuring that contracts clearly define responsibilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for verifying the PCI DSS compliance of third-party service providers. It includes procedures for obtaining evidence of compliance and maintaining oversight of third-party relationships.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Verify the PCI DSS compliance of all third-party service providers. Obtain evidence of compliance and maintain oversight of third-party relationships. WHO: Compliance and vendor management teams are responsible for verifying compliance. WHEN: Compliance verification must occur annually or whenever a new service provider is engaged. HOW: Request Attestation of Compliance from service providers and review contracts for compliance responsibilities. WHERE: This applies to all third-party service providers with access to cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Verify the PCI DSS compliance of all third-party service providers annually.
compliances.Scope: All third-party service providers with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure ongoing compliance of third-party service providers with PCI DSS requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches, financial penalties, reputational damage

mitigation.1: Conduct regular audits of third-party compliance
mitigation.2: Implement strong contractual obligations for compliance
mitigation.3: Provide regular training on compliance requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliant third-party providers leading to data breaches or regulatory fines
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units with cardholder data exposure

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Verify the PCI DSS compliance of new third-party service providers upon engagement.
compliances.Scope: All new third-party service providers with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure immediate compliance of new third-party service providers with PCI DSS requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Immediate data breaches, financial penalties, reputational damage

mitigation.1: Conduct thorough due diligence before engaging new providers
mitigation.2: Implement compliance checks in vendor onboarding process
mitigation.3: Require immediate attestation of compliance from new providers

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Engaging non-compliant new providers leading to immediate data breaches or regulatory fines
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units with new provider exposure

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain ongoing oversight of all third-party relationships with access to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All third-party service providers with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure continuous monitoring and management of third-party relationships to mitigate compliance risks
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of oversight leading to compliance breaches, data breaches, financial penalties

mitigation.1: Establish clear oversight processes and responsibilities
mitigation.2: Regularly review third-party performance against compliance requirements
mitigation.3: Implement escalation procedures for non-compliance incidents

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of oversight leading to compliance breaches and subsequent data breaches or financial penalties
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units with third-party relationships

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for using the Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) as a validation tool for PCI DSS compliance. It emphasizes the importance of accurately completing the SAQ and retaining documentation for compliance purposes. The policy aims to ensure that organizations effectively assess their compliance status and take necessary remediation actions.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all merchants and service providers that are required to assess their PCI DSS compliance using the SAQ. It covers all organizational units involved in compliance reporting and assessment. The policy is applicable to all geographic locations where the organization operates.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that organizations accurately assess their PCI DSS compliance status using the SAQ and take necessary remediation actions. Specific goals include maintaining accurate documentation of the SAQ and ensuring timely remediation of any compliance gaps.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for completing the Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) and retaining documentation for compliance purposes. It includes procedures for addressing 'no' answers and documenting remediation plans.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Complete the SAQ accurately and retain documentation for compliance. Address any 'no' answers with a remediation plan. WHO: Compliance teams are responsible for completing the SAQ and documenting remediation plans. WHEN: The SAQ must be completed annually or whenever there are changes in the environment. HOW: Use the SAQ Instructions and Guidelines document to ensure accurate completion. Documentation must be stored securely for future reference. WHERE: This applies to all merchants and service providers assessing their PCI DSS compliance.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All merchants and service providers must complete the SAQ annually as per PCI DSS requirements.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to all merchants and service providers assessing their PCI DSS compliance.
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely completion of the SAQ to maintain compliance with PCI DSS standards.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may result in fines, reputational damage, and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Ensure timely completion of the SAQ by setting reminders and deadlines
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update SAQ completion processes
mitigation.3: Provide training to compliance teams on SAQ completion requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to complete the SAQ annually may lead to non-compliance with PCI DSS standards and potential data breaches.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Non-compliance may impact the entire organization's reputation and financial stability.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Compliance teams must retain documentation related to SAQ completion and remediation plans securely for future reference.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to compliance teams responsible for completing the SAQ and documenting remediation plans.
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all documentation related to SAQ completion and remediation plans is securely stored for compliance purposes.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Loss of documentation may result in inability to prove compliance, leading to fines and penalties.

mitigation.1: Implement secure document storage procedures
mitigation.2: Regularly backup documentation to prevent loss
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits of documentation storage

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Loss of documentation may result in inability to prove compliance during audits, leading to fines and penalties.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Loss of documentation may impact compliance status and result in financial penalties.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Compliance teams must document remediation plans for addressing 'no' answers in the SAQ.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to compliance teams responsible for addressing 'no' answers in the SAQ.
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all 'no' answers in the SAQ are addressed with documented remediation plans for compliance purposes.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to document remediation plans may result in unresolved compliance issues and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Establish clear guidelines for documenting remediation plans
mitigation.2: Review and approve all remediation plans before implementation
mitigation.3: Monitor progress on remediation plans regularly

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to document remediation plans may result in unresolved compliance issues, leading to potential data breaches.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Unresolved compliance issues may impact the organization's security posture and reputation.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for maintaining compliance with the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS). It emphasizes the importance of assessing, remediating, and reporting on vulnerabilities related to cardholder data. The policy ensures that all entities involved in payment card processing adhere to PCI DSS requirements to protect sensitive information.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including IT, security, compliance, and operations personnel. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that handle cardholder data, including on-premises, cloud, and third-party services. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all entities maintain compliance with PCI DSS requirements, effectively manage risks associated with cardholder data, and continuously monitor and improve security controls. Specific goals include reducing vulnerabilities, ensuring timely remediation, and maintaining a secure environment for cardholder data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting regular vulnerability assessments and remediating identified vulnerabilities that could lead to unauthorized access to cardholder data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct vulnerability assessments on all systems handling cardholder data at least quarterly. Remediate identified vulnerabilities within 30 days of discovery. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for conducting assessments and remediation. WHEN: Assessments must be performed quarterly, and remediation must occur within 30 days of vulnerability identification. HOW: Utilize automated scanning tools to identify vulnerabilities and track remediation efforts through a ticketing system. WHERE: This applies to all systems within the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) and any systems that interact with it.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct vulnerability assessments on all systems handling cardholder data at least quarterly.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) and any systems that interact with it
compliances.Objective: To identify vulnerabilities in a timely manner to prevent unauthorized access to cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement regular vulnerability scanning schedules
mitigation.2: Prioritize and remediate critical vulnerabilities first
mitigation.3: Implement network segmentation to limit access to cardholder data

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, financial penalties, loss of customer trust

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Remediate identified vulnerabilities within 30 days of discovery.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) and any systems that interact with it
compliances.Objective: To promptly address and eliminate vulnerabilities to prevent security incidents
compliances.PossibleDamage: Exploitation of vulnerabilities leading to unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Establish a clear remediation process with defined timelines
mitigation.2: Implement automated patch management systems
mitigation.3: Regularly review and update security policies and procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, financial losses, reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize automated scanning tools to identify vulnerabilities.
compliances.Scope: All systems within the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) and any systems that interact with it
compliances.Objective: To efficiently and accurately detect vulnerabilities in systems handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Missed vulnerabilities that could lead to unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Regularly update scanning tools and databases
mitigation.2: Implement continuous monitoring for real-time vulnerability detection
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic reviews of scanning results for accuracy

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, regulatory fines, loss of customer trust

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for reporting compliance status and evidence of data protection controls to relevant stakeholders.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Prepare and submit an annual Report on Compliance (RoC) that includes contact information, executive summary, scope of work, reviewed environment details, quarterly scan results, and findings. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for compiling and submitting the RoC. WHEN: The RoC must be submitted annually, with quarterly updates on scan results. HOW: Use the PCI SSC template for the RoC and ensure all required documentation is included. WHERE: This applies to all entities that process, store, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Prepare and submit an annual Report on Compliance (RoC) using the PCI SSC template with contact information, executive summary, scope of work, reviewed environment details, quarterly scan results, and findings.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to all entities that process, store, or transmit cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To provide stakeholders with a detailed overview of compliance status and data protection controls
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to submit the RoC may result in non-compliance penalties and reputational damage

mitigation.1: Ensure timely submission of the RoC
mitigation.2: Regularly update quarterly scan results
mitigation.3: Conduct internal audits to verify compliance

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance fines, loss of customer trust, legal actions
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units involved in cardholder data processing

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Provide quarterly updates on scan results as part of the RoC submission
compliances.Scope: All entities processing, storing, or transmitting cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure ongoing monitoring of data protection controls and compliance status
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated or inaccurate scan results may lead to undetected vulnerabilities and non-compliance

mitigation.1: Regularly schedule and conduct scans
mitigation.2: Implement automated scanning tools
mitigation.3: Establish clear reporting procedures for scan results

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, non-compliance penalties
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units involved in cardholder data processing

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure the RoC includes a comprehensive executive summary outlining key compliance status and findings
compliances.Scope: All entities processing, storing, or transmitting cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To provide stakeholders with a concise overview of compliance status and key findings
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of executive summary may lead to misinterpretation of compliance status and findings

mitigation.1: Develop standardized executive summary templates
mitigation.2: Review and approve executive summaries before submission
mitigation.3: Provide executive summary training to relevant staff

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Miscommunication, misinterpretation of compliance status
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units involved in cardholder data processing

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines how PCI DSS compliance should be integrated into daily business operations to ensure ongoing adherence to security standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement processes to monitor security controls, review changes to the environment, and conduct periodic reviews of PCI DSS compliance. WHO: The Security Operations Team is responsible for monitoring and reviewing compliance. WHEN: Continuous monitoring should occur daily, with formal reviews conducted quarterly. HOW: Establish a compliance calendar and utilize monitoring tools to track compliance status. WHERE: This applies to all operational environments, including on-premises and cloud-based systems.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Establish processes to monitor security controls on a daily basis to ensure ongoing adherence to PCI DSS compliance.
compliances.Scope: All operational environments, including on-premises and cloud-based systems
compliances.Objective: To ensure that security controls are continuously monitored to maintain PCI DSS compliance
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of security breaches and non-compliance penalties

mitigation.1: Implement automated monitoring tools
mitigation.2: Regularly review monitoring reports
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to monitor security controls could lead to undetected vulnerabilities and non-compliance.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Security Operations and overall organizational security

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Quarterly Formal Reviews of PCI DSS Compliance
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct formal reviews of PCI DSS compliance on a quarterly basis to ensure adherence to security standards.
compliances.Scope: All operational environments, including on-premises and cloud-based systems
compliances.Objective: To verify ongoing compliance with PCI DSS standards through structured reviews
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to identify non-compliance issues and address security gaps

mitigation.1: Establish formal review procedures
mitigation.2: Document review findings and action items
mitigation.3: Implement corrective actions promptly

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of formal reviews may result in unidentified compliance issues and security vulnerabilities.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Security Operations and overall organizational security

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Create a compliance calendar to track and manage PCI DSS compliance activities.
compliances.Scope: All operational environments, including on-premises and cloud-based systems
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely and organized execution of compliance tasks
compliances.PossibleDamage: Missed compliance deadlines and lack of visibility into compliance activities

mitigation.1: Develop a detailed compliance calendar
mitigation.2: Assign responsibilities for compliance tasks
mitigation.3: Regularly update and review the compliance calendar

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of a compliance calendar may lead to missed deadlines and incomplete compliance tasks.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Security Operations and overall organizational security

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the guidelines for accessing and utilizing web resources related to the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI DSS). It is essential for ensuring that all personnel involved in payment card processing are informed and compliant with the latest security standards and practices. The policy aims to promote awareness and provide access to necessary training and resources to maintain a secure environment.
policies.PolicyName: Web Resources Access and Utilization Policy
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers involved in payment card processing within the organization. It covers all systems and applications used for accessing PCI DSS-related web resources, including on-premises and remote access environments. The policy also encompasses all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes relationships with third-party vendors and service providers.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all personnel have access to the necessary web resources to understand and comply with PCI DSS requirements. It aims to enhance security awareness, promote compliance with PCI standards, and mitigate risks associated with payment card processing. The policy also seeks to ensure that all personnel complete required training and utilize approved resources effectively.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Access to PCI Security Standards Council Resources
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for accessing PCI Security Standards Council resources, including training materials, FAQs, and approved products. It ensures that personnel are aware of the resources available to them and understand how to utilize them effectively.
subpolicies.Control: Personnel must access the PCI Security Standards Council website (https://www.pcisecuritystandards.org) to review the latest PCI DSS documentation and training materials. Access should be conducted by all employees involved in payment card processing at least quarterly. Training completion must be documented and reported to the compliance officer. Resources accessed must include FAQs, webinars, and training programs relevant to their roles.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Quarterly Review of PCI DSS Documentation
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All personnel involved in payment card processing must access the PCI Security Standards Council website quarterly to review the latest PCI DSS documentation.
compliances.Scope: All employees involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: Ensure personnel are up-to-date with the latest PCI DSS documentation
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to outdated knowledge of PCI DSS requirements and potential security vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Regular training sessions on PCI DSS updates
mitigation.2: Internal audits to verify compliance
mitigation.3: Documentation of training completion

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated knowledge leading to non-compliance with PCI DSS requirements

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Training completion must be documented and reported to the compliance officer.
compliances.Scope: All employees involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: Ensure training completion is tracked and reported for compliance monitoring
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of documentation may lead to non-compliance with internal training requirements and potential gaps in knowledge.

mitigation.1: Automated tracking system for training completion
mitigation.2: Regular reporting to compliance officer
mitigation.3: Remedial training for non-compliant employees

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance with internal training requirements and gaps in knowledge

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Access to FAQs, Webinars, and Training Programs
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Personnel must access FAQs, webinars, and training programs relevant to their roles.
compliances.Scope: All employees involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: Ensure personnel have access to resources relevant to their roles for continuous learning
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of access to relevant resources may result in outdated knowledge and inefficient processes.

mitigation.1: Regular communication on available resources
mitigation.2: Training needs assessment for resource relevance
mitigation.3: Feedback mechanism for resource improvement

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated knowledge and inefficiencies due to lack of access to relevant resources

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the training and certification requirements for personnel involved in payment card processing. It ensures that all relevant staff complete necessary training programs to maintain compliance with PCI DSS.
subpolicies.Control: All personnel must complete PCI awareness training within 30 days of employment and annually thereafter. The training must cover PCI DSS requirements, security best practices, and the use of approved resources. Compliance with training requirements will be monitored by the HR department, and records will be maintained for audit purposes.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All personnel must complete PCI awareness training within 30 days of employment and annually thereafter.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all personnel involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: Ensure all relevant staff complete necessary training programs to maintain compliance with PCI DSS
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to complete training may lead to non-compliance with PCI DSS, potential data breaches, and financial penalties.

mitigation.1: Regular reminders and notifications to staff about training deadlines
mitigation.2: Provide additional support and resources for training completion
mitigation.3: Implement consequences for non-compliance with training requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance with training requirements leading to data breaches, financial losses, and reputational damage
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units involved in payment card processing

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The training must cover PCI DSS requirements, security best practices, and the use of approved resources.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all personnel involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: Ensure staff are knowledgeable about PCI DSS requirements, security best practices, and approved resources
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inadequate training content may result in staff not following security protocols, leading to data breaches and non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update training content to align with current PCI DSS requirements
mitigation.2: Provide additional resources and materials for staff to reference
mitigation.3: Conduct assessments to ensure staff comprehension of training content

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate training content leading to staff not following security protocols and potential data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units involved in payment card processing

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Compliance with training requirements will be monitored by the HR department, and records will be maintained for audit purposes.
compliances.Scope: Applies to HR department and all personnel involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: Ensure consistent monitoring of staff training compliance and maintain accurate records for audit purposes
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: HR department and all business units involved in payment card processing
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of monitoring and record-keeping may result in non-compliance being overlooked and potential audit failures.

mitigation.1: Implement automated tracking systems for training completion
mitigation.2: Regularly review training records for accuracy and completeness
mitigation.3: Provide training to HR staff on monitoring and record-keeping requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of monitoring leading to non-compliance with training requirements and audit failures
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: HR department and all business units involved in payment card processing

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for utilizing PCI SSC approved products, solutions, and providers. It ensures that all personnel are aware of and use only approved solutions for payment card processing.
subpolicies.Control: Personnel must utilize only PCI SSC approved products and solutions as listed on the PCI Security Standards Council website. This includes PIN Transaction Security (PTS) devices, payment applications, and P2PE solutions. Compliance will be reviewed during annual audits, and any non-compliance must be reported to the compliance officer immediately.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Utilization of PCI SSC Approved Products
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All personnel must use only PCI SSC approved products and solutions for payment card processing as listed on the PCI Security Standards Council website.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all employees involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To ensure that only approved solutions are used to maintain security and compliance standards
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance could result in data breaches, financial losses, and reputational damage

mitigation.1: Regular training on approved products
mitigation.2: Strict monitoring of product usage
mitigation.3: Immediate reporting of non-compliance

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized use of non-approved products leading to data breaches and non-compliance penalties

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Compliance with the utilization of approved solutions will be reviewed during annual audits.
compliances.Scope: All departments involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To ensure ongoing adherence to the subpolicy requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to pass the audit could result in compliance violations and penalties

mitigation.1: Regular internal audits throughout the year
mitigation.2: Proactive remediation of any non-compliance issues
mitigation.3: Continuous monitoring of compliance status

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of compliance during audit leading to penalties and reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Any non-compliance with the utilization of approved solutions must be reported to the compliance officer immediately.
compliances.Scope: All employees involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To address non-compliance issues promptly and take corrective actions
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delayed reporting could lead to prolonged non-compliance and increased risk exposure

mitigation.1: Clear reporting procedures and channels
mitigation.2: Regular training on reporting requirements
mitigation.3: Escalation process for unresolved non-compliance issues

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undisclosed non-compliance leading to increased risk exposure and potential breaches

versions.PolicyName: Web Resources Access and Utilization Policy

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for building and maintaining a secure network and systems to protect cardholder data. It establishes the necessary controls to ensure that firewalls are configured correctly and that vendor-supplied defaults are not used. This policy is essential for safeguarding sensitive payment card information from unauthorized access.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units that handle cardholder data, including IT departments, security personnel, and third-party service providers. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure involved in payment card processing, including on-premises and cloud environments.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a secure network environment that protects cardholder data from unauthorized access and breaches. Specific goals include ensuring that firewalls are properly configured, vendor defaults are eliminated, and compliance with PCI DSS requirements is maintained. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with network vulnerabilities and enhance overall security posture.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for configuring and maintaining firewalls to protect cardholder data. It includes guidelines for firewall rule sets, access controls, and regular reviews.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement and maintain a firewall configuration that restricts access to cardholder data. WHO: Network security team is responsible for configuration and maintenance. WHEN: Firewalls must be reviewed and updated quarterly or whenever there are changes to the network. HOW: Use automated tools to monitor firewall configurations and ensure compliance with established rules. WHERE: Applies to all network segments that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Firewalls must be reviewed and updated quarterly to ensure compliance with established rules.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all network segments that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To maintain an up-to-date firewall configuration that restricts access to cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches.

mitigation.1: Perform regular firewall configuration audits
mitigation.2: Implement automated tools for continuous monitoring
mitigation.3: Train network security team on best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, loss of customer trust, regulatory fines.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain detailed documentation of firewall rule sets and access controls.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all network segments that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure transparency and accountability in firewall configuration management.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Misconfiguration leading to unauthorized access or network vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Regularly update firewall documentation
mitigation.2: Implement access control policies
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic reviews of documentation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, network downtime, compliance violations.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regularly review and update access controls to restrict unauthorized access to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all network segments that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access and ensure data security.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, loss of customer trust, regulatory fines.

mitigation.1: Implement role-based access controls
mitigation.2: Regularly review access permissions
mitigation.3: Monitor access logs for suspicious activity

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, loss of customer trust, regulatory fines.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for managing vendor-supplied defaults for system passwords and other security parameters. It ensures that all default settings are changed to enhance security.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Change all vendor-supplied defaults for system passwords and other security parameters before installing a system on the network. WHO: IT department is responsible for changing defaults. WHEN: This must be done prior to system installation and reviewed annually. HOW: Maintain a checklist of default settings and document changes made. WHERE: Applies to all systems that are part of the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All vendor-supplied default passwords must be changed before installing a system on the network.
compliances.Scope: IT department
compliances.Objective: Enhance security by preventing unauthorized access through default passwords.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to systems, data breaches, and potential loss of sensitive information.

mitigation.1: Implement strong password policies
mitigation.2: Enforce regular password changes
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Default passwords not changed leading to unauthorized access and data breaches.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain a checklist of default settings and document all changes made to enhance security.
compliances.Scope: IT department
compliances.Objective: Ensure transparency and accountability in managing default settings.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of documentation leading to confusion, errors, and potential security vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Implement documentation procedures
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update documentation
mitigation.3: Train staff on documentation best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of documentation leading to errors in default settings management.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review all default settings annually to ensure ongoing security enhancements.
compliances.Scope: IT department
compliances.Objective: Maintain up-to-date security measures by reviewing default settings regularly.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated default settings leading to security vulnerabilities and potential breaches.

mitigation.1: Schedule annual reviews
mitigation.2: Implement change management process for default settings
mitigation.3: Automate review processes where possible

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to review default settings leading to outdated security measures.

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for protecting stored cardholder data and ensuring secure transmission of this data across networks. It is critical for maintaining the confidentiality and integrity of payment card information.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all personnel who handle cardholder data, including employees, contractors, and third-party service providers. It encompasses all systems and applications that store or transmit cardholder data, regardless of the environment (on-premises or cloud).
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all cardholder data is adequately protected against unauthorized access and breaches. Specific goals include implementing strong encryption for stored and transmitted data, conducting regular audits, and ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with data exposure and enhance data security.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for encrypting stored cardholder data to protect it from unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All stored cardholder data must be encrypted using industry-standard encryption algorithms. WHO: Data security team is responsible for implementing encryption measures. WHEN: Encryption must be applied at the time of data storage and reviewed annually. HOW: Use encryption tools that comply with PCI DSS standards and maintain documentation of encryption methods used. WHERE: Applies to all databases and storage systems that contain cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All stored cardholder data must be encrypted using industry-standard encryption algorithms.
compliances.Scope: Data security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that cardholder data is protected from unauthorized access.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Regularly update encryption algorithms to industry standards
mitigation.2: Implement access controls to limit data exposure
mitigation.3: Monitor encryption implementation for vulnerabilities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches resulting in financial losses and reputational damage.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Data security team is responsible for implementing encryption measures.
compliances.Scope: Data security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that encryption measures are implemented effectively and consistently.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inconsistent or ineffective encryption implementation leading to data breaches.

mitigation.1: Provide regular training to the data security team on encryption best practices
mitigation.2: Implement encryption monitoring tools for real-time detection of vulnerabilities
mitigation.3: Conduct regular audits of encryption implementation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches due to inconsistent or ineffective encryption implementation.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Encryption must be applied at the time of data storage and reviewed annually.
compliances.Scope: Data security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that encryption measures are regularly reviewed and updated.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated encryption methods leading to vulnerabilities and data breaches.

mitigation.1: Schedule regular encryption reviews and updates
mitigation.2: Implement automated encryption review tools
mitigation.3: Conduct penetration testing to identify encryption weaknesses

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches due to outdated encryption methods

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for encrypting cardholder data during transmission across open or public networks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All cardholder data transmitted across open or public networks must be encrypted. WHO: Network operations team is responsible for ensuring encryption is in place. WHEN: Encryption must be implemented before any data transmission occurs and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Utilize secure protocols such as TLS or VPNs for data transmission. WHERE: Applies to all network segments that transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All cardholder data transmitted across open or public networks must be encrypted using secure protocols such as TLS or VPNs.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all network segments that transmit cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure the secure transmission of cardholder data across open or public networks
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches leading to unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update encryption protocols
mitigation.2: Implement multi-factor authentication for data access
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security training for network operations team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data during transmission
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Network operations and reputation of the organization

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Encryption protocols for cardholder data transmission must be reviewed quarterly to ensure compliance and effectiveness.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network operations team responsible for encryption
compliances.Objective: To maintain the security and integrity of encryption protocols for cardholder data transmission
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated or ineffective encryption protocols leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Regularly update encryption protocols based on industry standards
mitigation.2: Conduct penetration testing to identify vulnerabilities
mitigation.3: Implement encryption key management procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches due to outdated encryption protocols

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize secure protocols such as TLS or VPNs for data transmission of cardholder data across open or public networks.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all network segments transmitting cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure the confidentiality and integrity of cardholder data during transmission
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data interception leading to unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Implement multi-factor authentication for network access
mitigation.2: Regularly update encryption protocols to address vulnerabilities
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security training for network operations team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data interception and unauthorized access to cardholder data

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for maintaining a vulnerability management program to protect systems against malware and ensure secure applications. It is essential for identifying and mitigating vulnerabilities that could compromise cardholder data.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data, including servers, workstations, and applications. It covers all personnel involved in system maintenance and development, including IT staff and third-party vendors.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a comprehensive vulnerability management program that protects systems from malware and ensures the security of applications. Specific goals include regular updates of anti-virus software, secure application development practices, and timely remediation of identified vulnerabilities. The policy aims to reduce the risk of malware infections and enhance overall system security.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for protecting systems against malware and ensuring that anti-virus software is regularly updated.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All systems must have anti-virus software installed and regularly updated. WHO: IT security team is responsible for managing anti-virus solutions. WHEN: Anti-virus software must be updated at least weekly and monitored continuously. HOW: Use automated tools to ensure updates are applied and maintain logs of updates. WHERE: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for developing and maintaining secure systems and applications to prevent vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All applications must be developed following secure coding practices. WHO: Development teams are responsible for adhering to secure coding standards. WHEN: Security reviews must be conducted at each stage of the development lifecycle. HOW: Implement code review processes and use automated security testing tools. WHERE: Applies to all applications that interact with cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All applications must be developed following secure coding practices.
compliances.Scope: Development teams
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all applications are developed securely to prevent vulnerabilities.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches and unauthorized access to sensitive information.

mitigation.1: Regular security training for development teams
mitigation.2: Implementing secure coding guidelines and best practices
mitigation.3: Conducting regular code reviews

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, financial losses.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Security Reviews at Each Development Stage
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Security reviews must be conducted at each stage of the development lifecycle.
compliances.Scope: Development teams
compliances.Objective: To ensure that security is integrated into every stage of the development process.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Undetected vulnerabilities and security weaknesses in applications.

mitigation.1: Implementing security checkpoints at each development stage
mitigation.2: Regular security audits and reviews
mitigation.3: Continuous monitoring of application security

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security vulnerabilities in applications, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Use automated security testing tools in the development process.
compliances.Scope: Development teams
compliances.Objective: To enhance the security posture of applications through automated testing.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unidentified security vulnerabilities and weaknesses in applications.

mitigation.1: Integrating automated security testing tools in the CI/CD pipeline
mitigation.2: Regularly updating security testing tools
mitigation.3: Training development teams on tool usage

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security vulnerabilities in applications, data breaches

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for implementing strong access control measures to restrict access to cardholder data. It is vital for ensuring that only authorized personnel have access to sensitive information.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all personnel who have access to cardholder data, including employees, contractors, and third-party service providers. It covers all systems and locations where cardholder data is stored or processed.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that access to cardholder data is strictly controlled and monitored. Specific goals include limiting access based on business needs, ensuring proper identification and authentication, and conducting regular access reviews. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with unauthorized access and enhance data security.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for restricting access to cardholder data based on business need to know.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Access to cardholder data must be restricted to authorized personnel only. WHO: Access control team is responsible for managing access permissions. WHEN: Access permissions must be reviewed quarterly and updated as necessary. HOW: Implement role-based access controls and maintain an access control list. WHERE: Applies to all systems and locations where cardholder data is stored or processed.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement role-based access controls to restrict access to cardholder data based on business need to know.
compliances.Scope: All systems and locations where cardholder data is stored or processed
compliances.Objective: Ensure that only authorized personnel have access to cardholder data
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update access permissions
mitigation.2: Provide training on role-based access controls
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, financial penalties
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review access permissions quarterly and update as necessary to ensure access to cardholder data is restricted to authorized personnel only.
compliances.Scope: All systems and locations where cardholder data is stored or processed
compliances.Objective: Regularly review and update access permissions to maintain data security
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated access permissions leading to unauthorized access and data breaches

mitigation.1: Automate access permission reviews
mitigation.2: Implement access permission change management process
mitigation.3: Conduct regular access permission audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated access permissions, unauthorized access, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain an access control list to manage access permissions for cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and locations where cardholder data is stored or processed
compliances.Objective: Ensure accurate and up-to-date access control list to prevent unauthorized access
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate access control list leading to unauthorized access and data breaches

mitigation.1: Regularly update access control list
mitigation.2: Implement access control list change management process
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic access control list audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inaccurate access control list, unauthorized access, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for identifying and authenticating access to system components.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All users must be uniquely identified and authenticated before accessing system components. WHO: IT security team is responsible for implementing authentication measures. WHEN: Authentication must occur at the time of access and reviewed annually. HOW: Use multi-factor authentication methods where applicable. WHERE: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All users accessing system components must use multi-factor authentication methods where applicable.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To enhance security by requiring additional authentication factors for user access.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement biometric authentication for added security
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update authentication methods
mitigation.3: Provide training on proper use of multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access leading to data breaches, financial losses, and reputational damage.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Systems

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Authentication must occur at the time of access and be reviewed annually.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure ongoing security by regularly reviewing and updating authentication measures.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated authentication methods, increased risk of unauthorized access.

mitigation.1: Conduct regular audits of authentication logs
mitigation.2: Implement automated alerts for suspicious authentication activities
mitigation.3: Enforce regular password changes

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated authentication methods leading to increased risk of unauthorized access and data breaches.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Systems

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All users must be uniquely identified before accessing system components.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure accountability and traceability of user actions within the system.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to unauthorized access and data breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement user account management policies
mitigation.2: Enforce strong password requirements
mitigation.3: Regularly review user access permissions

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access due to lack of unique user identification, data breaches.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Cardholder Data Systems

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for regularly monitoring and testing networks to track access to network resources and cardholder data. It is essential for identifying potential security incidents and ensuring compliance with security standards.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all systems and personnel involved in monitoring and testing network security. It covers all network segments that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all access to cardholder data is monitored and that security systems are regularly tested for effectiveness. Specific goals include tracking access to sensitive data, conducting regular security tests, and ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy aims to enhance incident detection and response capabilities.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for tracking and monitoring all access to network resources and cardholder data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All access to network resources and cardholder data must be logged and monitored. WHO: Security operations team is responsible for access tracking. WHEN: Access logs must be reviewed daily and retained for at least one year. HOW: Use logging tools to capture access events and generate reports. WHERE: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Access logs must be reviewed daily by the security operations team.
compliances.Scope: All systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely monitoring of access to network resources and cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access or data breaches due to delayed monitoring

mitigation.1: Automate access log review process
mitigation.2: Implement alerts for suspicious access events
mitigation.3: Provide regular training to security operations team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, unauthorized access, compliance violations
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Security Operations Team, Cardholder Data Storage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Access logs must be retained for at least one year.
compliances.Scope: All systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure compliance with data retention regulations and facilitate historical access tracking
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance with data retention regulations, inability to track historical access

mitigation.1: Implement automated data retention policies
mitigation.2: Regularly audit access log retention practices
mitigation.3: Backup access logs to secure storage

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance penalties, inability to investigate historical access incidents

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Use logging tools to capture access events and generate reports.
compliances.Scope: All systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Facilitate accurate and efficient tracking of access to network resources and cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate access tracking, inability to generate access reports

mitigation.1: Regularly update logging tools
mitigation.2: Implement access event correlation mechanisms
mitigation.3: Provide training on logging tool usage

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, inaccurate access tracking, compliance violations

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for regularly testing security systems and processes to identify vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Security systems and processes must be tested regularly to identify vulnerabilities. WHO: IT security team is responsible for conducting tests. WHEN: Testing must occur at least quarterly and after any significant changes to the network. HOW: Use automated testing tools and conduct manual assessments. WHERE: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All security systems and processes must be tested at least quarterly to identify vulnerabilities.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure regular testing of security systems to identify and mitigate vulnerabilities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to conduct quarterly testing may result in undetected vulnerabilities leading to potential data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement automated testing tools
mitigation.2: Conduct manual assessments in addition to automated tools
mitigation.3: Increase testing frequency if vulnerabilities are detected

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected vulnerabilities leading to data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT security team, Data Protection team

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All security systems must be tested after any significant changes to the network to identify vulnerabilities.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that security systems are tested for vulnerabilities after network changes
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to test after network changes may leave vulnerabilities undetected, increasing the risk of exploitation

mitigation.1: Implement change management process to trigger post-change testing
mitigation.2: Conduct thorough testing after any network changes
mitigation.3: Review testing results with network changes team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Exploitation of vulnerabilities post-network changes
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT security team, Network Operations team

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: In addition to automated testing, manual security assessments must be conducted to identify vulnerabilities.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure comprehensive identification of vulnerabilities through manual assessments
compliances.PossibleDamage: Relying solely on automated tools may miss certain vulnerabilities that require manual assessment

mitigation.1: Train IT security team on manual assessment techniques
mitigation.2: Implement periodic manual assessment reviews
mitigation.3: Combine automated and manual testing results for comprehensive analysis

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected vulnerabilities due to lack of manual assessments
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT security team, Data Protection team

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for maintaining an information security policy that addresses security for all personnel. It is essential for fostering a culture of security awareness and compliance within the organization.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and third-party service providers who have access to cardholder data. It encompasses all organizational units and locations where cardholder data is processed or stored.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a comprehensive information security framework that promotes security awareness and compliance among all personnel. Specific goals include regular training programs, effective communication of security policies, and fostering a culture of security within the organization. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with human error and enhance overall security posture.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting regular security awareness training for all personnel.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: All personnel must undergo security awareness training. WHO: HR and security teams are responsible for training implementation. WHEN: Training must be conducted annually and upon onboarding of new employees. HOW: Use a combination of online training modules and in-person sessions. WHERE: Applies to all employees and contractors with access to cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All personnel must undergo security awareness training annually.
compliances.Scope: All employees and contractors with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure all personnel receive regular security awareness training to mitigate security risks.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased vulnerability to security breaches and data theft

mitigation.1: Regularly scheduled training sessions
mitigation.2: Tracking and monitoring training completion
mitigation.3: Implementing consequences for non-compliance

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by potential security breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All new employees must undergo security awareness training upon onboarding.
compliances.Scope: New employees with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure new employees are aware of security protocols and best practices from the start.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of security incidents due to lack of initial training

mitigation.1: Incorporate security training into onboarding process
mitigation.2: Provide resources for ongoing learning
mitigation.3: Regularly assess new employees' understanding of security practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: New employees unknowingly engaging in risky behavior, data breaches, compliance violations
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: HR and Security departments would be impacted by potential security incidents

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: HR and security teams are responsible for implementing security awareness training.
compliances.Scope: HR and Security departments
compliances.Objective: Ensure designated teams are accountable for training delivery and monitoring.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inconsistent or inadequate training delivery

mitigation.1: Establish clear roles and responsibilities
mitigation.2: Provide training for trainers
mitigation.3: Regularly review training effectiveness

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate training leading to security incidents, lack of accountability
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: HR and Security departments would be impacted by inadequate training delivery

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for communicating the information security policy to all personnel.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: The information security policy must be communicated to all personnel. WHO: Security team is responsible for policy communication. WHEN: Policy must be communicated at least annually and whenever there are updates. HOW: Use email notifications and internal communication platforms to disseminate the policy. WHERE: Applies to all organizational units that handle cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The information security policy must be communicated to all personnel at least annually.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure all personnel are aware of the information security policy on a regular basis
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of awareness leading to policy violations and security breaches

mitigation.1: Schedule regular policy communication sessions
mitigation.2: Provide refresher training on policy content
mitigation.3: Send reminders via email notifications

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Personnel unaware of policy updates leading to non-compliance
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The information security policy must be communicated to all personnel whenever there are updates.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: Ensure all personnel are informed of policy updates in a timely manner
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated policy knowledge leading to non-compliance

mitigation.1: Implement a process for immediate policy update dissemination
mitigation.2: Require acknowledgment of policy updates from all personnel
mitigation.3: Conduct regular audits to ensure policy awareness

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Personnel unaware of critical policy updates leading to non-compliance
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The security team is responsible for communicating the information security policy.
compliances.Scope: Security team
compliances.Objective: Ensure a designated team is accountable for policy communication
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of clear responsibility leading to communication gaps

mitigation.1: Define clear roles and responsibilities within the security team
mitigation.2: Provide training on effective policy communication
mitigation.3: Establish communication protocols for policy dissemination

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of designated responsibility leading to policy communication gaps

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for accurately determining the scope of the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) as mandated by PCI DSS.
