It is essential for organizations to identify all system components that handle cardholder data to ensure compliance and security. The policy emphasizes the importance of regular scoping and documentation to maintain a secure environment.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units and personnel involved in payment card processing, including IT, compliance, and security teams. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that handle cardholder data, including on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. Third-party service providers with access to cardholder data are also included in this scope.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all components of the Cardholder Data Environment are accurately identified and documented to maintain compliance with PCI DSS. This includes reducing risk by ensuring that all cardholder data is properly managed and that any out-of-scope data is handled appropriately. The policy aims to achieve measurable outcomes in compliance and risk mitigation.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the steps required to conduct the annual scoping process for the Cardholder Data Environment, ensuring all components are identified and documented.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct an annual review to identify all system components within the CDE. WHO: The IT Security team is responsible for conducting the review. WHEN: This review must be completed annually and prior to the PCI DSS assessment. HOW: Utilize network diagrams and inventory lists to document all components. WHERE: This process applies to all locations where cardholder data is processed or stored.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct an annual review to identify all system components within the Cardholder Data Environment using network diagrams and inventory lists.
compliances.Scope: IT Security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure all components within the CDE are identified and documented annually.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to conduct the annual review may result in unidentified components within the CDE, leading to potential security breaches.

mitigation.1: Regular training for IT Security team on identifying system components
mitigation.2: Implement automated scanning tools for continuous monitoring
mitigation.3: Engage third-party auditors for independent verification

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, non-compliance with PCI DSS standards

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for retaining documentation related to the scoping process for future assessments.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Retain documentation that verifies the scoping process and cardholder data locations. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for ensuring documentation is retained. WHEN: Documentation must be retained for a minimum of three years or until the next annual assessment. HOW: Store documentation in a secure, accessible location for assessor review. WHERE: This applies to all departments involved in PCI DSS compliance.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All departments involved in PCI DSS compliance must retain documentation that verifies the scoping process and cardholder data locations for a minimum of three years or until the next annual assessment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all departments involved in PCI DSS compliance
compliances.Objective: To ensure that documentation related to the scoping process is retained for future assessments
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All departments involved in PCI DSS compliance
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to retain documentation may lead to inaccurate scoping in future assessments and non-compliance with PCI DSS requirements.

mitigation.1: Regular audits to ensure documentation retention compliance
mitigation.2: Training for staff on the importance of retaining documentation
mitigation.3: Implementing automated documentation retention systems

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inaccurate scoping leading to potential data breaches or non-compliance fines
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All departments involved in PCI DSS compliance would be impacted by inaccurate scoping and potential non-compliance fines

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Compliance Officer Responsibility for Documentation Retention
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The Compliance Officer is responsible for ensuring that documentation related to the scoping process is retained for a minimum of three years or until the next annual assessment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the Compliance Officer
compliances.Objective: To assign responsibility for the retention of documentation related to the scoping process
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to assign responsibility may result in documentation not being retained as required, leading to non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Clearly define the Compliance Officer's responsibilities in the documentation retention policy
mitigation.2: Provide training to the Compliance Officer on documentation retention requirements
mitigation.3: Regularly monitor the Compliance Officer's compliance with documentation retention policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Documentation not being retained as required, leading to non-compliance

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Documentation related to the scoping process must be stored in a secure, accessible location for assessor review.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all departments involved in PCI DSS compliance
compliances.Objective: To ensure that documentation is securely stored and accessible for assessor review
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All departments involved in PCI DSS compliance
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to securely store documentation may lead to unauthorized access or tampering, compromising the integrity of the scoping process.

mitigation.1: Implement access controls to restrict unauthorized access to documentation
mitigation.2: Regularly review access logs to detect any unauthorized access attempts
mitigation.3: Encrypt sensitive documentation to protect against tampering

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access or tampering of stored documentation
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compromised integrity of the scoping process

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for network segmentation to isolate the Cardholder Data Environment (CDE) from other network components. Proper segmentation is crucial for reducing the scope of PCI DSS assessments and enhancing security. This policy outlines the necessary measures to ensure that out-of-scope systems do not impact the security of the CDE.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all network components, including routers, switches, and firewalls, that are part of the organization's IT infrastructure. It covers all personnel involved in network management and security, as well as third-party service providers that manage network components connected to the CDE.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to minimize the scope of PCI DSS assessments by ensuring effective network segmentation. This includes reducing risk by preventing unauthorized access to the CDE and ensuring that all network components are properly documented and reviewed. The policy aims to achieve compliance with PCI DSS requirements related to network security.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing network segmentation to isolate the CDE.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement network segmentation to isolate the CDE from other network components. WHO: The Network Security team is responsible for implementing and maintaining segmentation. WHEN: Segmentation must be established prior to any new system deployment and reviewed annually. HOW: Use firewalls and access control lists to enforce segmentation. WHERE: This applies to all network segments that interact with the CDE.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement network segmentation to isolate the CDE from other network components using firewalls and access control lists.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the Network Security team responsible for implementing and maintaining segmentation.
compliances.Objective: To protect the CDE by isolating it from other network components.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to the CDE leading to data breaches and non-compliance penalties.

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update firewall rules
mitigation.2: Implement intrusion detection systems
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to the CDE, data breaches, non-compliance penalties
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Network Security team, Data Protection team

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review network segmentation annually to ensure it effectively isolates the CDE.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the Network Security team responsible for maintaining segmentation.
compliances.Objective: To verify the effectiveness of network segmentation in isolating the CDE.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective segmentation leading to unauthorized access to the CDE.

mitigation.1: Conduct regular penetration testing
mitigation.2: Implement network monitoring tools
mitigation.3: Enforce strict change management procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Ineffective segmentation, unauthorized access to the CDE
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Network Security team, Data Protection team

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Establish segmentation prior to any new system deployment to isolate the CDE.
compliances.Scope: Applies to the Network Security team responsible for implementing segmentation for new systems.
compliances.Objective: To ensure that new systems are deployed with proper isolation of the CDE.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to isolate the CDE in new systems leading to security vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Include segmentation in system deployment checklist
mitigation.2: Conduct security assessments before deployment
mitigation.3: Implement automated configuration management

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security vulnerabilities in new systems, unauthorized access to the CDE
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Network Security team, IT Operations team

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the documentation requirements for network segmentation.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Maintain documentation of the network segmentation architecture and controls. WHO: The IT Security team is responsible for documentation. WHEN: Documentation must be updated whenever changes are made to the network architecture. HOW: Store documentation in a centralized repository accessible to relevant personnel. WHERE: This applies to all departments involved in network management.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain documentation of the network segmentation architecture and controls.
compliances.Scope: IT Security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure clear documentation of network segmentation architecture for effective management.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Misconfiguration of network segmentation leading to unauthorized access or data breaches.

mitigation.1: Regular audits of documentation
mitigation.2: Training for IT Security team on documentation best practices
mitigation.3: Implement version control for documentation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, network breaches, data loss

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Documentation Update on Network Architecture Changes
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Documentation must be updated whenever changes are made to the network architecture.
compliances.Scope: IT Security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure documentation reflects the current state of network architecture for accurate reference.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated documentation leading to misconfigurations or security vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Implement change management process for documentation updates
mitigation.2: Automate documentation update notifications
mitigation.3: Regular review of documentation by IT Security team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, network downtime

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Store documentation in a centralized repository accessible to relevant personnel.
compliances.Scope: All departments involved in network management
compliances.Objective: To ensure easy access to network segmentation documentation for relevant personnel.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All departments involved in network management
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccessibility of documentation leading to delays in troubleshooting or decision-making.

mitigation.1: Implement access controls for documentation repository
mitigation.2: Regularly audit access logs for documentation repository
mitigation.3: Provide training on accessing documentation to relevant personnel

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Delays in network troubleshooting, decision-making bottlenecks, security incidents
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All departments involved in network management

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for managing third-party service providers that store, process, or transmit cardholder data on behalf of the organization. It is essential to ensure that third-party providers comply with PCI DSS requirements to protect cardholder data effectively. This policy outlines the responsibilities of both the organization and the service providers.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all third-party service providers that handle cardholder data on behalf of the organization. It covers all personnel involved in vendor management, compliance, and security, as well as any contracts or agreements with third-party providers.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all third-party service providers comply with PCI DSS requirements to protect cardholder data. This includes establishing clear responsibilities and monitoring compliance to mitigate risks associated with outsourcing. The policy aims to achieve measurable outcomes in vendor management and compliance.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for assessing third-party service providers' PCI DSS compliance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Require third-party service providers to undergo PCI DSS assessments and provide evidence of compliance. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for managing third-party assessments. WHEN: Assessments must be conducted annually or upon contract renewal. HOW: Review the service provider's Attestation of Compliance and supporting documentation. WHERE: This applies to all third-party service providers with access to cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All third-party service providers must undergo an annual PCI DSS assessment and provide evidence of compliance.
compliances.Scope: All third-party service providers with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure third-party service providers maintain PCI DSS compliance
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units utilizing third-party service providers
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance could result in data breaches and financial penalties

mitigation.1: Ensure timely completion of assessments
mitigation.2: Implement regular monitoring of third-party compliance status
mitigation.3: Provide training to third-party providers on PCI DSS requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach due to third-party non-compliance leading to reputational damage and financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units relying on third-party services

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Contract Renewal Third-Party PCI DSS Assessment
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Third-party service providers must undergo a PCI DSS assessment upon contract renewal.
compliances.Scope: All third-party service providers with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure ongoing compliance with PCI DSS requirements
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units engaging third-party services
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to assess compliance at contract renewal could lead to continued non-compliance and data security risks

mitigation.1: Include assessment requirements in contract renewal terms
mitigation.2: Establish clear communication channels with third-party providers regarding assessment timelines
mitigation.3: Implement automated reminders for upcoming assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach due to lack of updated compliance assessments during contract renewals

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The Compliance Officer must review the service provider's Attestation of Compliance and supporting documentation.
compliances.Scope: All third-party service providers with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To verify the authenticity and accuracy of third-party compliance documentation
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to review compliance documentation could result in reliance on inaccurate or falsified information

mitigation.1: Establish standardized review procedures for compliance documentation
mitigation.2: Implement regular training for Compliance Officer on document verification techniques
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic audits of compliance documentation review process

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Relying on falsified compliance documentation leading to security breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compliance department and associated business units

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the contractual requirements for third-party service providers regarding PCI DSS compliance.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Include PCI DSS compliance requirements in contracts with third-party service providers. WHO: The Legal and Compliance teams are responsible for contract management. WHEN: Contracts must be reviewed and updated at least annually. HOW: Ensure that contracts clearly define the scope of services and compliance responsibilities. WHERE: This applies to all contracts with third-party service providers.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Inclusion of PCI DSS Compliance Requirements in Contracts
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All contracts with third-party service providers must clearly include PCI DSS compliance requirements.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all contracts with third-party service providers
compliances.Objective: To ensure that third-party service providers adhere to PCI DSS compliance requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to data breaches and financial penalties

mitigation.1: Regular monitoring of contract compliance
mitigation.2: Conducting periodic audits of third-party service providers
mitigation.3: Implementing data encryption measures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach due to third-party non-compliance with PCI DSS
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Legal and financial implications for the organization

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Review and Update of Contracts
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Contracts with third-party service providers must be reviewed and updated at least annually.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all contracts with third-party service providers
compliances.Objective: To ensure that contract terms remain current and compliant with PCI DSS requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated contracts may not reflect current compliance requirements, leading to non-compliance

mitigation.1: Establishing a contract review schedule
mitigation.2: Automating contract renewal reminders
mitigation.3: Conducting regular compliance audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance due to outdated contract terms
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Legal and financial risks for the organization

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Clear Definition of Services and Compliance Responsibilities
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Contracts with third-party service providers must clearly define the scope of services and compliance responsibilities.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all contracts with third-party service providers
compliances.Objective: To ensure mutual understanding of service scope and compliance obligations
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ambiguity in service scope and compliance responsibilities may lead to misunderstandings and non-compliance

mitigation.1: Detailed contract drafting with specific service descriptions
mitigation.2: Regular communication with service providers on compliance matters
mitigation.3: Periodic review of service scope and compliance obligations

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misinterpretation of service scope and compliance responsibilities
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Legal and financial risks for the organization

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for engaging a Qualified Security Assessor (QSA) to ensure compliance with PCI DSS standards. It is crucial for organizations to select a qualified firm that can accurately assess their adherence to PCI DSS requirements, provide guidance, and validate compliance. The policy aims to establish a structured approach for selecting and working with QSAs to enhance the security posture of the organization.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including merchants, service providers, and internal security teams. It covers all systems and applications that handle payment card data, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy is applicable to all personnel involved in the selection and engagement of QSAs, as well as any third-party vendors that may be involved in the assessment process.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that the organization engages a qualified and competent QSA to conduct PCI DSS assessments, thereby achieving compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy aims to establish measurable outcomes such as successful completion of assessments, accurate validation of compliance, and improved understanding of PCI DSS among internal staff. Additionally, it seeks to mitigate risks associated with non-compliance and enhance the overall security of payment card data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the criteria for selecting a Qualified Security Assessor (QSA) to ensure that the organization engages a qualified firm capable of conducting thorough PCI DSS assessments.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish a set of criteria for evaluating potential QSAs, including certification status, experience with similar organizations, and references. WHO: The Compliance Officer and IT Security Manager are responsible for the selection process. WHEN: The criteria must be established and reviewed annually or whenever a new QSA is needed. HOW: Use a standardized evaluation form to assess potential QSAs against the established criteria. WHERE: This process applies to all organizational units involved in PCI DSS compliance.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All potential QSAs must hold a valid certification from the PCI Security Standards Council.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all organizational units involved in PCI DSS compliance
compliances.Objective: Ensure that only certified QSAs are considered for conducting PCI DSS assessments
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: Compliance Officer and IT Security Manager
compliances.PossibleDamage: Engaging a non-certified QSA may result in inaccurate assessments and non-compliance penalties.

mitigation.1: Verify QSA certification status with the PCI Security Standards Council before engagement
mitigation.2: Maintain a list of certified QSAs for reference
mitigation.3: Regularly update the list of certified QSAs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Engaging a non-certified QSA may lead to inaccurate assessments, potential data breaches, and regulatory fines.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Potential QSAs must demonstrate experience in conducting PCI DSS assessments for organizations similar in size and complexity.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all organizational units involved in PCI DSS compliance
compliances.Objective: Ensure that selected QSAs have relevant experience to effectively assess the organization's compliance
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: Compliance Officer and IT Security Manager
compliances.PossibleDamage: Engaging an inexperienced QSA may result in incomplete assessments and unidentified vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Request case studies or references from potential QSAs
mitigation.2: Conduct interviews to assess the QSA's experience
mitigation.3: Consider feedback from similar organizations

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Engaging an inexperienced QSA may lead to incomplete assessments, unidentified vulnerabilities, and potential data breaches.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct reference checks for potential QSAs to validate their past performance and reputation.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all organizational units involved in PCI DSS compliance
compliances.Objective: Ensure that selected QSAs have a proven track record of successful PCI DSS assessments
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: Compliance Officer and IT Security Manager
compliances.PossibleDamage: Neglecting reference checks may result in engaging unreliable QSAs with a history of subpar performance.

mitigation.1: Request and contact references provided by potential QSAs
mitigation.2: Verify the authenticity of references and feedback
mitigation.3: Consider past performance in similar engagements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Neglecting reference checks may lead to engaging unreliable QSAs, compromised assessments, and potential data breaches.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the process for engaging a Qualified Security Assessor (QSA) to ensure compliance with PCI DSS standards.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Follow a structured engagement process that includes issuing a Request for Proposal (RFP), evaluating proposals, and conducting interviews with potential QSAs. WHO: The Compliance Officer will lead the engagement process, with input from relevant stakeholders. WHEN: The engagement process should begin at least six months prior to the scheduled assessment. HOW: Document the engagement process and maintain records of all communications and decisions. WHERE: This process applies to all departments involved in PCI DSS compliance.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Initiate the engagement process with a QSA at least six months prior to the scheduled assessment.
compliances.Scope: All departments involved in PCI DSS compliance
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely engagement with a QSA for compliance with PCI DSS standards
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delay in engaging a QSA may result in non-compliance with PCI DSS standards and potential fines.

mitigation.1: Set up reminders for engagement process milestones
mitigation.2: Allocate resources for timely engagement
mitigation.3: Establish clear communication channels with QSAs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Delay in engaging a QSA leading to non-compliance with PCI DSS standards

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Issue a Request for Proposal (RFP) and evaluate proposals from potential QSAs.
compliances.Scope: Compliance Officer, relevant stakeholders
compliances.Objective: To select a QSA that best meets the organization's PCI DSS compliance needs
compliances.PossibleDamage: Selecting an inadequate QSA may result in incomplete assessment and non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Develop clear evaluation criteria for proposals
mitigation.2: Conduct thorough background checks on potential QSAs
mitigation.3: Involve key stakeholders in the evaluation process

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Selection of an inadequate QSA leading to incomplete assessment

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct interviews with potential QSAs to assess their capabilities and fit for the organization.
compliances.Scope: Compliance Officer, relevant stakeholders
compliances.Objective: To select a QSA that aligns with the organization's culture and requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Selecting a QSA that does not align with organizational values may result in ineffective compliance assessment.

mitigation.1: Develop interview questions aligned with organizational values
mitigation.2: Include key stakeholders in the interview process
mitigation.3: Assess cultural fit along with technical capabilities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Selection of a QSA not aligned with organizational values

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for selecting and engaging an Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV) to ensure compliance with PCI DSS external vulnerability scanning requirements. It is crucial for organizations to choose qualified ASVs that adhere to PCI Security Standards Council guidelines to maintain the security of payment card data. The policy establishes the criteria for ASV selection, responsibilities, and compliance reporting.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including IT, compliance, and risk management teams. It covers all systems and applications that process, store, or transmit payment card information, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy also encompasses third-party relationships with ASVs and the handling of all types of payment card data.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all organizations engaging ASVs comply with PCI DSS requirements for external vulnerability scanning. This includes establishing measurable criteria for ASV selection, ensuring non-disruptive scanning practices, and facilitating effective communication between ASVs and customers. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with vulnerabilities in payment card processing systems and enhance overall security posture.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy defines the criteria for selecting an Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV) to ensure compliance with PCI DSS requirements. It outlines the qualifications, capabilities, and responsibilities of the ASV in relation to external vulnerability scanning.
subpolicies.Control: Organizations must evaluate potential ASVs based on the following criteria: 1) Qualification by the PCI Security Standards Council (WHAT), 2) Capability to perform non-disruptive scans without causing system reboots or DNS interference (WHAT), 3) Agreement on compliance reporting processes (WHAT), 4) Responsible roles include the IT Security Manager and Compliance Officer (WHO), 5) Evaluation must occur annually or upon significant changes in the ASV's services (WHEN), 6) Evaluation methods include reviewing ASV qualifications, conducting interviews, and assessing past performance (HOW), 7) This applies to all systems and applications involved in payment card processing (WHERE).

compliances.ComplianceTitle: ASV Qualification by PCI Security Standards Council
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Organizations must ensure that the selected ASV is qualified by the PCI Security Standards Council.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and applications involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To ensure that the ASV meets the necessary standards set by the PCI Security Standards Council
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may result in inadequate vulnerability scanning leading to potential data breaches

mitigation.1: Verify ASV qualification status with the PCI Security Standards Council
mitigation.2: Maintain documentation of ASV qualification status
mitigation.3: Regularly monitor ASV qualification status updates

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to verify ASV qualification may lead to ineffective vulnerability scanning and potential security incidents

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Organizations must ensure that the selected ASV is capable of performing non-disruptive scans without causing system reboots or DNS interference.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and applications involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To ensure that vulnerability scanning does not disrupt normal system operations
compliances.PossibleDamage: Disruptive scans may lead to system downtime, loss of data, or service interruptions

mitigation.1: Request demonstration of non-disruptive scanning capabilities from ASV
mitigation.2: Include non-disruptive scanning requirement in ASV contract
mitigation.3: Regularly monitor ASV scanning practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Disruptive scanning practices may cause system downtime, data loss, or service interruptions

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Organizations must have an agreement with the selected ASV on compliance reporting processes.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and applications involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To ensure that compliance reporting processes are clearly defined and agreed upon with the ASV
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of agreement on compliance reporting may lead to incomplete or inaccurate reporting

mitigation.1: Establish clear compliance reporting requirements with ASV
mitigation.2: Include compliance reporting agreement in ASV contract
mitigation.3: Regularly review compliance reports for accuracy

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incomplete or inaccurate compliance reporting may result in regulatory non-compliance and penalties

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for engaging an Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV) and the requirements for reporting the results of vulnerability scans to relevant stakeholders.
subpolicies.Control: Organizations must engage an ASV by formalizing a contract that includes the scope of services and reporting requirements (WHAT). The IT Security Manager is responsible for contract management and communication with the ASV (WHO). Engagement must occur prior to the scheduled vulnerability scans, typically on an annual basis or as needed (WHEN). The engagement process includes drafting and signing a contract, scheduling scans, and establishing reporting timelines (HOW). This applies to all departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting (WHERE).

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Organizations must engage an ASV by formalizing a contract that includes the scope of services and reporting requirements.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting
compliances.Objective: To ensure that vulnerability scans are conducted by a qualified ASV and that reporting requirements are met
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to engage an ASV may result in undetected vulnerabilities leading to potential data breaches and non-compliance penalties.

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update the ASV contract to ensure it aligns with current requirements
mitigation.2: Implement continuous monitoring of vulnerabilities to supplement periodic scans
mitigation.3: Conduct regular audits of ASV reports to verify accuracy

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to engage an ASV may result in undetected vulnerabilities leading to potential data breaches and non-compliance penalties.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting would be impacted by this risk

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The IT Security Manager is responsible for contract management and communication with the ASV.
compliances.Scope: All departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting
compliances.Objective: To ensure clear communication and effective management of the ASV contract
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of clear communication and effective management may lead to misunderstandings, delays, and non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Provide regular training and updates to the IT Security Manager on ASV engagement requirements
mitigation.2: Establish clear communication channels and escalation procedures with the ASV
mitigation.3: Implement regular performance reviews of the IT Security Manager's ASV management

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of clear communication and effective management may lead to misunderstandings, delays, and non-compliance.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting would be impacted by this risk

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Engagement must occur prior to the scheduled vulnerability scans, typically on an annual basis or as needed.
compliances.Scope: All departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting
compliances.Objective: To ensure that vulnerability scans are conducted in a timely manner to maintain security posture
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delayed engagement may result in missed vulnerability scans, leaving systems exposed to potential threats.

mitigation.1: Establish automated reminders for ASV engagement deadlines
mitigation.2: Implement a proactive engagement schedule based on risk assessment findings
mitigation.3: Conduct regular reviews of engagement timelines to identify and address delays

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Delayed engagement may result in missed vulnerability scans, leaving systems exposed to potential threats.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All departments involved in payment card processing and compliance reporting would be impacted by this risk

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for defining and managing the scope of the cardholder data environment (CDE) as part of PCI DSS compliance. It is essential for organizations to accurately identify all system components that handle cardholder data to ensure compliance and mitigate risks. This policy applies to all personnel involved in the management of cardholder data and related systems.
policies.PolicyName: Scope Definition and Management for PCI DSS Compliance
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units and personnel involved in the handling of cardholder data, including IT, compliance, and security teams. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that process, store, or transmit cardholder data, including on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. The policy also includes third-party service providers that interact with the CDE.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all components of the cardholder data environment are accurately identified and managed to maintain PCI DSS compliance. This includes establishing a clear understanding of data flows, ensuring that all systems are appropriately scoped, and mitigating risks associated with cardholder data handling. The policy aims to achieve measurable outcomes such as reduced compliance costs, improved security posture, and enhanced stakeholder protection.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Identification and Documentation of Cardholder Data Locations
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for identifying and documenting all locations of cardholder data within the organization. It ensures that all data flows are mapped and that the scope of PCI DSS compliance is accurately defined.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Identify and document all locations of cardholder data within the organization. WHO: The IT security team is responsible for conducting the identification process. WHEN: This process must be completed at least annually and prior to the annual PCI DSS assessment. HOW: Utilize data discovery tools and manual reviews to locate cardholder data. WHERE: This applies to all systems and locations where cardholder data may reside, including on-premises, cloud, and third-party environments.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Identification of Cardholder Data Locations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The IT security team must conduct an annual identification and documentation process of all locations of cardholder data within the organization.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and locations where cardholder data may reside.
compliances.Objective: To ensure accurate mapping of data flows and define the scope of PCI DSS compliance.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may result in unauthorized access to cardholder data and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement data discovery tools for regular scanning
mitigation.2: Conduct periodic manual reviews of data locations
mitigation.3: Implement access controls to restrict unauthorized access

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, regulatory fines.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Prior to the annual PCI DSS assessment, the IT security team must identify and document all locations of cardholder data within the organization.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and locations where cardholder data may reside.
compliances.Objective: To ensure readiness for the annual PCI DSS assessment and accurately define compliance scope.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to map data locations may result in incomplete compliance assessment and potential non-compliance penalties.

mitigation.1: Conduct regular data mapping exercises
mitigation.2: Implement automated data discovery tools
mitigation.3: Document data flow diagrams

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incomplete compliance assessment, non-compliance penalties, data breaches.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Verify and document all locations of cardholder data within third-party environments where the organization's data resides.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all third-party environments hosting cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure third-party compliance with PCI DSS requirements and data security standards.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: IT Security Team, Vendor Management Team
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance by third parties may result in data breaches and regulatory penalties for the organization.

mitigation.1: Conduct regular third-party audits
mitigation.2: Include data location verification in vendor contracts
mitigation.3: Implement data encryption for third-party data transfers

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Third-party data breaches, regulatory fines, reputational damage.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Vendor Management Team

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Segmentation and Isolation of the Cardholder Data Environment
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy provides guidelines for segmenting and isolating the cardholder data environment from other network components to reduce PCI DSS scope and enhance security.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement network segmentation to isolate the cardholder data environment from other systems. WHO: The network engineering team is responsible for designing and implementing segmentation controls. WHEN: Segmentation must be established prior to processing cardholder data and reviewed annually. HOW: Use firewalls and access control lists to enforce segmentation. WHERE: This applies to all network components that interact with the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement network segmentation to isolate the cardholder data environment from other systems using firewalls and access control lists.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all network components that interact with the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To reduce PCI DSS scope and enhance security by isolating cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, non-compliance penalties

mitigation.1: Regular review and update of segmentation controls
mitigation.2: Continuous monitoring of network traffic for anomalies
mitigation.3: Regular security training for network engineering team

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, unauthorized access to cardholder data, non-compliance fines

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review and validate network segmentation controls annually to ensure effectiveness
compliances.Scope: Applies to all network components that interact with the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To maintain the integrity of the cardholder data environment segmentation
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated segmentation controls, increased risk of unauthorized access

mitigation.1: Regularly scheduled annual reviews of segmentation controls
mitigation.2: Documentation of review findings and actions taken
mitigation.3: Training for network engineering team on updated controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated segmentation controls, increased risk of unauthorized access

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Maintain detailed documentation of the design and implementation of network segmentation controls
compliances.Scope: Applies to all network components that interact with the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To ensure transparency and accountability in the segmentation design process
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of transparency in segmentation design, difficulty in troubleshooting

mitigation.1: Comprehensive documentation of segmentation design and implementation
mitigation.2: Regular audits of documentation for accuracy and completeness
mitigation.3: Training for network engineering team on documentation standards

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Lack of transparency in segmentation design, difficulty in troubleshooting

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for managing and monitoring third-party service providers that have access to cardholder data, ensuring they comply with PCI DSS requirements.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and maintain contracts with third-party service providers that outline PCI DSS compliance responsibilities. WHO: The compliance team is responsible for managing third-party relationships. WHEN: Contracts must be reviewed and updated annually or upon changes in service. HOW: Conduct regular assessments of third-party compliance and require evidence of PCI DSS assessments. WHERE: This applies to all third-party service providers that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Establish Contracts with Third-Party Service Providers
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All third-party service providers must have contracts outlining their PCI DSS compliance responsibilities.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all third-party service providers that store, process, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure third-party service providers comply with PCI DSS requirements.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance could lead to data breaches and financial penalties.

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update contracts
mitigation.2: Conduct regular assessments of third-party compliance
mitigation.3: Require evidence of PCI DSS assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compliance team and cardholder data security

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Review of Third-Party Service Provider Contracts
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Contracts with third-party service providers must be reviewed and updated annually or upon changes in service.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all contracts with third-party service providers.
compliances.Objective: To ensure contracts remain up-to-date and reflect current PCI DSS compliance responsibilities.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated contracts could lead to misunderstandings and non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Establish a contract review schedule
mitigation.2: Update contracts promptly upon changes in service
mitigation.3: Communicate contract changes clearly to third-party providers

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misunderstandings, non-compliance

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular assessments of third-party compliance with PCI DSS requirements.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all third-party service providers storing, processing, or transmitting cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To verify ongoing compliance with PCI DSS requirements.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of regular assessments could lead to undetected non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Establish assessment schedule
mitigation.2: Document assessment findings and remediation plans
mitigation.3: Escalate non-compliance issues to management

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected non-compliance, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Compliance team and cardholder data security

versions.PolicyName: Scope Definition and Management for PCI DSS Compliance

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for merchants and service providers to complete the Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) as part of their compliance with the PCI DSS. It ensures that organizations accurately report their PCI DSS compliance status and take necessary remediation actions when required. The policy applies to all entities that process, store, or transmit cardholder data and are not required to submit a Report on Compliance (ROC).
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all merchants and service providers that accept, process, store, or transmit cardholder data. It covers all organizational units involved in payment processing, including IT, compliance, and finance departments. The policy is applicable to all systems, applications, and infrastructure used for payment processing, including on-premises and cloud environments. It also includes third-party service providers that handle cardholder data on behalf of the organization.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all applicable merchants and service providers complete the Self-Assessment Questionnaire accurately and timely, thereby maintaining compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with cardholder data handling and to ensure that organizations take appropriate remediation actions when compliance gaps are identified. Successful implementation will result in improved security posture and reduced risk of data breaches.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the process for selecting the appropriate Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) based on the merchant's payment processing environment and completing the SAQ accurately.
subpolicies.Control: Organizations must identify the correct SAQ type by consulting with their acquiring bank or payment card brand (WHO). The SAQ must be completed annually (WHEN) by designated compliance personnel using the PCI DSS SAQ Instructions and Guidelines document (HOW). The completed SAQ must be submitted to the acquiring bank or payment card brand within 30 days of completion (WHERE).

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Organizations must identify the correct SAQ type by consulting with their acquiring bank or payment card brand.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all organizations processing payment transactions
compliances.Objective: To ensure the accurate selection of the appropriate SAQ type
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may result in inaccurate SAQ completion and potential security vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regular training on SAQ types and requirements
mitigation.2: Documentation of SAQ selection process
mitigation.3: Internal audits to verify SAQ type selection

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incorrect SAQ type selection leading to non-compliance with PCI DSS

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The SAQ must be completed annually by designated compliance personnel using the PCI DSS SAQ Instructions and Guidelines document.
compliances.Scope: Designated compliance personnel
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely and accurate completion of the SAQ
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to complete the SAQ annually may result in non-compliance and security vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Establish clear timelines for SAQ completion
mitigation.2: Provide training on SAQ completion process
mitigation.3: Implement reminders for upcoming SAQ deadlines

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Missed SAQ deadlines leading to non-compliance

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The completed SAQ must be submitted to the acquiring bank or payment card brand within 30 days of completion.
compliances.Scope: Compliance personnel, finance department
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely submission of the completed SAQ
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delayed submission may result in penalties and non-compliance

mitigation.1: Establish clear submission process
mitigation.2: Implement reminders for submission deadlines
mitigation.3: Engage with acquiring bank for submission guidance

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Delayed SAQ submission leading to non-compliance

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for documenting remediation actions when any SAQ question is answered 'no'.
subpolicies.Control: If any SAQ question is answered 'no', the responsible compliance officer must document the remediation actions and timelines (WHAT). This documentation must be reviewed and approved by management (WHO) within 15 days of the SAQ completion (WHEN). The documentation must be stored securely and made available for audit purposes (HOW) within the organization's compliance management system (WHERE).

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Remediation Action Documentation for SAQ No Answers
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Document the remediation actions and timelines when any SAQ question is answered 'no'. Review and approve by management within 15 days of SAQ completion. Store securely in compliance management system.
compliances.Scope: All business units within the organization
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely and accurate documentation of remediation actions for non-compliant SAQ responses
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential fines or penalties for non-compliance, reputational damage

mitigation.1: Regular training on SAQ requirements
mitigation.2: Automated reminders for documentation deadlines
mitigation.3: Periodic audits of remediation documentation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to document remediation actions could lead to regulatory non-compliance and financial penalties
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units could be impacted by regulatory fines or reputational damage

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for reporting PCI DSS compliance status to acquiring financial institutions and payment card brands. It ensures that all entities involved in payment card processing adhere to the necessary reporting standards, including the submission of Self-Assessment Questionnaires (SAQs) and Reports on Compliance (RoCs). The policy aims to maintain transparency and accountability in the handling of cardholder data.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including merchants, service providers, and third-party vendors. It covers all systems and applications that handle cardholder data, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile environments. The policy is applicable across all geographic locations where the organization operates and includes all data types related to payment card transactions.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that all entities involved in payment card processing maintain compliance with PCI DSS reporting requirements. This includes achieving timely submission of compliance reports, ensuring the accuracy of reported information, and mitigating risks associated with non-compliance. The policy aims to enhance the security posture of the organization and protect cardholder data effectively.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for completing and submitting the Self-Assessment Questionnaire (SAQ) as part of the PCI DSS compliance reporting process. It specifies the responsibilities of personnel involved in the assessment and the timeline for submission.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Complete and submit the appropriate SAQ based on the entity's payment card processing environment. WHO: The Compliance Officer is responsible for overseeing the completion of the SAQ. WHEN: The SAQ must be submitted annually or as required by the acquiring financial institution. HOW: Utilize the SAQ template provided by the PCI SSC and ensure all sections are accurately filled out. WHERE: The SAQ must be submitted through the designated portal of the acquiring financial institution.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All entities must complete and submit the appropriate SAQ annually or as required by the acquiring financial institution.
compliances.Scope: All personnel involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely submission of accurate SAQs for PCI DSS compliance reporting
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may result in fines, penalties, or suspension of payment processing services.

mitigation.1: Set up reminders for SAQ submission deadlines
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update SAQ templates
mitigation.3: Provide training on completing SAQ accurately

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Late or inaccurate SAQ submissions leading to compliance violations

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Utilization of PCI SSC SAQ Template
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All personnel must utilize the SAQ template provided by the PCI SSC and ensure all sections are accurately filled out.
compliances.Scope: All personnel involved in completing SAQs
compliances.Objective: To ensure consistency and accuracy in SAQ submissions
compliances.PossibleDamage: Incomplete or inaccurate SAQ submissions may lead to compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Provide training on using the SAQ template
mitigation.2: Implement review process for completed SAQs
mitigation.3: Regularly update SAQ template based on changes in PCI DSS requirements

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incomplete or inaccurate SAQ submissions due to lack of template utilization

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All entities must submit the SAQ through the designated portal of the acquiring financial institution.
compliances.Scope: All entities processing payment cards
compliances.Objective: To ensure secure and compliant submission of SAQs
compliances.PossibleDamage: Submission through unauthorized portals may compromise data security and lead to compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Provide training on portal usage
mitigation.2: Implement access controls to designated portal
mitigation.3: Regularly monitor portal access logs

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach due to submission through unauthorized portals

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for preparing and submitting the Report on Compliance (RoC) for entities undergoing on-site assessments. It includes guidelines for documenting the assessment process and findings.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Prepare and submit the RoC, including all required sections as outlined in the PCI SSC template. WHO: The Compliance Officer and the designated assessor are responsible for the preparation and submission of the RoC. WHEN: The RoC must be submitted within 30 days of the completion of the on-site assessment. HOW: Follow the RoC template and ensure all findings and observations are documented accurately. WHERE: The RoC must be submitted to the acquiring financial institution and retained for internal records.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The RoC must be prepared and submitted to the acquiring financial institution within 30 days of the completion of the on-site assessment.
compliances.Scope: Applies to Compliance Officer and designated assessor
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely submission of RoC to meet regulatory requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may result in fines or penalties from regulatory authorities

mitigation.1: Set up reminders and alerts for submission deadlines
mitigation.2: Establish a review process to ensure timely completion
mitigation.3: Allocate sufficient resources for RoC preparation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to submit RoC on time may lead to regulatory scrutiny and reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All findings and observations from the on-site assessment must be accurately documented in the RoC.
compliances.Scope: Applies to Compliance Officer and designated assessor
compliances.Objective: Ensure transparency and completeness of assessment documentation
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate documentation may lead to misinterpretation of assessment results

mitigation.1: Provide training on proper documentation practices
mitigation.2: Implement a review process for documentation accuracy
mitigation.3: Use standardized templates for consistent documentation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inaccurate documentation may result in incorrect remediation actions and ineffective compliance measures

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Use of RoC template for submission
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The RoC must be prepared and submitted using the PCI SSC template to ensure consistency and completeness.
compliances.Scope: Applies to Compliance Officer and designated assessor
compliances.Objective: Maintain alignment with industry standards and best practices
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance with industry standards may result in audit findings

mitigation.1: Provide training on template usage
mitigation.2: Regularly update templates to reflect current requirements
mitigation.3: Conduct template compliance checks before submission

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance with template usage may lead to inconsistencies in reporting and assessment

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for conducting and reporting quarterly network scans to ensure ongoing compliance with PCI DSS. It outlines the responsibilities for monitoring and remediating vulnerabilities.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct quarterly network scans and report the results to the Compliance Officer. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for performing the scans and documenting the results. WHEN: Scans must be conducted quarterly, with reports submitted within 10 days of scan completion. HOW: Utilize an Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV) to perform the scans and ensure all findings are addressed. WHERE: Reports must be submitted to the Compliance Officer and stored securely for audit purposes.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct quarterly network scans as per PCI DSS requirements
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure ongoing compliance with PCI DSS
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to data breaches and financial penalties

mitigation.1: Implement regular training for IT Security Team on conducting scans
mitigation.2: Implement automated scanning tools for efficiency
mitigation.3: Regularly review and update scanning procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches due to undetected vulnerabilities

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Report quarterly network scan results to Compliance Officer
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure transparency and accountability in compliance reporting
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to regulatory fines and audit failures

mitigation.1: Establish clear reporting procedures and timelines
mitigation.2: Implement secure data transfer protocols for reports
mitigation.3: Regularly review and verify reported scan results

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to report vulnerabilities may result in undetected security threats

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Submit quarterly network scan reports within 10 days of completion
compliances.Scope: IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely reporting and remediation of vulnerabilities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delayed reporting may lead to prolonged exposure to security risks

mitigation.1: Establish automated reporting mechanisms for timely submissions
mitigation.2: Implement escalation procedures for delayed submissions
mitigation.3: Regularly monitor submission timelines and enforce compliance

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Delayed reporting may result in unaddressed vulnerabilities

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for integrating PCI DSS compliance into the daily operations of the organization. It emphasizes the importance of continuous monitoring and maintenance of security controls to ensure ongoing compliance with PCI DSS standards. By embedding PCI DSS into business-as-usual processes, organizations can proactively manage security risks and maintain a secure environment for payment card data.
policies.PolicyName: Integration of PCI DSS into Business-as-Usual Processes
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including IT, security, compliance, and operations. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that handle payment card data, including on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. The policy also extends to third-party vendors and service providers that interact with payment card information.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that PCI DSS compliance is maintained as an integral part of the organization's operational processes. Specific goals include the timely identification and remediation of security control failures, regular updates to PCI DSS scope following environmental changes, and ongoing training and awareness for personnel involved in payment card processing. The policy aims to mitigate risks associated with payment card data and ensure adherence to PCI DSS standards.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Monitoring and Response to Security Controls
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for monitoring security controls and responding to any failures in a timely manner. It establishes procedures for continuous assessment of security measures to ensure they are functioning as intended.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement continuous monitoring of security controls to assess their effectiveness. WHO: The IT Security Team is responsible for monitoring and reporting. WHEN: Monitoring must occur on a daily basis, with immediate reporting of any failures. HOW: Utilize automated monitoring tools and manual checks to evaluate control performance. WHERE: This applies to all systems and applications that process payment card data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement continuous monitoring of security controls to assess their effectiveness on a daily basis.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that process payment card data
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely identification of any security control failures
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential unauthorized access to payment card data

mitigation.1: Regular security control audits
mitigation.2: Immediate response to control failures
mitigation.3: Enhanced monitoring tools

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to payment card data leading to financial losses

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Report any security control failures immediately upon identification.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that process payment card data
compliances.Objective: Ensure prompt response to security control failures
compliances.PossibleDamage: Extended exposure to vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Establish clear reporting procedures
mitigation.2: Regular training on incident response
mitigation.3: Automated alerting systems

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Delayed response to security control failures leading to data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize automated monitoring tools to evaluate control performance.
compliances.Scope: All systems and applications that process payment card data
compliances.Objective: Enhance efficiency and accuracy of security control monitoring
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inaccurate assessment of control effectiveness

mitigation.1: Regular tool updates and maintenance
mitigation.2: Training on tool usage
mitigation.3: Manual verification of automated results

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misinterpretation of automated monitoring results leading to security gaps

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Change Management and PCI DSS Scope Review
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the review of changes to the environment that may affect PCI DSS compliance. It ensures that any new systems or changes in configurations are assessed for their impact on PCI DSS scope.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Review all changes to systems and configurations prior to implementation to assess PCI DSS impact. WHO: The Change Management Team must conduct the reviews. WHEN: Reviews must occur before any changes are finalized. HOW: Implement a formal change request process that includes PCI DSS impact assessment. WHERE: This applies to all organizational units involved in system changes.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All changes to systems and configurations must be reviewed prior to implementation to assess PCI DSS impact.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units involved in system changes
compliances.Objective: To ensure that changes are assessed for their impact on PCI DSS scope before implementation.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance may lead to unauthorized access to cardholder data and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement multi-factor authentication for system access
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor system logs for suspicious activities
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic security assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, financial losses

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: The Change Management Team must conduct reviews of all changes to systems and configurations.
compliances.Scope: Change Management Team
compliances.Objective: To ensure that changes are assessed for their impact on PCI DSS scope by a dedicated team.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to conduct reviews may result in overlooking critical PCI DSS compliance issues.

mitigation.1: Provide regular training to the Change Management Team on PCI DSS requirements
mitigation.2: Implement automated change impact assessment tools
mitigation.3: Establish clear communication channels between the Change Management Team and other departments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Overlooking critical PCI DSS compliance issues, non-compliance penalties

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement a formal change request process that includes PCI DSS impact assessment.
compliances.Scope: All organizational units involved in system changes
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all system changes go through a structured process that includes PCI DSS impact assessment.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of a formal process may lead to uncontrolled changes and potential non-compliance with PCI DSS requirements.

mitigation.1: Establish clear change request guidelines and approval workflows
mitigation.2: Automate change request tracking and reporting
mitigation.3: Conduct regular process audits to identify gaps and areas for improvement

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Uncontrolled system changes, potential non-compliance penalties

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirement for periodic reviews of PCI DSS compliance and effective communication of security practices to all relevant personnel.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct periodic reviews of PCI DSS compliance and communicate findings to all stakeholders. WHO: The Compliance Team is responsible for conducting reviews and disseminating information. WHEN: Reviews must occur at least quarterly, with communications issued immediately following each review. HOW: Utilize compliance checklists and reporting tools to assess adherence to PCI DSS. WHERE: This applies to all departments involved in payment card processing.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct quarterly reviews of PCI DSS compliance using compliance checklists and reporting tools.
compliances.Scope: All departments involved in payment card processing
compliances.Objective: To ensure ongoing adherence to PCI DSS requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance could lead to data breaches and financial penalties

mitigation.1: Implement regular training on PCI DSS requirements
mitigation.2: Implement multi-factor authentication for access to payment card data
mitigation.3: Regularly update security patches and software

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach resulting in loss of customer trust and financial penalties

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Communicate compliance findings to all stakeholders immediately following each review.
compliances.Scope: All relevant personnel
compliances.Objective: To ensure transparency and awareness of compliance status
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of awareness could lead to continued non-compliance and potential security incidents

mitigation.1: Establish regular communication channels for compliance updates
mitigation.2: Provide training on interpreting compliance findings
mitigation.3: Implement feedback mechanisms for stakeholders

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misinterpretation of compliance findings leading to incorrect actions

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Document all compliance reviews and findings for record-keeping and audit purposes.
compliances.Scope: Compliance Team
compliances.Objective: To maintain a comprehensive record of compliance activities
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of documentation could lead to audit failures and compliance gaps

mitigation.1: Implement a centralized compliance documentation system
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update compliance records
mitigation.3: Train compliance team on proper documentation practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Incomplete or inaccurate compliance documentation leading to audit findings

versions.PolicyName: Integration of PCI DSS into Business-as-Usual Processes

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for building and maintaining a secure network and systems to protect cardholder data. It outlines the necessary configurations for firewalls and routers, ensuring that all connections to the cardholder data environment are secure and monitored. The policy aims to prevent unauthorized access and data breaches through stringent network security controls.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including IT departments, network administrators, and any personnel managing systems that store, process, or transmit cardholder data. It covers all network devices, applications, and infrastructure within the cardholder data environment, including on-premises and cloud-based systems, as well as remote access devices.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the security of cardholder data by implementing robust network security measures. Specific goals include preventing unauthorized access, ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements, and maintaining operational effectiveness through regular reviews and updates of security configurations.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the standards for configuring firewalls and routers to protect cardholder data. It includes requirements for documentation, testing, and regular reviews of configurations.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Establish and implement firewall and router configuration standards. WHO: Network administrators and IT security personnel. WHEN: Configuration changes must be tested and documented immediately; reviews of configuration rule sets must occur at least every six months. HOW: Use documented procedures for testing configurations and maintaining diagrams of cardholder data flows. WHERE: Applicable to all firewalls and routers within the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Documentation of Firewall and Router Configurations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All firewall and router configurations must be documented including rules, settings, and changes made.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network administrators and IT security personnel managing firewalls and routers within the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Objective: To ensure transparency and accountability in firewall and router configurations.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to document configurations may lead to misconfigurations, unauthorized access, and data breaches.

mitigation.1: Implement automated documentation tools
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update documentation
mitigation.3: Provide training on documentation best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized changes to configurations, lack of visibility into network traffic, and increased vulnerability to cyber attacks.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Testing of Firewall and Router Configurations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All configuration changes must be tested immediately to ensure they do not introduce vulnerabilities or impact network performance.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network administrators and IT security personnel responsible for implementing configuration changes.
compliances.Objective: To maintain the integrity and security of firewall and router configurations.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to test configurations may result in misconfigurations, security vulnerabilities, and network downtime.

mitigation.1: Implement automated testing tools
mitigation.2: Establish testing protocols and procedures
mitigation.3: Conduct regular audits of testing processes

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unidentified vulnerabilities, network downtime, and unauthorized access due to untested configurations.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Regular Review of Firewall and Router Configurations
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Configuration rule sets must be reviewed at least every six months to ensure they align with security policies and business requirements.
compliances.Scope: Applies to network administrators and IT security personnel conducting configuration reviews.
compliances.Objective: To maintain compliance with security standards and adapt configurations to changing business needs.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Infrequent reviews may result in outdated configurations, non-compliance with security standards, and increased vulnerability to cyber threats.

mitigation.1: Establish a regular review schedule
mitigation.2: Automate configuration review processes
mitigation.3: Implement change management controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated configurations, non-compliance with security standards, and increased exposure to cyber threats.
