subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the restriction of all traffic to and from untrusted networks and hosts, ensuring that only necessary protocols are allowed.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Build firewall and router configurations that restrict all traffic from untrusted networks. WHO: Network security team. WHEN: Configurations must be implemented prior to deployment and reviewed regularly. HOW: Utilize firewall rules to deny all traffic except for necessary protocols. WHERE: Applies to all network devices within the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement firewall rules to deny all traffic except for necessary protocols as per the subpolicy Traffic Restriction and Access Control.
compliances.Scope: Network security team
compliances.Objective: To restrict all traffic to and from untrusted networks and hosts
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to network resources, data breaches

mitigation.1: Regular review of firewall rules
mitigation.2: Continuous monitoring of network traffic
mitigation.3: Implementation of intrusion detection systems

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, network breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure firewall configurations are reviewed regularly to maintain restriction of all traffic from untrusted networks.
compliances.Scope: Network security team
compliances.Objective: To ensure ongoing effectiveness of traffic restriction measures
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective traffic restrictions, increased vulnerability to cyber attacks

mitigation.1: Automated firewall configuration audits
mitigation.2: Periodic penetration testing
mitigation.3: Implementation of network segmentation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misconfigured firewall rules, unauthorized access

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regularly review network device configurations to ensure all traffic is restricted as per the subpolicy Traffic Restriction and Access Control.
compliances.Scope: Network security team
compliances.Objective: To verify adherence to traffic restriction policies on all network devices
compliances.PossibleDamage: Misconfigured devices, unauthorized access

mitigation.1: Automated configuration audits
mitigation.2: Configuration change management process
mitigation.3: Employee training on device configurations

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misconfigured network devices, unauthorized access

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy prohibits direct public access to any system component in the cardholder data environment to enhance security.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Prohibit direct public access between the Internet and any system component in the cardholder data environment. WHO: IT security team. WHEN: This must be enforced at all times. HOW: Implement network segmentation and access controls. WHERE: Applies to all systems within the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement network segmentation to prohibit direct public access between the Internet and any system component in the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: Enhance security by restricting public access to sensitive systems
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breach

mitigation.1: Implement firewall rules to restrict access
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update access controls
mitigation.3: Implement intrusion detection systems

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Enforce access controls to prohibit direct public access between the Internet and any system component in the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: Prevent unauthorized access to sensitive systems
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breach, loss of sensitive information

mitigation.1: Implement strong authentication mechanisms
mitigation.2: Regularly audit access logs
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access attempts, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regularly review and update network segmentation to ensure prohibition of direct public access between the Internet and any system component in the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: IT security team
compliances.Objective: Maintain the effectiveness of network segmentation controls
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access, data breaches

mitigation.1: Conduct quarterly network segmentation audits
mitigation.2: Implement automated monitoring tools
mitigation.3: Train IT staff on network segmentation best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated network segmentation, unauthorized access

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy requires the installation of personal firewall software on devices accessing the cardholder data environment from outside the network.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Install personal firewall software on devices connecting to the Internet. WHO: Employees and IT support staff. WHEN: Installation must occur before devices are used to access the cardholder data environment. HOW: Use approved firewall software and ensure it is configured correctly. WHERE: Applies to all devices, including company and employee-owned devices.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All devices connecting to the Internet must have personal firewall software installed before accessing the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: All employees and IT support staff
compliances.Objective: To protect cardholder data by preventing unauthorized access through devices connecting to the Internet.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Regular monitoring of firewall logs for suspicious activities
mitigation.2: Periodic firewall software updates to address vulnerabilities
mitigation.3: Employee training on recognizing phishing attempts and social engineering tactics

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by potential data breaches and financial losses

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Only approved firewall software should be used on devices connecting to the Internet.
compliances.Scope: All employees and IT support staff
compliances.Objective: To ensure consistency and effectiveness of firewall protection across all devices accessing the cardholder data environment.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective firewall protection leading to unauthorized access and data breaches.

mitigation.1: Regular review of approved firewall software list to include latest security features
mitigation.2: Strict enforcement of using only approved firewall software on all devices
mitigation.3: Periodic testing of firewall software configurations for vulnerabilities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Ineffective firewall protection, unauthorized access, data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by potential data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure that approved firewall software is correctly configured on all devices connecting to the Internet.
compliances.Scope: All employees and IT support staff
compliances.Objective: To maximize the effectiveness of firewall protection by ensuring correct configuration settings.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Misconfigured firewall settings leading to vulnerabilities and unauthorized access.

mitigation.1: Regular audits of firewall configurations for compliance with security standards
mitigation.2: Employee training on firewall software configuration best practices
mitigation.3: Automated configuration management tools for consistent settings across devices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misconfigured firewall settings, vulnerabilities, unauthorized access
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by potential vulnerabilities and unauthorized access

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy ensures that all related security policies and operational procedures are documented and communicated to all affected parties.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document and communicate security policies and operational procedures. WHO: Compliance officers and IT security personnel. WHEN: Documentation must be updated regularly and communicated to all staff. HOW: Use internal communication channels and training sessions. WHERE: Applies to all personnel involved in the cardholder data environment.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All security policies and operational procedures must be updated at least quarterly and communicated to all staff.
compliances.Scope: All personnel involved in the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: Ensure that all staff are aware of the latest security policies and procedures to maintain a secure environment.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased vulnerability to security breaches and non-compliance issues.

mitigation.1: Implement automated reminders for policy updates
mitigation.2: Conduct regular training sessions on updated policies
mitigation.3: Establish a clear communication channel for policy dissemination

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated policies leading to non-compliance issues and security breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All personnel involved in the cardholder data environment must undergo annual training on security policies and procedures.
compliances.Scope: All personnel involved in the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: Ensure that all staff have a clear understanding of security policies and procedures to mitigate risks effectively.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of awareness leading to security incidents and non-compliance issues.

mitigation.1: Develop interactive training modules
mitigation.2: Conduct regular assessments to ensure understanding
mitigation.3: Provide refresher courses on specific topics

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Staff unaware of security policies leading to breaches and compliance violations

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Any changes to security policies and procedures must be communicated to all staff within 48 hours of implementation.
compliances.Scope: All personnel involved in the cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: Ensure that all staff are promptly informed of any policy changes to maintain compliance and security.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Misinterpretation of new policies leading to non-compliance issues.

mitigation.1: Establish a clear communication protocol for policy updates
mitigation.2: Provide training on new policies immediately
mitigation.3: Conduct follow-up sessions to address any questions or concerns

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Staff unaware of policy changes leading to non-compliance

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the requirements for protecting cardholder data to prevent unauthorized access and misuse. It establishes guidelines for data storage, encryption, and key management to ensure compliance with PCI DSS standards. The policy applies to all entities that accept, process, store, or transmit cardholder data.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units and personnel involved in payment card processing, including merchants, service providers, and third-party vendors. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that handle cardholder data, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile environments. The policy encompasses all types of cardholder data, including Primary Account Number (PAN), cardholder name, service code, and expiration date.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of cardholder data by limiting data storage to necessary information, implementing strong encryption methods, and establishing robust key management practices. The policy aims to achieve compliance with PCI DSS requirements, mitigate risks associated with data breaches, and protect stakeholders' sensitive information.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for the storage and retention of cardholder data, ensuring that data is only retained as necessary for business, legal, or regulatory purposes.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Limit cardholder data storage and retention to necessary information and purge unnecessary data quarterly. WHO: Data management teams and compliance officers are responsible for enforcing this subpolicy. WHEN: Data retention reviews must occur quarterly. HOW: Implement data retention policies and automated purging processes. WHERE: This applies to all systems and databases that store cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Data management teams must conduct quarterly reviews to identify and purge unnecessary cardholder data from all systems and databases.
compliances.Scope: Applies to data management teams and compliance officers
compliances.Objective: To ensure that only necessary cardholder data is retained for business, legal, or regulatory purposes
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential exposure to unauthorized access or misuse of retained cardholder data

mitigation.1: Implement automated data purging processes
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor data retention compliance
mitigation.3: Provide training on data retention policies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to retained cardholder data leading to data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement data retention policies across all systems and databases storing cardholder data, specifying the necessary information to be retained and the automated purging processes to be followed.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems and databases storing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To establish clear guidelines for data storage and retention in compliance with business, legal, and regulatory requirements
compliances.PossibleDamage: Non-compliance with data retention policies leading to excessive data storage and increased risk of data breaches

mitigation.1: Regularly update data retention policies based on changing requirements
mitigation.2: Conduct periodic audits to ensure policy adherence
mitigation.3: Provide training on data retention best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Accumulation of unnecessary cardholder data leading to increased storage costs and data security risks

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Establish automated data purging processes to remove unnecessary cardholder data from systems and databases on a quarterly basis.
compliances.Scope: Applies to data management teams and IT departments
compliances.Objective: To ensure timely and secure removal of unnecessary cardholder data to reduce data security risks
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to purge unnecessary data leading to data breaches, non-compliance, and increased storage costs

mitigation.1: Regularly test and monitor data purging processes
mitigation.2: Implement data backup and recovery procedures
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic reviews of purged data

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Accumulation of outdated cardholder data leading to security breaches and regulatory fines

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for encrypting cardholder data to protect it from unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Render cardholder data unreadable using strong encryption methods. WHO: IT security teams are responsible for implementing encryption solutions. WHEN: Encryption must be applied at the time of data storage and transmission. HOW: Utilize strong cryptographic algorithms and key management practices. WHERE: This applies to all storage locations, including databases, backup media, and portable devices.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize strong cryptographic algorithms for encrypting cardholder data
compliances.Scope: IT security teams responsible for implementing encryption solutions
compliances.Objective: Ensure cardholder data is rendered unreadable using robust encryption methods
compliances.PossibleDamage: Risk of unauthorized access to sensitive cardholder data

mitigation.1: Implement multi-factor authentication for access control
mitigation.2: Regularly update encryption algorithms and key management practices
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic security audits and penetration testing

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches resulting in financial losses and reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement secure key management practices for encryption
compliances.Scope: IT security teams responsible for key management
compliances.Objective: Ensure encryption keys are securely stored and managed to protect cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Risk of unauthorized access to encryption keys compromising data security

mitigation.1: Implement encryption key rotation policy
mitigation.2: Use hardware security modules for key storage
mitigation.3: Regularly review and update key management procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Loss of encryption keys leading to data exposure

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Encrypt cardholder data during transmission
compliances.Scope: IT security teams responsible for data transmission security
compliances.Objective: Protect cardholder data from interception during transmission
compliances.PossibleDamage: Risk of data interception leading to unauthorized access

mitigation.1: Implement secure communication protocols (e.g., TLS)
mitigation.2: Encrypt data at rest and in transit
mitigation.3: Monitor network traffic for anomalies

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Man-in-the-middle attacks compromising data integrity

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for managing cryptographic keys used for encrypting cardholder data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Document and implement key management processes to protect encryption keys from unauthorized access. WHO: IT security personnel are responsible for key management. WHEN: Key management procedures must be reviewed and updated annually. HOW: Implement access controls and logging for key management activities. WHERE: This applies to all systems that utilize encryption for cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Review of Key Management Procedures
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Key management procedures must be reviewed and updated annually to ensure the protection of encryption keys.
compliances.Scope: All systems that utilize encryption for cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that key management procedures are regularly reviewed and updated to maintain security
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to encryption keys leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Regularly scheduled annual reviews
mitigation.2: Implementing access controls and logging for key management activities
mitigation.3: Training IT security personnel on key management best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches due to outdated key management procedures
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT security and overall business operations

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Access Controls for Key Management Activities
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement access controls and logging for key management activities to prevent unauthorized access to encryption keys.
compliances.Scope: All systems that utilize encryption for cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To restrict access to key management activities to authorized personnel only
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to encryption keys leading to data breaches

mitigation.1: Role-based access control implementation
mitigation.2: Regular monitoring of access logs
mitigation.3: Two-factor authentication for key management activities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches due to unauthorized access to encryption keys

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Training for IT Security Personnel on Key Management Best Practices
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Provide training to IT security personnel on key management best practices to ensure proper handling of encryption keys.
compliances.Scope: IT security personnel
compliances.Objective: To enhance the knowledge and skills of IT security personnel in key management
compliances.PossibleDamage: Mismanagement of encryption keys leading to security vulnerabilities

mitigation.1: Regular training sessions on key management best practices
mitigation.2: Certification programs for key management
mitigation.3: Internal knowledge sharing sessions on key management

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security vulnerabilities due to lack of key management knowledge

policies.PolicyDescription: The Vulnerability Management Program is designed to systematically identify, assess, and remediate vulnerabilities within the payment card infrastructure. This policy ensures that all systems are protected against malware and that security patches are applied promptly to mitigate risks. It encompasses the deployment of anti-virus software, regular vulnerability scans, and adherence to secure coding practices.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including IT, security, and development teams. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that process, store, or transmit cardholder data, including on-premises, cloud, and mobile environments. The policy also extends to third-party vendors and service providers that interact with the organization's payment card systems.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to establish a comprehensive framework for identifying and mitigating vulnerabilities in the payment card infrastructure. Specific goals include reducing the risk of malware infections, ensuring timely application of security patches, and maintaining compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy aims to enhance the overall security posture of the organization and protect cardholder data from unauthorized access.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for deploying and maintaining anti-virus software across all systems commonly affected by malware. It includes guidelines for ensuring that anti-virus mechanisms are current, actively running, and cannot be disabled by unauthorized users.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Deploy anti-virus software on all systems commonly affected by malware and ensure it is kept current. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for deployment and maintenance. WHEN: Anti-virus software must be deployed immediately on new systems and updated at least monthly. HOW: Use automated tools to manage updates and perform periodic scans. WHERE: Applies to all organizational systems that process, store, or transmit cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All systems commonly affected by malware must have anti-virus software deployed and kept current.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all organizational systems that process, store, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure that systems are protected against malware threats.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Systems may be vulnerable to malware attacks leading to data breaches.

mitigation.1: Regularly update anti-virus software
mitigation.2: Implement network segmentation to isolate infected systems
mitigation.3: Conduct regular security audits

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, system downtime, loss of sensitive information
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Data Protection Team

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Anti-virus software must be updated at least monthly on all systems commonly affected by malware.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all organizational systems that process, store, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To ensure that anti-virus mechanisms are current and effective.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated anti-virus software may not provide adequate protection against new malware threats.

mitigation.1: Schedule regular updates at non-peak hours
mitigation.2: Implement automated update tools
mitigation.3: Monitor update status regularly

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Malware infections, data breaches, system vulnerabilities
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Data Protection Team

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure that anti-virus software cannot be disabled by unauthorized users.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all organizational systems that process, store, or transmit cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: To maintain the integrity and effectiveness of anti-virus protection.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Disabling anti-virus software may expose systems to malware attacks.

mitigation.1: Implement user access controls
mitigation.2: Monitor software disablement attempts
mitigation.3: Regularly review user permissions

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Malware infections, data breaches, system vulnerabilities
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team, Data Protection Team

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the process for identifying, testing, and applying security patches to all critical and non-critical systems. It emphasizes the importance of timely patch deployment based on risk assessments.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Identify and apply security patches to all systems, prioritizing critical patches within one month of release. WHO: IT Operations Team is responsible for patch management. WHEN: Patches must be evaluated and applied within defined timelines based on risk assessments. HOW: Utilize a patch management system to track and document patch status. WHERE: Applies to all systems within the payment card infrastructure.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Timely Deployment of Critical Security Patches
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All critical security patches must be identified and applied within one month of release.
compliances.Scope: IT Operations Team
compliances.Objective: Ensure timely deployment of critical security patches to mitigate potential vulnerabilities.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Exposure to critical security vulnerabilities leading to potential data breaches or system compromises.

mitigation.1: Automate patch deployment processes
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor patch status and compliance
mitigation.3: Implement rollback procedures in case of patch failures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to deploy critical security patches may result in unauthorized access to sensitive data or systems.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Patches must be evaluated and applied within defined timelines based on risk assessments.
compliances.Scope: IT Operations Team
compliances.Objective: Prioritize patch deployment based on risk assessments to address the most critical vulnerabilities first.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to prioritize patch deployment may leave critical systems vulnerable to cyber attacks.

mitigation.1: Establish risk assessment criteria for patch prioritization
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update risk assessment parameters
mitigation.3: Implement a feedback loop for continuous improvement

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Failure to prioritize patch deployment based on risk assessments may result in targeted cyber attacks exploiting known vulnerabilities.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Utilize a patch management system to track and document patch status.
compliances.Scope: IT Operations Team
compliances.Objective: Implement a centralized system for tracking and documenting patch status to ensure visibility and accountability.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of visibility into patch status may lead to oversight of critical patches and non-compliance with security policies.

mitigation.1: Implement automated patch status monitoring
mitigation.2: Regularly audit patch management system for accuracy
mitigation.3: Provide training on patch management system usage

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate tracking of patch status may result in compliance violations and increased vulnerability to cyber threats.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the incorporation of security practices throughout the software development lifecycle to protect against vulnerabilities in applications. It includes guidelines for secure coding and change control procedures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement secure coding practices and conduct security assessments for all software development projects. WHO: Development Teams and Security Analysts are responsible for adherence to secure coding standards. WHEN: Security practices must be integrated into all phases of the software development lifecycle. HOW: Conduct regular code reviews and security testing. WHERE: Applies to all internally developed and third-party software applications.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All software development projects must implement secure coding practices as per the guidelines provided in the subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: Development Teams and Security Analysts
compliances.Objective: To ensure that secure coding practices are followed to protect against vulnerabilities in applications.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches, unauthorized access, and compromised systems due to insecure code.

mitigation.1: Regular training sessions on secure coding practices
mitigation.2: Code reviews by security experts
mitigation.3: Automated code scanning tools

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, system vulnerabilities, financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Development and security teams, overall business operations

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Security Assessments for Software Development Projects
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All software development projects must undergo security assessments to identify and address potential vulnerabilities.
compliances.Scope: Development Teams and Security Analysts
compliances.Objective: To ensure that security assessments are conducted to mitigate risks associated with software vulnerabilities.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Undetected vulnerabilities leading to security breaches, data leaks, and system compromises.

mitigation.1: Regular security assessments throughout the development lifecycle
mitigation.2: Penetration testing by external security experts
mitigation.3: Implementation of secure coding practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, system vulnerabilities, reputational damage
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Development and security teams, customer trust

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Integration of Security Practices in Software Development Lifecycle
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Security practices must be integrated into all phases of the software development lifecycle.
compliances.Scope: Development Teams and Security Analysts
compliances.Objective: To ensure that security practices are followed throughout the software development lifecycle.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Weaknesses in security controls, increased vulnerability to cyber threats, potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Security training for development teams
mitigation.2: Regular security audits
mitigation.3: Incident response planning

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, system vulnerabilities, regulatory fines
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: Development and security teams, regulatory compliance

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for implementing strong access control measures to protect cardholder data and ensure that access is granted only on a need-to-know basis. It is crucial for maintaining the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive payment information. The policy applies to all personnel who have access to cardholder data and systems, ensuring that access is limited to authorized individuals based on their job responsibilities.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units and personnel involved in the processing, storage, or transmission of cardholder data. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that handle cardholder data, including on-premises and cloud environments. The policy also encompasses third-party vendors and service providers with access to cardholder data, ensuring compliance across all operational environments.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure that access to cardholder data is strictly controlled and monitored, thereby reducing the risk of unauthorized access and data breaches. Specific goals include limiting access to only those individuals whose job functions require it, ensuring compliance with PCI DSS requirements, and enhancing overall security posture through effective access control measures.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for user identification and authentication to ensure that all users accessing cardholder data are uniquely identified and authenticated using strong methods.
subpolicies.Control: All users must be assigned a unique identification (ID) before accessing cardholder data. Authentication methods must include at least one of the following: something you know (password), something you have (token), or something you are (biometric). Multi-factor authentication is required for all non-console administrative access and remote access to the cardholder data environment. This control must be implemented by IT security personnel during user onboarding and reviewed annually.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All users must be assigned a unique identification (ID) before accessing cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All users accessing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure each user is uniquely identified for accountability and traceability purposes.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, potential data breaches

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update user IDs
mitigation.2: Implement automated ID assignment processes
mitigation.3: Implement user ID access controls

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Multi-factor authentication is required for all non-console administrative access and remote access to the cardholder data environment.
compliances.Scope: Non-console administrative and remote access to cardholder data environment
compliances.Objective: To enhance security by requiring multiple authentication factors for sensitive access.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access, data breaches

mitigation.1: Implement multi-factor authentication solutions
mitigation.2: Regularly review and update authentication methods
mitigation.3: Provide user training on multi-factor authentication best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Authentication methods must be reviewed annually by IT security personnel.
compliances.Scope: All authentication methods used for accessing cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure the ongoing effectiveness and security of authentication methods.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Outdated or insecure authentication methods, increased risk of unauthorized access

mitigation.1: Establish regular review schedules
mitigation.2: Implement automated authentication method checks
mitigation.3: Provide training on updated authentication best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated authentication methods, increased risk of unauthorized access

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for controlling physical access to systems that store or process cardholder data, ensuring that only authorized personnel can access sensitive areas.
subpolicies.Control: Physical access to cardholder data environments must be restricted using facility entry controls. Onsite personnel must be distinguished from visitors through ID badges. Access must be authorized based on job function and revoked immediately upon termination. This control must be enforced by facility management and reviewed quarterly.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Physical Access Control - Facility Entry Controls
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Restrict physical access to cardholder data environments using facility entry controls.
compliances.Scope: All employees and visitors accessing sensitive areas storing or processing cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: Ensure only authorized personnel can access sensitive areas containing cardholder data.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Implement biometric access controls
mitigation.2: Regularly audit access logs
mitigation.3: Implement two-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized individuals gaining access to cardholder data storage areas.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Physical Access Control - ID Badge Requirement
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Distinguish onsite personnel from visitors through the use of ID badges.
compliances.Scope: All employees and visitors accessing sensitive areas storing or processing cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: Clearly identify authorized personnel within the facility.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized individuals posing as employees gaining access to sensitive areas.

mitigation.1: Implement ID badge verification checkpoints
mitigation.2: Regularly audit ID badge issuance records
mitigation.3: Implement visitor badge system

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized individuals gaining access by posing as employees with fake IDs.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Physical Access Control - Job Function Authorization
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Authorize access based on job function and revoke immediately upon termination.
compliances.Scope: All employees accessing sensitive areas storing or processing cardholder data.
compliances.Objective: Ensure access to sensitive areas is based on job roles and terminated promptly upon employment termination.
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Former employees retaining access to sensitive areas post-termination.

mitigation.1: Automate access revocation process upon termination
mitigation.2: Regularly review access permissions
mitigation.3: Implement role-based access control

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Former employees retaining access to cardholder data storage areas post-termination.
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy mandates the documentation and communication of access control policies and procedures to ensure all personnel are aware of their responsibilities regarding access to cardholder data.
subpolicies.Control: Access control policies and procedures must be documented, communicated to all affected personnel, and reviewed annually. All personnel must acknowledge their understanding of these policies. This control will be managed by the compliance officer and monitored through training records.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All access control policies and procedures must be documented in a clear and concise manner, outlining the responsibilities of personnel regarding access to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All personnel with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all personnel are aware of their responsibilities and obligations regarding access to cardholder data
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to document access control policies may lead to unauthorized access to cardholder data, resulting in data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Regular training sessions on access control policies
mitigation.2: Periodic audits to ensure policy documentation compliance
mitigation.3: Implementing access controls to restrict unauthorized access

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches, financial penalties
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Access control policies and procedures must be effectively communicated to all personnel with access to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All personnel with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure that all personnel understand and acknowledge their responsibilities regarding access to cardholder data
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of communication may lead to misunderstandings, non-compliance, and increased risk of unauthorized access to cardholder data.

mitigation.1: Regular training sessions on access control policies
mitigation.2: Implementing communication channels for policy updates
mitigation.3: Conducting awareness campaigns on access control best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Misunderstandings, non-compliance, unauthorized access
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Annual Review of Access Control Policies
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Access control policies and procedures must be reviewed annually to ensure their effectiveness and relevance.
compliances.Scope: All personnel with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To verify that access control policies are up-to-date and aligned with the organization's security requirements
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to review policies annually may result in outdated controls, increasing the risk of unauthorized access to cardholder data.

mitigation.1: Establish a formal review process for access control policies
mitigation.2: Conduct regular audits to assess policy effectiveness
mitigation.3: Implement a feedback mechanism for policy improvement

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated controls, increased risk of unauthorized access
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling cardholder data

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the requirements for regularly monitoring and testing networks to ensure the security of payment card data. It aims to identify and remediate vulnerabilities in network devices and systems, thereby preventing unauthorized access to cardholder data. The policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment processing and mandates logging, monitoring, and testing protocols to maintain compliance with PCI DSS requirements.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, personnel, systems, applications, and infrastructure involved in payment card processing. It covers all locations including stores, regional offices, headquarters, and remote access points. The policy encompasses all types of data related to cardholder information and includes third-party service providers involved in payment processing.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the continuous security of payment card data by implementing effective monitoring and testing protocols. Specific goals include identifying vulnerabilities, ensuring compliance with PCI DSS, mitigating risks associated with unauthorized access, and maintaining operational effectiveness through regular reviews and updates of security measures.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for implementing audit trails that link all access to system components to individual users. It mandates the creation of automated audit trails for critical events and the secure retention of these logs.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement audit trails for all system components to track user access and actions. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for implementation and maintenance. WHEN: Audit trails must be implemented immediately and reviewed daily. HOW: Use automated logging tools to capture and store audit data. WHERE: Applicable to all systems processing cardholder data and network resources.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement automated audit trails for all system components to track user access and actions.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to all systems processing cardholder data and network resources.
compliances.Objective: To ensure all user access and actions are tracked and logged for security and compliance purposes.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access or actions may go undetected, leading to data breaches or compliance violations.

mitigation.1: Regularly monitor and review audit logs for suspicious activities
mitigation.2: Implement multi-factor authentication to enhance user access security
mitigation.3: Regularly update audit trail tools to ensure effectiveness

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, compliance violations

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Audit trails must be reviewed daily to ensure timely detection of unauthorized access or actions.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to all systems processing cardholder data and network resources.
compliances.Objective: To promptly identify and respond to any suspicious activities or security breaches.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delayed detection of unauthorized access or actions may result in prolonged exposure to security threats.

mitigation.1: Implement automated alerts for unusual activities in audit logs
mitigation.2: Establish incident response procedures for immediate action upon detection of security incidents
mitigation.3: Regularly train IT security team on efficient audit log analysis techniques

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected security breaches, prolonged exposure to threats

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure secure retention of audit logs to prevent unauthorized tampering or deletion.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to all systems processing cardholder data and network resources.
compliances.Objective: To maintain the integrity and reliability of audit logs for compliance and forensic purposes.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized modification or deletion of audit logs may lead to the concealment of security incidents or non-compliance.

mitigation.1: Implement access controls to restrict unauthorized access to audit logs
mitigation.2: Encrypt audit logs to prevent tampering during storage or transmission
mitigation.3: Regularly verify the integrity of audit logs through checksums or digital signatures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Tampering or deletion of audit logs, concealment of security incidents

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for conducting regular vulnerability scans and penetration tests to identify and address security weaknesses in the network. It includes guidelines for both internal and external testing.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct internal and external vulnerability scans quarterly and perform penetration testing annually. WHO: IT Security Team and Approved Scanning Vendors (ASVs) are responsible for conducting scans and tests. WHEN: Scans must be performed quarterly and after significant network changes; penetration tests must be conducted annually. HOW: Utilize vulnerability scanning tools and penetration testing methodologies. WHERE: All systems within the cardholder data environment must be included in the testing scope.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct internal vulnerability scans quarterly to identify security weaknesses in the network.
compliances.Scope: Applies to IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: To proactively identify and address security vulnerabilities within the network.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of unauthorized access or data breaches due to unidentified vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Implement patches and updates promptly
mitigation.2: Implement network segmentation to limit exposure
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication for sensitive systems

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, network downtime

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct annual penetration testing to identify and address security weaknesses in the network.
compliances.Scope: Applies to IT Security Team and Approved Scanning Vendors (ASVs)
compliances.Objective: To simulate real-world attacks and identify potential vulnerabilities that could be exploited by malicious actors.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches, financial losses, reputational damage.

mitigation.1: Implement recommendations from penetration testing
mitigation.2: Regularly update security policies and procedures
mitigation.3: Train employees on security best practices

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, financial losses, reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct external vulnerability scans quarterly to identify security weaknesses in the network.
compliances.Scope: Applies to IT Security Team
compliances.Objective: To identify vulnerabilities that could be exploited by external attackers.
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of unauthorized access or data breaches due to unidentified vulnerabilities.

mitigation.1: Implement patches and updates promptly
mitigation.2: Implement network segmentation to limit exposure
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication for sensitive systems

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to sensitive data, data breaches, network downtime

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for reviewing logs and security events to identify anomalies or suspicious activities. It mandates daily reviews and the establishment of incident response procedures.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Review logs for anomalies and suspicious activities daily. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for log reviews and anomaly detection. WHEN: Log reviews must occur daily, with immediate reporting of any anomalies. HOW: Use log management tools to analyze and report on log data. WHERE: All systems generating logs related to cardholder data must be included in the review process.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: IT Security Team must review logs for anomalies and suspicious activities daily.
compliances.Scope: All systems generating logs related to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To identify and respond to anomalies or suspicious activities in a timely manner
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to detect and respond to security incidents could lead to data breaches and financial losses.

mitigation.1: Implement automated log review processes
mitigation.2: Provide regular training to IT Security Team on log analysis
mitigation.3: Establish incident response procedures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected security incidents leading to data breaches and financial losses
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team and overall organization

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Any anomalies detected during log reviews must be reported immediately.
compliances.Scope: All systems generating logs related to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure prompt response and investigation of potential security incidents
compliances.PossibleDamage: Delay in reporting anomalies could allow malicious activities to persist and escalate.

mitigation.1: Establish clear reporting procedures for anomalies
mitigation.2: Implement real-time alerting mechanisms for suspicious activities
mitigation.3: Conduct regular audits of anomaly reporting process

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Undetected anomalies leading to security incidents
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team and overall organization

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Establish incident response procedures for handling detected anomalies and security incidents.
compliances.Scope: All systems generating logs related to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure a structured and effective response to security incidents
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of incident response procedures could result in prolonged security breaches and data exposure.

mitigation.1: Develop and document incident response plans
mitigation.2: Conduct regular incident response drills and simulations
mitigation.3: Provide incident response training to relevant staff

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate response to security incidents leading to data breaches
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: IT Security Team and overall organization

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy establishes the framework for maintaining an Information Security Policy that governs the protection of cardholder data across the organization. It ensures that all personnel are aware of their responsibilities regarding information security and outlines the processes for risk assessment, policy dissemination, and compliance with PCI DSS requirements. Regular reviews and updates are mandated to adapt to changes in the security environment.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, employees, and contractors who handle cardholder data. It encompasses all systems, applications, and infrastructure involved in payment card processing, including on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. The policy also covers third-party service providers that interact with cardholder data and includes all types of data related to payment card transactions.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of cardholder data by establishing a comprehensive Information Security Policy that is regularly reviewed and updated. It aims to enhance employee awareness of security responsibilities, mitigate risks associated with cardholder data, and ensure compliance with PCI DSS requirements. The policy seeks to create a secure environment for processing payment card transactions and to protect the organization from potential security breaches.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for conducting a risk assessment to identify critical assets, threats, and vulnerabilities related to cardholder data. It mandates the documentation of findings and the implementation of necessary controls to mitigate identified risks.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Conduct a formal risk assessment annually and upon significant changes to the environment. WHO: The Information Security Officer is responsible for overseeing the risk assessment process. WHEN: The assessment must be performed at least once a year and whenever there are significant changes in the environment. HOW: Utilize a standardized risk assessment framework to identify and evaluate risks, document findings, and recommend mitigation strategies. WHERE: The assessment applies to all systems and processes that handle cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct a formal risk assessment annually to identify critical assets, threats, and vulnerabilities related to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and processes that handle cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To identify and evaluate risks, document findings, and recommend mitigation strategies
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement encryption for sensitive data
mitigation.2: Regularly update security patches
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct a risk assessment upon significant changes to the environment to identify new risks related to cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and processes that handle cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To proactively identify and mitigate new risks arising from changes
compliances.PossibleDamage: Exposure to new vulnerabilities and threats

mitigation.1: Perform regular security audits
mitigation.2: Implement change management processes
mitigation.3: Conduct penetration testing

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Exploitation of new vulnerabilities, data leakage

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Document findings from the risk assessment process including identified risks, vulnerabilities, and mitigation strategies.
compliances.Scope: All systems and processes that handle cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To maintain a record of risks and mitigation efforts for future reference
compliances.PossibleDamage: Lack of visibility into identified risks and mitigation progress

mitigation.1: Implement risk register for tracking
mitigation.2: Regularly update risk documentation
mitigation.3: Conduct periodic risk reviews

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Loss of historical risk data, ineffective risk mitigation

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy establishes the requirements for a formal security awareness program aimed at educating all personnel about their responsibilities regarding cardholder data security.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement a security awareness training program for all employees. WHO: The Human Resources department will coordinate the training sessions, with support from the Information Security team. WHEN: Training must be conducted at least annually and whenever there are updates to security policies. HOW: Use a combination of online training modules, in-person workshops, and regular communications to reinforce security awareness. WHERE: Training will be applicable to all employees and contractors who have access to cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All employees and contractors with access to cardholder data must undergo annual security awareness training sessions.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to all employees and contractors with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure all personnel are educated about their responsibilities regarding cardholder data security
compliances.PossibleDamage: Increased risk of data breaches and non-compliance penalties

mitigation.1: Regular monitoring of training completion
mitigation.2: Implement consequences for non-compliance
mitigation.3: Provide refresher training sessions

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches, unauthorized access to cardholder data
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by potential data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: All employees and contractors must undergo training whenever there are updates to security policies.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to all employees and contractors with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure all personnel are aware of and understand any changes to security policies
compliances.PossibleDamage: Misunderstanding of updated policies leading to non-compliance

mitigation.1: Provide detailed explanations of policy updates
mitigation.2: Conduct Q&A sessions after training
mitigation.3: Require confirmation of understanding

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Non-compliance with updated security policies
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by non-compliance

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Regular communications must be used to reinforce security awareness among all employees and contractors.
compliances.Scope: Applicable to all employees and contractors with access to cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To maintain a high level of security awareness and vigilance among personnel
compliances.PossibleDamage: Decreased security awareness leading to increased vulnerability

mitigation.1: Use multiple communication channels
mitigation.2: Include real-life examples in communications
mitigation.3: Encourage reporting of suspicious activities

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Increased susceptibility to social engineering attacks
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units would be impacted by decreased security awareness

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy details the requirements for establishing and maintaining an incident response plan to address potential security breaches involving cardholder data.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Develop and implement an incident response plan that outlines procedures for responding to security breaches. WHO: The Incident Response Team, led by the Chief Information Security Officer, is responsible for the plan's execution. WHEN: The plan must be reviewed and tested at least annually and updated as necessary. HOW: Conduct regular drills and simulations to ensure readiness, and document all incidents and responses for review. WHERE: The plan applies to all systems and personnel involved in the processing of cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Develop and implement an incident response plan that outlines procedures for responding to security breaches involving cardholder data.
compliances.Scope: All systems and personnel involved in the processing of cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To establish a structured approach for responding to security breaches
compliances.PossibleDamage: Potential loss of sensitive cardholder data, financial penalties, reputational damage

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update the incident response plan
mitigation.2: Conduct regular drills and simulations to test readiness
mitigation.3: Document all incidents and responses for review

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Security breaches resulting in data loss, financial penalties, and reputational damage

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Testing and Review of Incident Response Plan
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Review and test the incident response plan at least annually and update as necessary.
compliances.Scope: All systems and personnel involved in the processing of cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure the incident response plan remains effective and up-to-date
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective response to security breaches, increased risk of data loss

mitigation.1: Regularly review and update the incident response plan
mitigation.2: Conduct regular drills and simulations to test readiness
mitigation.3: Document all incidents and responses for review

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Outdated incident response plan leading to ineffective response to security breaches

compliances.ComplianceTitle: Drills and Simulations for Incident Response
compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Conduct regular drills and simulations to ensure readiness for responding to security breaches.
compliances.Scope: All systems and personnel involved in the processing of cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To test the effectiveness of the incident response plan in real-world scenarios
compliances.PossibleDamage: Inadequate preparedness for security breaches, delayed response times

mitigation.1: Conduct regular drills and simulations to test readiness
mitigation.2: Document all incidents and responses for review
mitigation.3: Implement lessons learned from drills into the incident response plan

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Inadequate response to security breaches due to lack of preparedness

policies.PolicyDescription: This policy outlines the framework for implementing compensating controls when an entity is unable to meet specific PCI DSS requirements due to legitimate technical or business constraints. It emphasizes the importance of risk mitigation through alternative measures that must be validated by an assessor. The policy ensures that compensating controls are effective and appropriate for the specific environment in which they are deployed.
policies.PolicyName: Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units involved in payment card processing, including but not limited to IT, compliance, and risk management teams. It covers all systems, applications, and infrastructure that handle payment card data, including on-premises, cloud, and remote environments. The policy also encompasses third-party vendors and service providers that interact with payment card information.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to provide a structured approach for implementing compensating controls that effectively mitigate risks associated with unmet PCI DSS requirements. It aims to ensure compliance with PCI DSS standards while maintaining operational effectiveness and protecting sensitive payment card information. The policy seeks to establish measurable outcomes for the effectiveness of compensating controls and to ensure ongoing assessment and documentation.

subpolicies.SubPolicyName: Assessment and Validation of Compensating Controls
subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the procedures for assessing and validating compensating controls implemented in lieu of specific PCI DSS requirements. It details the responsibilities of assessors and the criteria for evaluating the effectiveness of these controls.
subpolicies.Control: Assessors (Compliance Team) will review compensating controls implemented by entities to ensure they sufficiently mitigate risks. This review will occur at least annually or upon significant changes to the environment. The assessment will involve evaluating the control's configuration, effectiveness, and integration with existing security measures. Documentation of the assessment results will be maintained in the compliance repository.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Assessors (Compliance Team) will review compensating controls implemented by entities at least annually to ensure they sufficiently mitigate risks.
compliances.Scope: All entities subject to PCI DSS requirements
compliances.Objective: To ensure that compensating controls are regularly reviewed for effectiveness
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units handling payment card data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Failure to review compensating controls annually may lead to undetected vulnerabilities and potential data breaches.

mitigation.1: Conduct regular assessments as per schedule
mitigation.2: Implement automated monitoring tools for continuous control evaluation
mitigation.3: Provide regular training to assessors on control evaluation

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breach due to ineffective compensating controls
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units handling payment card data

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Assessors (Compliance Team) will evaluate the integration of compensating controls with existing security measures.
compliances.Scope: All entities implementing compensating controls
compliances.Objective: To ensure that compensating controls are effectively integrated with existing security measures
compliances.BusinessUnitsCovered: All business units implementing compensating controls
compliances.PossibleDamage: Ineffective integration may lead to gaps in security coverage and increased vulnerability to cyber threats.

mitigation.1: Conduct thorough integration testing before deployment
mitigation.2: Regularly monitor the performance of integrated controls
mitigation.3: Implement automated alerts for integration failures

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Increased vulnerability to cyber threats due to ineffective control integration
compliances.RiskBusinessImpact: All business units implementing compensating controls

versions.PolicyName: Compensating Controls for PCI DSS Requirements

policies.PolicyDescription: The Data Security Policy establishes the framework for protecting cardholder data and sensitive authentication data in compliance with PCI DSS. It outlines the necessary security measures and controls to safeguard data integrity and confidentiality, ensuring that all personnel understand their responsibilities in maintaining a secure environment. This policy applies to all systems and personnel involved in processing, storing, or transmitting cardholder data.
policies.Scope: This policy applies to all organizational units, personnel, systems, applications, and infrastructure involved in payment card processing. It covers on-premises and cloud environments, including all geographic locations where cardholder data is processed. Third-party vendors and service providers that handle cardholder data are also included in this scope.
policies.Objective: The objective of this policy is to ensure the protection of cardholder data through the implementation of robust security measures, compliance with PCI DSS requirements, and the establishment of a culture of security awareness among all personnel. Specific goals include reducing the risk of data breaches, ensuring regulatory compliance, and protecting the organization’s reputation.

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy outlines the requirements for establishing and maintaining a secure network to protect cardholder data. It includes the implementation of firewalls, secure configurations, and the prohibition of default vendor passwords.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Implement firewalls and secure configurations to protect cardholder data. WHO: IT Security Team is responsible for firewall configuration and maintenance. WHEN: Firewalls must be installed prior to processing cardholder data and reviewed quarterly. HOW: Use industry-standard firewall configurations and regularly update them based on security best practices. WHERE: Applies to all network segments that handle cardholder data.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement industry-standard firewalls to protect cardholder data as per network security controls subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: All network segments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To establish a secure network environment for protecting cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Exposure of cardholder data leading to regulatory fines and reputational damage

mitigation.1: Regularly update firewall configurations
mitigation.2: Conduct quarterly reviews of firewall settings
mitigation.3: Implement intrusion detection systems

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Unauthorized access to cardholder data, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Implement secure configurations to protect cardholder data as per network security controls subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: All network segments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To ensure secure network configurations for protecting cardholder data
compliances.PossibleDamage: Data breaches, unauthorized access to cardholder data

mitigation.1: Regularly update system configurations
mitigation.2: Implement access controls and least privilege principle
mitigation.3: Conduct regular vulnerability assessments

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: System vulnerabilities exploited, data breaches

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Ensure default vendor passwords are not used to protect cardholder data as per network security controls subpolicy.
compliances.Scope: All network segments handling cardholder data
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access to cardholder data through default vendor passwords
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access, data breaches

mitigation.1: Change default passwords immediately upon deployment
mitigation.2: Implement password management policies
mitigation.3: Regularly audit password usage

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Default passwords exploited, unauthorized access to systems

subpolicies.Description: This subpolicy specifies the requirements for encrypting cardholder data during transmission across open and public networks to prevent unauthorized access.
subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Encrypt all cardholder data transmitted over public networks. WHO: Network Administrators are responsible for implementing encryption protocols. WHEN: Encryption must be applied to all data transmissions immediately upon implementation of the network. HOW: Utilize strong encryption protocols such as TLS 1.2 or higher. WHERE: Applies to all systems transmitting cardholder data over public networks.

compliances.ComplianceItemDescription: Encrypt all cardholder data transmitted over public networks using strong encryption protocols such as TLS 1.2 or higher.
compliances.Scope: Applies to all systems transmitting cardholder data over public networks
compliances.Objective: To prevent unauthorized access to cardholder data during transmission
compliances.PossibleDamage: Unauthorized access to cardholder data leading to data breaches and financial losses

mitigation.1: Implement regular security audits to ensure encryption protocols are up to date
mitigation.2: Provide regular training to network administrators on encryption best practices
mitigation.3: Implement multi-factor authentication for access to cardholder data

compliances.PotentialRiskScenarios: Data breaches resulting in financial penalties and loss of customer trust

subpolicies.Control: WHAT: Restrict access to cardholder data to authorized personnel only. WHO: Access Control Manager is responsible for managing user access rights. WHEN: Access rights must be reviewed bi-annually. HOW: Implement role-based access controls and maintain an access log. WHERE: Applies to all systems and applications that store or process cardholder data.
